title: Verify Epic 2 fixes: all memory types round-trip correctly through all paths
description:
## Purpose
After fixing the SQLite layer, type casts, and CLI CRUD, verify that all 4 memory types (lesson, solution, pattern, preference) correctly round-trip through every path:

## Verification Matrix
1. JSONL write → JSONL read (all types, including pattern field)
2. JSONL → SQLite sync → keyword search (all types, pattern in FTS5)
3. JSONL → vector search (all types, pattern preserved)
4. MCP capture → storage → MCP search (all types)
5. CLI show/update/delete (all types)
6. Compact (all types preserved)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Integration test covering all 6 paths above
- [ ] pnpm test passes (100% pass rate)
- [ ] pnpm lint passes (zero violations)
- [ ] No data loss for any memory type through any path

title: Simplify AgentTeam instructions across ALL phases
description:
## Problem
ALL 5 phase templates (commands + skills) contain verbose code blocks with exact tool syntax for AgentTeam spawning. Claude skips all of it because the instructions are too prescriptive.

Example of what Claude ignores (brainstorm command, lines 22-29):
```
TeamCreate: team_name="brainstorm-<topic-slug>"
Task: name="docs-explorer", subagent_type="Explore", team_name="brainstorm-<topic-slug>"
  prompt: "Scan docs/ for architecture docs, specs, research..."
Task: name="code-explorer", subagent_type="Explore", team_name="brainstorm-<topic-slug>"
  prompt: "Research codebase areas relevant to: <topic>"
```

This pattern repeats in ALL phases. Claude knows how to use TeamCreate and Task -- it does not need code blocks showing exact syntax.

## Desired Approach
Describe WHAT to delegate (purpose per agent), not HOW to invoke tools. Example:

Before: 8-line code block with TeamCreate + Task syntax
After: 'Spawn docs-explorer and code-explorer in parallel to research the topic. Synthesize their findings.'

## Changes Per Phase

### Brainstorm (commands.ts + skills.ts)
Current: 8-line code block spawning docs-explorer + code-explorer
New: 'Spawn docs-explorer and code-explorer in parallel to research the topic.'
Also simplify skills.ts which says 'Create an AgentTeam (TeamCreate) and spawn docs-explorer + code-explorer as parallel teammates via Task tool with team_name'

### Plan (commands.ts + skills.ts)
Current: 9-line code block spawning docs-analyst + repo-analyst + memory-analyst
New: 'Spawn docs-analyst, repo-analyst, and memory-analyst in parallel to research constraints and patterns.'
Also simplify skills.ts

### Work (commands.ts + skills.ts + agents-workflow.ts)
Current: 18-line conditional branching (trivial/simple/complex, test-analyst, sequential/iterative modes)
New: 'Review tasks, decide parallelization, spawn test-writer and implementer. Test-writer writes tests, implementer makes them pass.'
Remove: complexity tiers, test-analyst agent, sequential/iterative modes, ping-pong references
Update agents-workflow.ts: remove Sequential Mode and Iterative Mode sections from test-writer.md and implementer.md

### Review (commands.ts + skills.ts)
Current: 14-line code block with tiered reviewer list and exact prompts
New: Keep adaptive tiers (useful specificity) but remove code block format. Just list which reviewers to spawn per tier without Task syntax.

### Compound (commands.ts + skills.ts)
Current: 15-line code block spawning 6 analysis agents with full prompts
New: 'Spawn the analysis pipeline: context-analyzer, lesson-extractor, docs-reviewer, pattern-matcher, solution-writer, compounding. They pass results downstream via SendMessage.'

## Files to Change

### Template files:
1. src/setup/templates/commands.ts — all 5 phase commands
2. src/setup/templates/skills.ts — all 5 phase skills
3. src/setup/templates/agents-workflow.ts — test-writer.md and implementer.md (remove modes)

### Test files (assertions that check for verbose patterns):
4. src/setup/templates/work-phase.test.ts — ~15 tests on complexity/modes
5. src/setup/templates/brainstorm-phase.test.ts — AgentTeam assertions
6. src/setup/templates/plan-phase.test.ts — AgentTeam assertions
7. src/setup/templates/review-phase.test.ts — AgentTeam assertions
8. src/setup/templates/compound-phase.test.ts — AgentTeam assertions
9. src/setup/templates/skills.test.ts — minor updates

## TDD Approach
1. Update all test files first (new simplified assertions)
2. Verify tests fail
3. Update templates
4. Verify tests pass
5. pnpm test && pnpm lint
close_reason: Simplified AgentTeam instructions across all 5 phases. Removed complexity tiers, code blocks, sequential/iterative modes.

title: Doc gardening subagent
description: Inspired by OpenAI paper: recurring agent that scans for stale docs. A subagent (invocable as /doc-garden or scheduled) that: (1) Checks docs/ files against actual code — are references still valid? (2) Flags docs that reference deleted/renamed files, (3) Checks if index.md files are up to date, (4) Identifies lessons that may have been superseded by code changes, (5) Proposes updates (or opens issues via beads). LOW PRIORITY but compounds over time — prevents doc rot that both papers warn about.

title: P0: Harden work.md verification gate — cannot close without /implementation-reviewer
description:
PROBLEM: work.md lines 161-163 list the 8-step verification pipeline. In practice, 0 of 8 subagents were invoked. The verification gate is entirely advisory.

FIELD EVIDENCE (session 37b79df7):
- 264 tests passed (good)
- 0/8 verification subagents ran (bad)
- No /implementation-reviewer gate was triggered
- Epic was closed directly after tests passed
- CLAUDE.md says this is INVIOLABLE: 'Work is NOT complete until /implementation-reviewer returns APPROVED'

ROOT CAUSE: The 'Verification Gate' section is at the bottom of the template. By the time Claude reaches step 15 ('bd close'), it considers the work done. The verification gate is a 'should do', not a 'must do before closing'.

FIX (in src/setup/templates/commands.ts, work.md template):
1. Move verification gate from after step 15 to BETWEEN step 14 (close task) and the end
2. Restructure as a HARD GATE:
   ```
   ## MANDATORY VERIFICATION — DO NOT CLOSE TASK WITHOUT THIS
   STOP. Before running bd close, you MUST:
   1. Run: pnpm test && pnpm lint (quality gates)
   2. Run /implementation-reviewer on the changed code
   3. Wait for APPROVED status
   If /implementation-reviewer returns REJECTED: fix ALL issues, re-run tests, resubmit.
   DO NOT close the task until approved. This is INVIOLABLE per CLAUDE.md.
   ```
3. Simplify the verification pipeline: require ONLY /implementation-reviewer as the mandatory gate (the other 7 are valuable but making all 8 mandatory is unrealistic for every task)
4. Add to task closure: 'Before bd close <id>, run ca verify-gates <epic-id> to check all gates'

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- work.md has verification gate BEFORE task closure, not after
- /implementation-reviewer is marked as INVIOLABLE gate, not optional checklist item
- Template includes explicit STOP language preventing bd close without approval
- Other 7 subagents remain recommended but not blocking
close_reason: Verification Gate replaced with MANDATORY VERIFICATION. STOP + /implementation-reviewer required.

title: Create lessons-reviewer subagent definition
description:
Create a new subagent that Claude spawns to review flagged lesson pairs from the clean-lessons command output. The subagent classifies each pair and proposes cleanup actions.

## New file: .claude/agents/lessons-reviewer/AGENT.md

Follow the pattern of `.claude/agents/implementation-reviewer/AGENT.md` (top-level agent with YAML frontmatter + markdown body).

## YAML frontmatter

```yaml
---
name: lessons-reviewer
description: Reviews flagged lesson pairs for duplicates, refinements, and contradictions. Proposes cleanup actions.
tools: Read, Bash, Grep, Glob
model: sonnet
permissionMode: default
---
```

## Markdown body — Agent instructions

The agent should:

### Input
- Run `npx ca clean-lessons` to get the diagnostic output with flagged pairs

### Analysis workflow (for each pair)
1. Read both lessons: `npx ca show <id>` for full details
2. Compare the two insights carefully
3. Classify the relationship:
   - **duplicate**: Both say essentially the same thing in different words
   - **refinement**: One is a more specific, accurate, or complete version of the other
   - **contradiction**: They give conflicting or opposite advice
   - **related**: Different but topically related — both valuable, keep both

4. Propose an action:
   - **duplicate**: `npx ca wrong <older-id> --reason \"Duplicate of <newer-id>\"` (invalidate the older/less specific one)
   - **refinement**: Invalidate the older one, keep the newer/more specific one
   - **contradiction**: DO NOT auto-resolve. Report to user with both insights and ask which is correct. If possible, check the codebase (grep for relevant patterns) to determine which is accurate.
   - **related**: No action needed

### Output format

For each pair:
```
Pair: <id-a> <-> <id-b> (similarity: NN%)
Classification: duplicate | refinement | contradiction | related
Reasoning: <1-2 sentences explaining why>
Proposed action: <the CLI command to run, or 'No action' for related>
```

Summary at the end:
```
Summary:
- N duplicate(s) to remove
- N refinement(s) to supersede
- N contradiction(s) requiring human review
- N related pair(s) (no action)
```

### Rules
- NEVER auto-execute destructive actions. Only PROPOSE them.
- When uncertain, classify as 'related' (conservative default).
- Prefer keeping the more specific, more recent lesson.
- For contradictions: search the codebase for evidence before asking user.
- Always explain reasoning clearly.

## No code changes needed

This is a pure file creation task — no TypeScript, no tests, no barrel exports.
close_reason: Created .claude/agents/lessons-reviewer/AGENT.md with YAML frontmatter and analysis workflow

title: Epic: Optimize test suite performance
description:
## Overview

Refactor the test suite to improve execution speed while maintaining test quality and isolation.

## Current State

- **17 test files** with ~8,265 lines of test code
- **cli.test.ts** alone is ~4,000 lines (50% of test code)
- Heavy operations per test: temp directory creation, SQLite rebuilds, process spawning
- No parallelization configured
- Property tests run 100 iterations by default

## Goals

1. **Faster local development** - Quick feedback loop with `test:fast`
2. **Parallel execution** - Utilize multi-core CPUs
3. **Smarter test organization** - Split large files for better parallelization
4. **Reduced overhead** - In-memory databases, fewer iterations locally
5. **Measurable improvements** - Baseline metrics to verify gains

## Approach

Batched implementation - all optimizations in one branch, verified together.

## Tasks (in recommended order)

1. Document baseline test metrics
2. Enable Vitest parallelization
3. Add test tags and `test:fast` script (skip integration, keep property tests)
4. Reduce fast-check iterations for local development
5. Add in-memory SQLite option for unit tests
6. Split cli.test.ts by command (~5-7 files)
7. Configure test result caching

## Success Criteria

- [ ] `pnpm test:fast` completes significantly faster than `pnpm test`
- [ ] All tests still pass
- [ ] No test isolation regressions
- [ ] Baseline vs final metrics documented

## Out of Scope

- Shared test fixtures (too risky for test isolation)
- Mocking business logic (violates TDD principles)
close_reason: Epic complete: test suite optimized from 122s to 62s (49% faster), test:fast runs in 5.7s

title: Compound: E1 lesson synthesis
P0: Harden lfg.md with mechanical STOP gates between phases
description:
PROBLEM: The lfg.md template lists all 5 phases but Claude executes 3 and stops. The instructions are soft ('Announce the current phase', 'Shut down team before next phase') — Claude treats them as suggestions.

FIELD EVIDENCE (session 37b79df7):
- Phases 1-3 (brainstorm, plan, work) completed
- Phase 4 (review) never started — no team created, no reviewers spawned
- Phase 5 (compound) never started — no learnings captured
- Session jumped from 'all tests pass' to 'bd close epic' to 'git commit'

ROOT CAUSE: No mechanical checkpoint between phases. The template flows as one continuous text block. Claude's completion bias triggers when tests pass.

FIX (in src/setup/templates/commands.ts, lfg.md template):
1. Add STOP-AND-VERIFY markers between each phase:
   ```
   ## PHASE GATE — DO NOT SKIP
   Before starting Phase 4 (Review), verify:
   - [ ] Run: bd list --status=open | grep 'Review:'
   - [ ] If no review task exists, CREATE IT NOW with bd create
   - [ ] All work tasks are closed (bd list --status=in_progress returns empty)
   VIOLATION: Proceeding without review = incomplete work. The session completion protocol WILL fail.
   ```
2. Add explicit 'run ca verify-gates <epic-id>' checkpoint before closing epic
3. Add 'CRITICAL: This is NOT done. 3/5 phases is failure, not success.' language
4. Each phase should end with 'NEXT: Phase N. Do not close the session.'

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- lfg.md template has explicit STOP gates between each phase pair
- Gates reference concrete bd commands to verify prerequisites
- Phase 4 and 5 are marked as INVIOLABLE, not optional
- Template includes ca verify-gates call before epic closure
close_reason: PHASE GATE 3->4, 4->5, FINAL GATE added to lfg.md. ca verify-gates call before epic closure.

title: External reviewer: Codex CLI headless integration
description:
## Summary

Add OpenAI Codex CLI as an optional external reviewer in the compound-agent review pipeline. When enabled via config, a dedicated agent template calls codex exec in headless mode after /implementation-reviewer approves, providing a cross-model review perspective.

## Motivation

Same rationale as Gemini integration: cross-model review catches different classes of issues. Codex brings OpenAI reasoning strengths (o-series models) to the review, complementing Claude and Gemini perspectives.

## Design

### Config flag

Add to externalReviewers array in config:

  { "externalReviewers": ["codex"] }

Can coexist with "gemini" -- both run sequentially.

### Agent template

New file: .claude/agents/compound/external-reviewer-codex.md

The agent should:
1. Check command -v codex is available (graceful skip if not)
2. Read the current beads issue context (bd show <id>)
3. Build a context-rich prompt: beads issue description + git diff of session changes
4. Call codex in headless exec mode:
   git diff HEAD~N | codex exec "<review prompt>" --quiet
   Or for structured output:
   codex exec --jsonl "Review the recent changes..."
5. Parse the output (stdout for text mode, JSONL events for structured mode)
6. Present findings as actionable items to the user

### Integration point

- Same as Gemini: runs AFTER /implementation-reviewer returns APPROVED
- Advisory, not blocking
- If both gemini and codex are enabled, run sequentially (gemini first, then codex)

### Codex headless specifics

- Use codex exec (alias codex e) for non-interactive mode
- --quiet for minimal noise, machine-readable output
- --jsonl for event stream (types: item.message, turn.completed, etc.)
- --sandbox workspace-write for safe read-only analysis
- --path . to scope to current repo
- Final answer is on stdout, progress on stderr

### Review prompt template

Same structure as Gemini:
- Beads issue title + description (the WHY)
- Git diff (the WHAT) -- can use --path . instead of piping diff
- Instruction to focus on: correctness, security, missed edge cases, code quality
- Instruction to be concise and actionable
- Instruction to output structured findings (numbered list)

### Setup / activation

- npx ca config set externalReviewers codex (or manual edit)
- Requires: npm i -g @openai/codex + codex login --api-key
- npx ca setup --update deploys the new agent template

### Error handling

- Same pattern as Gemini: graceful skip on any failure
- Check command -v codex before attempting
- Timeout handling (codex can be slow with reasoning models)
- Never block the pipeline on external reviewer failure

## Files to modify

- src/setup/templates/agents-phase11.ts -- add external-reviewer-codex.md template
- src/setup/templates/index.ts -- export new template
- Config reading logic (shared with Gemini feature)
- Workflow templates that orchestrate the review phase

## Testing

- Unit test: agent template exists and has correct structure
- Unit test: config flag parsing (shared with Gemini)
- Integration test: graceful skip when codex not available
- Integration test: review prompt includes beads context + diff

## Dependencies

- Shares config infrastructure with Gemini reviewer feature
- Both features should use a common ExternalReviewer abstraction if possible
close_reason: Closed

title: Claude doesn't use CLI - edits JSONL directly
description:
Claude edits .claude/lessons/index.jsonl directly instead of using CLI.

## Root Cause
AGENTS.md doesn't explicitly prohibit direct edits or emphasize CLI usage.

## Implementation

### 1. Update AGENTS.md template
In `src/commands/setup.ts`, update the AGENTS.md template to include:

```markdown
## CRITICAL RULES

**NEVER edit .claude/lessons/index.jsonl directly.**

The JSONL file is the source of truth and requires:
- Proper ID generation
- Schema validation
- SQLite index sync

Always use CLI commands:
- \`npx lna learn "insight" --severity high\` - Add lesson
- \`npx lna update <id> --insight "new"\` - Modify lesson
- \`npx lna delete <id>\` - Remove lesson
- \`npx lna wrong <id>\` - Mark lesson as invalid

Direct edits will cause:
- Missing IDs
- Schema validation failures
- SQLite index desync
- Lost lessons
```

### 2. Add warning comment in JSONL
When creating index.jsonl, add first line:
```
# DO NOT EDIT DIRECTLY - Use 'npx lna learn' command
```

### 3. Validate in prime command
The `lna prime` output should repeat this rule prominently.

### Reference
Pattern: beads AGENT_INSTRUCTIONS.md "DO NOT use bd edit" section
close_reason: Added CRITICAL RULES section to AGENTS.md template with 'NEVER edit index.jsonl' rule and CLI alternatives

title: Implement Gemini CLI Compatibility (Adapter Pattern)
description:
# Objective
Implement full compatibility with Gemini CLI without altering the core Claude-centric logic (`src/`). This will be achieved via an "Adapter Pattern", bridging Gemini's expected JSON format to our plain-text CLI tools, and proxying slash commands/skills.

## Plan

1. **Setup Command Enhancement**:
   - Add a new `npx ca setup gemini` subcommand (or integrate it into the default `setup`) that scaffolds the `.gemini/` structure.

2. **Hook Wrappers (`.gemini/hooks/*.sh`)**:
   - Create lightweight Bash wrapper scripts that consume JSON via `stdin` (as expected by Gemini) and run the respective `npx ca hooks run ...` command.
   - Wrappers should handle exit codes properly and output `{"decision": "allow"}` via `stdout` to conform to Gemini CLI's expected output.
   - Example mappings:
     - `SessionStart` -> `npx ca prime`
     - `BeforeAgent` -> `npx ca hooks run user-prompt`
     - `AfterTool` -> `npx ca hooks run post-tool-success` / `post-tool-failure` / `post-read`
     - `BeforeTool` -> `npx ca hooks run phase-guard`

3. **Register Hooks (`.gemini/settings.json`)**:
   - Generate `.gemini/settings.json` referencing these shell scripts, mapping them accurately to the Gemini hook lifecycle events (`SessionStart`, `BeforeAgent`, `AfterTool`, `BeforeTool`).
   - Add appropriate `matcher` regex strings so tools like `run_shell_command`, `replace`, and `write_file` trigger the same logic as Claude's `Bash`, `Edit`, `Write`.

4. **Slash Commands Integration (`.gemini/commands/*.toml`)**:
   - Instead of duplicating logic, generate TOML files in `.gemini/commands/` that use Gemini's file injection macro (`@{path}`) to embed the existing Claude markdown instructions.
   - e.g., `@.claude/commands/learn.md` inside `learn.toml`.

5. **Skill Bridging (`.gemini/skills/compound/SKILL.md`)**:
   - Create a proxy skill file that points back to the Claude instructions (`@.claude/skills/compound/instructions.md`), maintaining a single source of truth.
close_reason: Closed

title: Add full CRUD CLI commands for lesson management
description:
Add complete CRUD operations similar to beads so Claude can manage lessons without editing JSONL directly.

**Current state:**
- `learn` - creates quick lessons (no severity)
- `capture` - creates with trigger/insight (no severity)
- `list` - lists all lessons
- `search` - searches lessons

**Missing commands:**
1. `update <id>` - Update lesson fields
2. `delete <id>` - Soft delete (tombstone)
3. `show <id>` - Show single lesson details

**Enhancements to existing:**
- `learn --severity high --type full` - Create full lessons with severity
- `capture --severity medium` - Capture with severity

**Command specifications:**

### `learning-agent update <id>`
```bash
learning-agent update L001 --insight "Updated insight"
learning-agent update L001 --severity high
learning-agent update L001 --tags "api,auth"
learning-agent update L001 --confirmed true
learning-agent update L001 --json  # Output as JSON
```

### `learning-agent delete <id>`
```bash
learning-agent delete L001           # Soft delete (tombstone)
learning-agent delete L001 --json    # Output as JSON
learning-agent delete L001 L002 L003 # Multiple IDs
```

### `learning-agent show <id>`
```bash
learning-agent show L001        # Human-readable
learning-agent show L001 --json # JSON output
```

**All commands must:**
1. Support `--json` flag for programmatic output
2. Auto-sync to SQLite after mutation (see learning_agent-xxx)
3. Validate inputs against LessonSchema
notes:
## Tests to Implement

### Unit Tests - update command
- [ ] `update <id> --insight "new"` changes insight field
- [ ] `update <id> --severity high` changes severity
- [ ] `update <id> --tags "a,b"` parses and sets tags array
- [ ] `update <id> --confirmed true` sets confirmed
- [ ] `update <id> --json` outputs JSON
- [ ] Invalid ID -> error with helpful message
- [ ] Invalid severity value -> error with valid options

### Unit Tests - delete command
- [ ] `delete <id>` creates tombstone record
- [ ] `delete <id> --json` outputs JSON
- [ ] `delete L001 L002` deletes multiple
- [ ] Already deleted -> graceful no-op
- [ ] Invalid ID -> error

### Unit Tests - show command
- [ ] `show <id>` displays human-readable lesson
- [ ] `show <id> --json` outputs JSON
- [ ] Invalid ID -> error
- [ ] Deleted lesson -> shows deleted status

### Unit Tests - learn enhancements
- [ ] `learn "insight" --severity high` creates with severity
- [ ] `learn "insight" --type full` creates full lesson
- [ ] `learn --severity high` auto-sets type=full
- [ ] `learn --type full` without severity is valid

### Integration Tests
- [ ] CRUD cycle: create -> show -> update -> delete -> list (gone)
- [ ] Updated lesson visible in search results
- [ ] Deleted lesson excluded from list/search
- [ ] All mutations trigger auto-sync (verify SQLite updated)

### Edge Cases
- [ ] Update non-existent ID
- [ ] Delete non-existent ID
- [ ] Update deleted lesson -> error or resurrect?
- [ ] Concurrent updates to same lesson
- [ ] Very long insight text
- [ ] Special characters in insight
close_reason: Closed

title: Full test audit: review all ~100 test files against TDD standards
description: Audit every test file in src/**/*.test.ts against the established TDD pipeline standards (invariant-designer, test-first-enforcer, anti-cargo-cult-reviewer, property-test-generator, module-boundary-reviewer). Identify: cargo-cult tests (fake assertions, mocked business logic), weak assertions (conditional expects, overly permissive matchers), missing edge cases, dead/redundant tests, tests that don't test what they claim. title: Verify package.json and tarball contents
description:
## Overview

Verify package.json configuration and ensure tarball contains only necessary files.

## Package.json Checks

```bash
# View current version
node -p "require('./package.json').version"

# Check exports
node -p "require('./package.json').exports"
```

### Required Fields
- [ ] `name`: "learning-agent"
- [ ] `version`: "0.2.0"
- [ ] `description`: Accurate and concise
- [ ] `main`: "dist/index.js"
- [ ] `types`: "dist/index.d.ts"
- [ ] `bin`: { "learning-agent": "dist/cli.js" }
- [ ] `files`: ["dist"]
- [ ] `engines`: { "node": ">=20" }
- [ ] `repository`: Correct GitHub URL
- [ ] `keywords`: Relevant npm keywords
- [ ] `license`: "MIT"

### Dependencies
- [ ] All runtime deps in `dependencies`
- [ ] Build/test deps in `devDependencies`
- [ ] No unused dependencies
- [ ] No missing dependencies

## Tarball Verification

```bash
# See what will be published
pnpm pack --dry-run

# Create actual tarball for inspection
pnpm pack
tar -tzf learning-agent-0.2.0.tgz
```

### Should Include
- `dist/` (compiled JS + type declarations)
- `package.json`
- `README.md`
- `LICENSE`
- `CHANGELOG.md`

### Should NOT Include
- `src/` (source TypeScript)
- `*.test.ts` files
- `.claude/` directory
- `.beads/` directory
- `node_modules/`
- `.git/`
- `tsconfig.json`
- `vitest.config.ts`

## Checklist

- [ ] Version is 0.2.0
- [ ] All required fields present
- [ ] Dependencies correct
- [ ] Tarball size reasonable (<1MB without model)
- [ ] No dev files in tarball
- [ ] Clean install works: `npm install ./learning-agent-0.2.0.tgz`
close_reason: Package tarball verified: includes dist/, README, CHANGELOG, LICENSE, package.json

title: Simplify embeddings: use resolveModelFile + switch to EmbeddingGemma-300M
description:
## Problem

The current embedding setup in `src/embeddings/download.ts` is heavyweight compared to OpenClaw's approach:

1. **~90 lines of custom download code** for:
   - Progress reporting
   - Temp file handling with atomic rename
   - Stream backpressure management
   - Manual HuggingFace URL construction

2. **Large model** - nomic-embed-text-v1.5 is ~500MB, heavier than needed for memory search

3. **No fallback options** - if local embedding fails, the system is stuck

## Proposed Solution

### 1. Replace custom download with node-llama-cpp's built-in `resolveModelFile()`:

```typescript
// Before (download.ts + nomic.ts = ~190 lines)
export const MODEL_URL = 'https://huggingface.co/nomic-ai/...';
await downloadFile(MODEL_URL, modelPath);
const model = await llama.loadModel({ modelPath });

// After (~10 lines)
import { resolveModelFile } from 'node-llama-cpp';
const MODEL = 'hf:ggml-org/embeddinggemma-300M-GGUF/embeddinggemma-300M-Q8_0.gguf';
const resolved = await resolveModelFile(MODEL);
const model = await llama.loadModel({ modelPath: resolved });
```

### 2. Switch from nomic-embed-text-v1.5 to EmbeddingGemma-300M:

| Model | Size | Dimensions | Use Case |
|-------|------|------------|----------|
| nomic-embed-text-v1.5.Q4_K_M | ~500MB | 768 | Higher quality, heavier |
| **embeddinggemma-300M-Q8_0** | ~300MB | varies | Lighter, good for memory search |

## Benefits

- **Delete ~90 lines** of download infrastructure
- **~40% smaller model** (~300MB vs ~500MB)
- **Built-in caching** handled by node-llama-cpp
- **Simpler API** - just use `hf:` prefix for HuggingFace models
- **Faster cold start** - smaller model loads faster

## Reference

OpenClaw's implementation: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/main/src/memory/embeddings.ts

## Scope

- Remove `src/embeddings/download.ts` (or reduce to minimal re-export)
- Rename/update `src/embeddings/nomic.ts` to use `resolveModelFile` + new model
- **Update vector dimension constants** if EmbeddingGemma uses different dimensions
- **Rebuild any existing SQLite indexes** (embedding dimensions may change)
- Update tests
- Update CLI `download-model` command (may become optional/deprecated)
- Update documentation referencing nomic model

## Migration Note

Switching models means existing embeddings in SQLite will be incompatible. Users will need to rebuild their lesson index.
close_reason: Implemented: deleted ~460 lines of custom download code, switched to EmbeddingGemma-300M Q4_0 (~150MB vs ~500MB)

title: Implement Stop hook for phase gate verification
description:
NEW HOOK: Stop — prevents Claude from stopping without gate verification on phase transitions.

HOOK CONFIG (templates.ts):
- matcher: '' (no matcher, fires on every stop)
- command: 'npx ca hooks run phase-audit 2>/dev/null || true'

HANDLER (hooks.ts):
1. Read JSON from stdin (contains stop_hook_active boolean)
2. If stop_hook_active=true: exit 0 with empty JSON (prevent infinite loop)
3. Check if .claude/.ca-phase-state.json exists and lfg_active=true
4. If no state file or not active: exit 0 with empty JSON (no interference)
5. Read current_phase, phase_index, gates_passed from state
6. Determine expected gate for current phase:
   - After plan (phase_index=2): expect 'post-plan' in gates_passed
   - After work (phase_index=3): expect 'gate-3' in gates_passed
   - After review (phase_index=4): expect 'gate-4' in gates_passed
   - After compound (phase_index=5): expect 'final' in gates_passed
7. If expected gate NOT in gates_passed AND phase appears complete (heuristic: next phase start was attempted or beads notes say COMPLETE):
   Output JSON: { continue: false, stopReason: 'PHASE GATE NOT VERIFIED: You completed {phase} but did not verify {gate}. Run: ca phase-check gate {gate-name}' }
8. Otherwise: exit 0 with empty JSON (allow stop)

NOTE: This only triggers on phase transitions, not every turn. The heuristic is conservative — only fires when there's clear evidence a phase completed without gate verification.

DEPENDS ON: ca phase-check (needs state file)
close_reason: Fully implemented: Stop hook blocks stop when no phase gate passed

title: EPIC 7: Review Phase
description:
# Implement /compound:review workflow

## Goal
Fourth workflow phase: multi-agent review with inter-communication.
Reviewers can talk to each other. Implementation-reviewer has final authority.

## Scope
- review.md command: workflow instructions for Claude
- review/SKILL.md: reviewer roles, communication patterns, quality gates
- Reviewer agent definitions:
  - security.md, architecture.md, performance.md
  - test-coverage.md, simplicity.md
  - (project-specific reviewers based on tech stack)
- Inter-agent communication: reviewers share findings directly
- Implementation-reviewer gate: mandatory approval before completion
- Findings -> beads issues: P1 findings create blocking beads tasks
- Severity classification: P1 (block), P2 (should fix), P3 (nice to have)

## Exit Criteria
- /compound:review invokable as slash command
- Spawns specialized reviewer team (5+ agents)
- Reviewers communicate findings to each other
- Implementation-reviewer provides final verdict
- P1 findings create blocking beads tasks
- Review report synthesized from all findings
- Integration tests verify the workflow

## Context
- Architecture: doc/ARCHITECTURE-V2.md (Phase 4: Review)
- Agent model: Full team (5-10 reviewers) with inter-communication
- Current review process: .claude/CLAUDE.md (verification subagents)
- Builds on existing implementation-reviewer pattern

WHAT TO MOVE to docs/: (1) Full subagent sequence + descriptions -> docs/verification/subagent-pipeline.md, (2) Exit criteria checklists (all 6 categories) -> docs/verification/exit-criteria.md, (3) Closed-loop review process diagram -> already in docs/verification/, (4) Code organization details -> docs/standards/, (5) Anti-patterns detailed list -> docs/standards/, (6) Test architecture details -> docs/standards/test-architecture.md. APPROACH: Each moved section gets replaced by a one-line pointer: '> See docs/verification/subagent-pipeline.md for full sequence'. Result: CLAUDE.md ~200 lines, focused, with clear pointers.

title: Add short CLI alias 'lna' via npm bin
description:
Add a short alias for the CLI to reduce typing. **This becomes the primary command for Claude to use.**

**Current:**
```bash
npx learning-agent learn "insight" --severity high
npx learning-agent list
npx learning-agent update L001 --insight "new"
```

**Proposed:**
```bash
npx lna learn "insight" --severity high
npx lna list
npx lna update L001 --insight "new"
```

**Implementation:**
Update package.json bin field:
```json
{
  "bin": {
    "learning-agent": "./dist/cli.js",
    "lna": "./dist/cli.js"
  }
}
```

**IMPORTANT: lna is the PRIMARY command**

All Claude-facing documentation MUST use `lna`:
- AGENTS.md template: ALL examples use `npx lna`
- Pre-commit hook message: use `npx lna capture`
- Claude Code hooks: use `npx lna load-session`
- Error messages: suggest `npx lna download-model`

The long form `learning-agent` is kept only for:
- Backwards compatibility
- README (mention both, prefer lna)
- npm package name (can't change)

**Rationale:**
- Shorter = faster for Claude to type
- Consistent with beads (`bd`) pattern
- Reduces token usage in conversations

title: Redesign prime command with Beads-style trust patterns
description:
# Overview

Redesign `lna prime` to use Beads-style trust language patterns that make Claude believe and follow the injected guidelines.

## Current vs New

| Aspect | Current (load-session) | New (prime) |
|--------|------------------------|-------------|
| Focus | Inject lessons | Teach HOW to use system |
| Language | Neutral | Trust patterns (MUST, CRITICAL) |
| Content | Raw lessons | Guidelines + top lessons |
| Format | Plain text | Structured with protocols |

## Trust Language Patterns (from Beads research)

### 1. Explicit Prohibitions
```markdown
- **Default**: Use lesson_search for complex decisions
- **Prohibited**: Do NOT guess when lessons might help
```

### 2. Emergency Framing
```markdown
# 🔴 Mandatory Recall

You MUST use `lesson_search` BEFORE architectural decisions.
```

### 3. Workflow Sequencing
```markdown
- **Workflow**: Search lessons BEFORE planning, capture AFTER corrections
```

### 4. Consequences
```markdown
**NEVER skip lesson search for complex decisions.** Past mistakes will repeat.
```

## Prime Output Template

```markdown
# Learning Agent Active

> Context Recovery: Run \`lna prime\` after compaction

# 🔴 Mandatory Recall

You MUST use \`lesson_search\` BEFORE:
- Architectural decisions or complex planning
- Patterns you've implemented before in this repo
- After user corrections ("actually...", "wrong", "use X instead")

**NEVER skip lesson search for complex decisions.** Past mistakes will repeat.

## Capture Protocol

Use \`lesson_capture\` AFTER:
- User corrects you
- Test fail → fix → pass cycles  
- You discover project-specific knowledge

**Workflow**: Search BEFORE deciding, capture AFTER learning.

## CLI Commands (manual use)
- \`lna search "query"\` - Find relevant lessons
- \`lna learn "insight"\` - Save new lesson
- \`lna list\` - Show recent lessons

---

## High-Severity Lessons

[TOP 3-5 LESSONS INJECTED HERE]

Format per lesson:
### L001: [trigger]
**Insight**: [insight]
**Tags**: [tags]
```

## Implementation

```typescript
// src/commands/prime.ts
export async function getPrimeContext(): Promise<string> {
  const guidelines = getGuidelinesTemplate();
  const topLessons = await getHighSeverityLessons(5);
  const formattedLessons = formatLessonsForInjection(topLessons);
  
  return `${guidelines}\n\n---\n\n## High-Severity Lessons\n\n${formattedLessons}`;
}

export const primeCommand = new Command('prime')
  .description('Output prime context for hooks (guidelines + top lessons)')
  .action(async () => {
    const context = await getPrimeContext();
    console.log(context);
  });
```

## Token Budget

Target: < 2K tokens total
- Guidelines template: ~500 tokens (fixed)
- Top 5 lessons: ~300 tokens each = 1500 tokens max
- Buffer for formatting: ~200 tokens

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Uses Beads-style trust language patterns
- [ ] Includes mandatory recall section with MUST language
- [ ] Includes capture protocol with workflow
- [ ] Injects top 3-5 high-severity lessons
- [ ] Total output < 2K tokens
- [ ] Works as MCP resource (lessons://prime) and CLI command
close_reason: Implemented Beads-style trust language patterns with comprehensive test coverage. All 41 tests pass. Implementation-reviewer APPROVED.

title: Capture lesson about worktree state routing
description: After implementation is complete, capture a compound-agent lesson via npx ca learn about the worktree state routing constraint. Key insight: Claude Code hooks always run with cwd=primary working directory. When a git worktree is added as an additional directory (not primary), all .ca-* state files (.ca-phase-state.json, .ca-failure-state.json) are misrouted to the main repo. The fix is architectural: worktrees MUST be opened as the primary working directory in a separate Claude Code session. This is an inherent Claude Code limitation (hooks are dispatched from primary dir, settings.json is only read from primary dir). Tags: worktree, hooks, architecture, state-routing.

title: Test harness: phase enforcement hooks (unit + integration + E2E)
description:
Comprehensive test harness for the phase enforcement system. TDD: tests written BEFORE implementation.

## Test Files (6 total)

### 1. src/commands/phase-check.test.ts (Unit, fast)
State machine logic — init, start, gate, status, clean. Edge cases: missing file, corrupted JSON, invalid inputs, idempotency.

### 2. src/commands/phase-check.cli.test.ts (Integration, tags: ['hooks'])
CLI invocations via runCli(). All subcommands + --dry-run flag + invalid args.

### 3. src/setup/hooks-phase-guard.test.ts (Unit, fast)
Phase guard handler: no state -> {}, lfg_active=false -> {}, skill read -> {}, skill NOT read -> warning, corrupted -> {}.

### 4. src/setup/hooks-read-tracker.test.ts (Unit, fast)
Read tracker handler: no state -> {}, non-skill read -> no change, skill read -> appends, duplicate -> no dup, corrupted -> {}.

### 5. src/setup/hooks-stop-audit.test.ts (Unit, fast)
Stop hook handler: no state -> {}, stop_hook_active -> {}, gate verified -> {}, gate NOT verified + completing -> continue:false, mid-phase -> {}.

### 6. src/setup/hooks-lifecycle.test.ts (E2E, tags: ['hooks'])
Full LFG loop simulation: init -> read skills -> phase transitions -> gate checks -> cleanup -> verify all hooks return {} after cleanup.

## Safety Invariants (in EVERY hook test)
- No state file -> always {} (silent no-op)
- Corrupted state -> always {} (fail safe)
- lfg_active=false -> always {} (inactive)
- Error in handler -> exit 0 + {} (never crash)

## Tags
- Unit tests: no tag (pnpm test:fast)
- Integration/E2E: { tags: ['hooks'] }

## --dry-run mode
Each hook handler gets --dry-run: reads state, logs decision, returns {} (no side effects).

## TDD ORDER
Write tests FIRST, then implement. Tests should fail for the right reason (missing implementation) before any code is written.
close_reason: TDD test harness written: 6 test files for phase enforcement hooks

title: Investigate ralph-wiggum Stop hook pattern for compound-agent
description: Every uses Anthropic's ralph-wiggum plugin (Stop hook + state file + completion promise) to keep Claude running until the full lfg pipeline completes. Investigate whether compound-agent should implement a similar pattern: a Stop hook that blocks exit until all phases are done, using .claude/lfg-state.local.md as the state file. This is a research/design task — decide if it's worth implementing or if our PreCompact prime hook is sufficient.
notes:
## Ralph-Wiggum Stop Hook Research Findings

### 1. What Ralph-Wiggum IS

The Ralph Wiggum pattern (created by Geoffrey Huntley) is fundamentally a bash loop that restarts Claude Code repeatedly until work is done:

    while :; do cat PROMPT.md | claude-code ; done

There are TWO distinct implementations:
- **External bash loop** (original): A shell script wraps Claude Code, restarting it each time it exits
- **Stop hook plugin** (Anthropic-blessed): A Claude Code plugin that uses the Stop hook API to prevent Claude from exiting prematurely

The compound-engineering plugin from Every uses the STOP HOOK variant, not the bash loop.

### 2. How the Stop Hook Mechanism Works

The pattern has 3 components:

**A. The slash command (/ralph-loop)**
Invoked as: /ralph-wiggum:ralph-loop "finish all slash commands" --completion-promise "DONE"
- Takes a task description and a completion-promise string
- Creates a state file (.claude/ralph-loop.local.md) tracking the session
- The state file stores: original prompt, iteration count, promise text

**B. The Stop hook (hooks.json + stop-hook.sh)**
hooks.json registers a Stop event handler:
{
  "hooks": {
    "Stop": [{
      "matcher": "ralph-wiggum:*",
      "hooks": [{
        "type": "command",
        "command": "path/to/stop-hook.sh"
      }]
    }]
  }
}

When Claude tries to exit, the Stop hook:
1. Reads the state file to check if a ralph session is active
2. Scans Claude's last output for <promise>DONE</promise> tags
3. If promise found -> allows exit (exit 0)
4. If NO promise -> blocks exit, increments iteration counter, re-injects the original prompt
5. This forces Claude to keep working

**C. The promise tag (<promise>DONE</promise>)**
The final step in the workflow must output this tag. In Every's lfg.md:
- Step 9: Output <promise>DONE</promise> when video is in PR
Only when the stop-hook detects this in Claude's output does it allow exit.

### 3. How Every's compound-engineering Uses It

Their lfg.md is elegantly simple (611 bytes):
1. /ralph-wiggum:ralph-loop "finish all slash commands" --completion-promise "DONE"
2. /workflows:plan
3. /compound-engineering:deepen-plan
4. /workflows:work
5. /workflows:review
6. /compound-engineering:resolve_todo_parallel
7. /compound-engineering:test-browser
8. /compound-engineering:feature-video
9. Output <promise>DONE</promise> when video is in PR

The ralph-loop WRAPS the entire sequence. If Claude exits at step 4 (e.g., context runs out), the stop-hook blocks it and restarts, forcing it to continue from where it left off.

### 4. Analysis for Compound-Agent

**Problem the Stop Hook solves:**
Claude prematurely exits before completing a multi-step workflow. This happens when:
- Context fills up and Claude decides to compact/exit
- Claude misinterprets completion and tries to exit mid-workflow
- An error causes Claude to give up

**Our current approach:**
- We use ca prime on PreCompact hook for context recovery
- Our lfg delegates to /compound:* slash commands sequentially
- We have no mechanism to prevent premature exit

**Key comparison:**

| Aspect | Ralph Stop Hook | Our PreCompact Hook |
|--------|----------------|---------------------|
| Trigger | Claude tries to EXIT | Context is about to be COMPACTED |
| Action | Blocks exit, re-injects prompt | Primes context with project state |
| Scope | Prevents premature session end | Preserves context across compaction |
| Problem solved | Claude gives up too early | Claude loses context mid-session |

These are COMPLEMENTARY, not redundant. They solve different problems:
- PreCompact: handles context loss within a session
- Stop hook: prevents premature session termination

### 5. Recommendation: DEFER

**Reasoning:**
- The Stop hook is genuinely useful for preventing premature exit
- However, our current /compound:lfg workflow is still being stabilized (v1.2.x)
- We need to first finish: phase gate relocation, lfg slim-down, frontmatter
- The Stop hook adds complexity: shell scripts, state files, platform compatibility (macOS/Linux sed differences, flock vs mkdir locking)
- The ralph-loop-pp implementation is 200+ lines of bash for the stop hook alone
- We can approximate the behavior with prompt engineering (instructing Claude not to exit until all phases complete)
- Once our workflow is stable, a Stop hook would be a natural v1.3 enhancement

**If we implement later, the approach would be:**
1. Add a hooks/ directory with hooks.json + stop-hook.sh
2. The stop-hook reads .claude/compound-lfg.local.md state file
3. Checks for <promise>LFG_COMPLETE</promise> in output
4. If not found, blocks exit and re-injects the lfg prompt
5. Keep it simple: ~50 lines of bash, not 200+

**VERDICT: DEFER to v1.3. Focus on stabilizing current workflow first.**
close_reason: DEFER to v1.3. Ralph-wiggum Stop hook is complementary to our PreCompact prime hook (prevents premature exit vs preserves context). Not critical now while workflow is being stabilized.

title: Make SQLite cache lossless for unified Lesson optional fields
description:
Context: Lesson schema is now unified with optional evidence/severity. SQLite is a cache rebuilt from JSONL and should preserve data without mutation. Currently rebuildIndex drops evidence/severity for type=quick, and rowToLesson defaults evidence='' and severity='medium' for full lessons, which mutates data and can affect ranking/clients.

Acceptance Criteria:
- [ ] In rebuildIndex(), store evidence and severity for all lessons (no type gating).
- [ ] rowToLesson maps NULL -> undefined (do not default evidence to '' or severity to 'medium').
- [ ] Update any related SQL inserts/reads to allow NULL values without mutation.
- [ ] Add sqlite.test.ts coverage for roundtrip: JSONL -> rebuildIndex -> searchKeyword/read, verifying evidence/severity preserved for: quick with evidence/severity, full without them, and full with them.
- [ ] Ensure ranking behavior still defaults to medium when severity is undefined (severityBoost already handles undefined).

Files: src/storage/sqlite.ts, src/storage/sqlite.test.ts (maybe src/search/ranking.ts only if needed).
close_reason: Fixed lossless roundtrip: store evidence/severity for all lessons, map NULL to undefined

title: RELEASE: v0.2.2 - Hardening & Quality Gates
description:
## Release Goals
Hardening release focused on preventing stale lessons and context pollution. Based on LANDSCAPE.md reviewer feedback.

## Scope
This release adds quality gates and feedback mechanisms without expanding core functionality.

## Features Included
1. **Age-based temporal validity** (learning_agent-eik) - Flag lessons >90 days old
2. **Count warning** (learning_agent-qp9) - Warn when >20 lessons
3. **Optional citations** (learning_agent-tn3) - Store file/line provenance
4. **Manual invalidation** (learning_agent-mov) - `lna wrong <id>` command

## Research Sources
- Beads: Temporal compaction strategy
- GitHub Copilot: Citation tracking approach
- Mem0: Quality gates (simplified)
- HaluMem/Academic: Feedback mechanisms

## Release Criteria
- [ ] All 4 features implemented and tested
- [ ] All tests passing (including existing --severity and download-model)
- [ ] Documentation updated
- [ ] CHANGELOG entry written

## Not In Scope (deferred)
- Memory typing (Mem0 research showed limited value)
- AI-powered compaction (requires API integration)
- Citation verification (too complex)
- Full feedback analytics (implicit signals deferred)
close_reason: All LANDSCAPE.md features implemented and documented

title: CRITICAL: Pre-commit hook broken - MODULE_NOT_FOUND
description:
# Problem

Pre-commit hook fails with MODULE_NOT_FOUND error:
```
Error: Cannot find module '.../node_modules/learning-agent/dist/cli.js'
```

# Root Cause

User installed from **GitHub URL** instead of **npm**:
- GitHub install: Gets source code WITHOUT dist/ (gitignored)
- npm install: Gets built dist/ folder (included in package)

# Solution

The package is already published to npm: https://www.npmjs.com/package/learning-agent

**Fix is documentation + setup command:**

1. Update README to clearly show npm install (not GitHub)
2. Update `lna setup` to warn if installed from GitHub
3. Ensure `lna setup --status` detects this misconfiguration

# Implementation

### 1. README Update
```bash
# Correct (from npm)
pnpm add -D learning-agent

# WRONG (from GitHub - will break hooks)
# pnpm add -D github:user/learning-agent
```

### 2. Setup Detection
In `lna setup` or `lna setup --status`:
```typescript
// Check if dist/ exists
const distExists = fs.existsSync(path.join(__dirname, '../dist'));
if (!distExists) {
  console.error('ERROR: learning-agent was installed from GitHub without dist/');
  console.error('Please reinstall from npm: pnpm add -D learning-agent');
  process.exit(1);
}
```

# Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] README clearly shows npm install command
- [ ] README warns against GitHub install
- [ ] `lna setup` detects and warns about GitHub install
- [ ] Error message tells user exactly how to fix
close_reason: Implemented install-check utility with 50 tests

title: EPIC 10: Polish, Documentation & Release
description:
# Final quality pass: everything ultra clean, polished, professional

## Goal
Complete documentation overhaul, migration guide, professional README,

Add --embed flag to the index-docs command and proper resource cleanup.

Add .option('--embed', 'Embed chunks for semantic search') after existing --force option

4. 'does not display embedding count when zero' — mock indexDocs with chunksEmbedded: 0, verify output does not contain 'embedded'

## Existing patterns to follow

title: BUG: JSONL edits not synced to SQLite index
description:
Manual JSONL edits are not reflected in SQLite until explicit rebuild. User added lesson via Edit tool but loadSessionLessons queries JSONL directly, yet the SQLite was out of sync.

**Observed behavior:**
1. User manually added high-severity lesson to index.jsonl
2. New Claude session still said 'No high-severity lessons found'
3. SQLite had different data than JSONL

**Root cause investigation needed:**
- loadSessionLessons reads from JSONL directly via readLessons()
- But other operations might use SQLite
- sync mechanism might not trigger on external edits

**Evidence:**
```
User: 'I've added a high-severity lesson to the JSONL'
Claude: 'SessionStart:startup hook success: No high-severity lessons found.'
```

**Note:** This might actually be a different bug - need to verify if loadSessionLessons really reads JSONL or SQLite
notes:
## Tests to Implement

### Unit Tests
- [ ] loadSessionLessons reads directly from JSONL (verify with mock)
- [ ] Manual JSONL append is immediately visible to readLessons
- [ ] No SQLite dependency for session loading

### Integration Tests
- [ ] Write to JSONL externally -> loadSessionLessons sees it
- [ ] Append lesson via echo >> file -> CLI list shows it
- [ ] SQLite out of sync -> JSONL is source of truth

### Edge Cases
- [ ] JSONL has invalid line -> skip with warning, continue
- [ ] JSONL modified while reading -> consistent snapshot
- [ ] Empty JSONL file -> returns empty array
- [ ] JSONL doesn't exist -> returns empty array (not error)
- [ ] Race condition: two processes appending simultaneously

### Diagnosis Needed
- [ ] Verify loadSessionLessons actually reads JSONL not SQLite
- [ ] Check if there's caching that could cause stale reads
- [ ] Test with 'cat' to JSONL vs 'appendLesson()' function
Investigation complete - NOT a bug.

Root cause: User manual JSONL edit had invalid data (likely type:'quick' without severity field), silently skipped by readLessons().

Code verification:
✅ loadSessionLessons() reads JSONL directly (not SQLite)
✅ No caching layer
✅ JSONL is source of truth

Real issue: Silent parse error handling makes manual edits risky.

Invariants documented: doc/invariants/jsonl_source_of_truth_invariants.md

Recommended follow-up issues:
1. Add 'validate' CLI command
2. Enable parse error logging in CLI
3. Add --strict flag for load-session
close_reason: Investigation complete: NOT A BUG. JSONL-as-source-of-truth is working correctly. User error due to silent parse failures when manually editing JSONL. Created follow-up issues for UX improvements.

title: Auto-sync SQLite after every CLI mutation
description:
Every CLI command that mutates lessons should automatically sync to SQLite.

**Current state:**
- appendLesson() writes to JSONL only
- SQLite requires explicit rebuildIndex() or syncIfNeeded()
- Manual JSONL edits never sync

**Proposed behavior:**
After every mutation (learn, capture, update, delete), automatically:
1. Append to JSONL (current behavior)
2. Call syncIfNeeded() to update SQLite index
3. Optionally regenerate embeddings for changed lessons

**Implementation:**
```typescript
// In cli.ts after appendLesson:
await appendLesson(repoRoot, lesson);
await syncIfNeeded(repoRoot);  // NEW: auto-sync
```

**Commands affected:**
- learn
- capture
- update (new)
- delete (new)
- import

**Performance consideration:**
- syncIfNeeded uses content hash to skip if already synced
- Should be fast for single-lesson operations
- Consider batch mode for import (sync once at end)
notes:
## Tests to Implement

### Unit Tests
- [ ] learn command calls syncIfNeeded after appendLesson
- [ ] capture command calls syncIfNeeded after appendLesson
- [ ] update command calls syncIfNeeded after mutation
- [ ] delete command calls syncIfNeeded after tombstone
- [ ] import command calls syncIfNeeded once at end (not per-lesson)

### Integration Tests
- [ ] After `learn`, lesson immediately searchable via searchKeyword
- [ ] After `update`, changed fields visible in SQLite
- [ ] After `delete`, lesson excluded from SQLite results
- [ ] loadSessionLessons sees newly created high-severity lesson

### Performance Tests
- [ ] Single lesson sync < 100ms
- [ ] Bulk import (100 lessons) + single sync < 5s
- [ ] syncIfNeeded skips when already synced (hash match)

### Edge Cases
- [ ] SQLite file missing -> recreate
- [ ] SQLite file corrupted -> rebuild from JSONL
- [ ] JSONL empty -> sync creates empty tables
- [ ] Concurrent CLI invocations -> no race conditions
close_reason: Closed

title: Build generic rule engine (lna rules)
description: Inspired by OpenAI paper: mechanical enforcement over documentation. When a rule is in docs, agents may miss it. When its a lint with an agent-legible error message, it always fires. DESIGN: (1) Rules defined in a config file (e.g., .claude/rules.yaml or similar), (2) Each rule has: id, description, check (glob pattern + regex or custom script), severity, remediation message, (3) lna rules check runs all rules against the codebase, (4) Output format is agent-legible: one line per violation with file:line, rule-id, and remediation, (5) Plugin ships with no default rules — rules are repo-specific, (6) Future: subagent can suggest rules from recurring lessons. EXAMPLE RULE: { id: 'max-function-length', check: 'eslint-based or custom', message: 'Function exceeds 50 lines. Split into smaller functions per docs/standards/typescript-best-practices.md' }. This is the framework — specific rules come from lessons and team preferences.

title: Update AGENTS.md template with explicit plan-time instruction
description:
## Overview

Enhance the AGENTS.md template injected by `init` command to include explicit plan-time lesson check instructions.

## Background

Claude Code doesn't have a `PlanCreated` hook event. The workaround is to instruct Claude via AGENTS.md to manually run `check-plan` when creating implementation plans.

This is the same approach beads uses - inject instructions into AGENTS.md that Claude reads at session start.

## Current Template

Location: `src/cli.ts` lines 285-331 (`AGENTS_MD_TEMPLATE`)

Current plan-time section:
```markdown
### Retrieval Points

- **Session start**: High-severity lessons loaded automatically
- **Plan creation**: Semantic search for relevant lessons
```

This is too vague. Claude may not know HOW to do semantic search.

## Target Template

```markdown
### Automatic Lesson Loading

High-severity lessons are loaded automatically at session start via Claude Code hooks.

### Plan-Time Lesson Check (REQUIRED)

Before implementing any plan, check for relevant lessons:

\`\`\`bash
npx learning-agent check-plan --plan "brief summary of your plan"
\`\`\`

Display results under a \`## Lessons Check\` heading after your plan.

Example:
\`\`\`
## Implementation Plan
1. Add authentication middleware
2. Create JWT validation
3. Add protected routes

## Lessons Check
- API v2 requires X-Request-ID header (relevance: 0.87)
- Always validate token expiry before processing (relevance: 0.72)
\`\`\`
```

## Requirements

1. **Explicit command**: Show exact command to run
2. **Example output**: Show what Claude should display
3. **REQUIRED label**: Make it clear this is mandatory
4. **Remove ambiguity**: No vague "semantic search" language

## Implementation

Update `AGENTS_MD_TEMPLATE` constant in `src/cli.ts`.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Template includes explicit `check-plan` command
- [ ] Template shows example output format
- [ ] Template marks plan-time check as REQUIRED
- [ ] Existing init tests still pass
- [ ] New repos get updated template

## Notes

- This is a template change only
- Existing repos won't be updated (idempotency check sees header exists)
- Users can re-run `init` with manual AGENTS.md edit to get new template
close_reason: AGENTS.md template now includes explicit plan-time instructions

The compounding.md agent exists as a template but is not part of the execution flow. Fix: Add a 5th step in the compound phase that invokes compounding synthesis after solution-writer stores lessons — read fresh lessons, cluster by embedding similarity, synthesize CCT patterns, and persist to .claude/lessons/cct-patterns.jsonl.

title: Write agent definition markdown templates
description: Create markdown templates for all agent definitions that setup will generate. These are .md files with YAML frontmatter (name, description, model). Categories: research (repo-analyst, memory-analyst), review (security, architecture, performance, test-coverage, simplicity), compound (context-analyzer, lesson-extractor, pattern-matcher, solution-writer), work (test-writer, implementer). Store templates in src/setup/templates/agents/. Each agent needs: role description, when to use, what tools to use, output format.

title: E4: Go Embedding Spike
description:
## E4: Go Embedding Spike — Refined Spec

### Problem Statement
node-llama-cpp loads ~556 MB RSS for embedding operations (36 MB import + 422 MB model + overhead), with 2.6s import time + 2.6s model load. This is excessive for a CLI tool that embeds short text queries (~50 tokens). Evaluate alternatives that reduce memory pressure while preserving embedding quality.

### Baseline Measurements (node-llama-cpp on darwin-arm64, Node v25.2.1)
| Metric | Value |
|--------|-------|
| Import RSS delta | 36.3 MB |
| Model load RSS delta | 422.0 MB |
| Total RSS (loaded) | 556.3 MB |
| Embedding latency | 38-63 ms/query |
| Import time | 2,575 ms |
| Model load time | 2,583 ms |
| Vector dimensions | 768 |
| Model file | 278 MB (Q4_0 GGUF) |

### Candidates to Evaluate

1. **llama-embedding binary (C++ subprocess)** — llama.cpp's own embedding CLI, zero Go dependency
2. **Transformers.js + ONNX Runtime** — Pure JS, uses ONNX format, ~50-100 MB. EmbeddingGemma support in progress (HF issue #1418)
3. **Go + gollama.cpp (purego)** — No CGo, cross-platform, uses shared lib
4. **Go + CGo + llama.cpp (go-skynet)** — Traditional CGo bindings, cross-compile difficulty
5. **ONNX Runtime Node (onnxruntime-node)** — Direct ONNX inference in Node, no browser overhead

### In Scope
- Install Go toolchain if needed
- Build minimal PoC for each viable candidate
- Benchmark: RSS delta, cold-start latency, embedding latency, embedding quality
- Vector compatibility test: cosine_sim(candidate_vec, node_llama_vec) > 0.999 for same model+input
- Assess distribution fit: npm optionalDependencies pattern (esbuild model)
- Produce decision report with recommendation

### Out of Scope
- TypeScript wrapper/integration code
- Consumer migration
- Distribution pipeline
- Full CLI migration

### EARS Requirements (spike-specific)

**Ubiquitous:**
- U-S1: All benchmark results include RSS delta (not absolute), cold-start time, and per-query latency
- U-S2: Vector compatibility tested with ≥5 diverse text inputs
- U-S3: Decision report covers all candidates with quantified comparison

**Event-driven:**
- E-S1: When Go binary produces embedding, cosine_sim vs node-llama-cpp > 0.999 for identical input
- E-S2: When ONNX model produces embedding, cosine_sim vs node-llama-cpp > 0.995 (format conversion may introduce variance)

**State-driven:**
- S-S1: Subprocess-based candidates (Go, llama-embedding) measure RSS in the subprocess, not parent
- S-S2: Each candidate tested on darwin-arm64 (linux-amd64 via CI or Docker if feasible)

**Unwanted:**
- W-S1: Spike code shall NOT be merged into production src/ — all PoCs live in spike/ directory
- W-S2: Spike shall NOT modify existing embeddings or cached vectors

### Scenario Table

| ID | Source | Category | Precondition | Trigger | Expected Outcome |
|----|--------|----------|--------------|---------|------------------|
| S1 | E-S1 | happy | Go binary built, model file present | Embed "TypeScript error handling" | 768-dim vector, cosine_sim > 0.999 vs node-llama-cpp |
| S2 | E-S2 | happy | ONNX model converted, runtime loaded | Embed same text | 768-dim vector, cosine_sim > 0.995 |
| S3 | U-S1 | boundary | Cold start (no warm cache) | First embedding call | Measure cold-start latency < 5s |
| S4 | S-S1 | happy | Subprocess candidate running | Embed 10 queries sequentially | Measure per-query latency, compare to baseline 38-63ms |
| S5 | W-S1 | adversarial | Spike code exists | Run pnpm test | Zero test regressions, spike/ not tested by main suite |
| S6 | U-S3 | happy | All candidates benchmarked | Generate report | Report has RSS, latency, quality, distribution feasibility for each |
| S7 | E-S1 | error | Go binary built, model file missing | Attempt embed | Clear error message, non-zero exit |
| S8 | S-S2 | boundary | darwin-arm64 only | Cross-compile for linux-amd64 | Document feasibility and steps |

### Gate B Decision Criteria
After this spike, the team decides:
- **Proceed to E5 (Go)**: If Go binary meets RSS < 150 MB subprocess, latency < 100ms, cosine_sim > 0.999, and cross-platform is feasible
- **Switch to lighter TS runtime**: If ONNX/Transformers.js meets RSS < 150 MB, acceptable latency, and cosine_sim > 0.995
- **Status quo + optimization**: If no alternative beats node-llama-cpp significantly after E1-E3 optimizations

### Highest Risk
Vector compatibility across providers. If any candidate produces different vectors for same model+input, all cached embeddings would be invalidated. (Lesson L89a2538)

### Research Sources
- Go bindings: go-skynet/go-llama.cpp (CGo), dianlight/gollama.cpp (purego)
- llama.cpp embedding: llama-embedding CLI, llama-server --embeddings endpoint
- Transformers.js: @huggingface/transformers v4, EmbeddingGemma issue #1418
- ONNX Runtime: onnxruntime-node package
- npm distribution pattern: esbuild optionalDependencies model
- User's Go literature: nathandela/docs_personal
notes: Phase: REVIEW COMPLETE | Next: COMPOUND | impl-reviewer APPROVED
close_reason: E4 complete. Gate B decision: Switch to Transformers.js + onnxruntime-node. Cancel E5.

title: Eliminate redundant readMemoryItems call during search
description: During ca search, the JSONL file is parsed twice: once in syncIfNeeded() (to rebuild the SQLite index if stale) and again in searchVector() which calls readMemoryItems() unconditionally. The second parse + Zod validation wastes 5-15ms. Fix: either pass the parsed items from syncIfNeeded through to searchVector, or have searchVector read from the already-synced SQLite cache instead of re-reading JSONL. The latter approach is cleaner since it means searchVector depends only on the SQLite index, which is guaranteed fresh after syncIfNeeded. Key files: src/memory/search/vector.ts, src/memory/storage/sqlite/sync.ts, src/memory/storage/jsonl.ts.

title: Strengthen structural tests: AST barrel validation, recursive detection, shared globs
description:
## Context
Structural tests (src/__structural__/project-structure.test.ts) have 3 gaps identified in review (finding 6).

## Gaps
1. **hasTsSourceFiles** (line 63): Only checks direct children, misses nested .ts files in deeper subdirectories
2. **Barrel re-export validation** (line 125): Uses regex pattern blacklist which can be evaded. Should use AST parsing for robust detection.
3. **Test hygiene scan** (line 201): Only scans src/**/*.test.ts, misses tools/**/*.test.js files

## Fix
1. Make hasTsSourceFiles recursive (check all descendants, not just direct children)
2. For barrel re-export validation: use a tighter allowlist approach (only allow lines matching export/import/comment patterns) instead of a denylist
3. Extend test hygiene scan to also cover tools/**/*.test.js (match vitest config include)
4. Add recursive 'has TS descendants' check for barrel export existence test

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] hasTsSourceFiles checks all descendants recursively
- [ ] Barrel validation uses allowlist (only re-exports) not denylist (block known patterns)
- [ ] Test hygiene scans both src/ and tools/ test files
- [ ] All 17+ structural tests still pass
close_reason: Closed

title: Enhance load-session output for Claude context
description:
## Overview

Enhance `load-session` command output to be optimal for Claude Code context injection via SessionStart hooks.

## Background

When SessionStart hook runs `npx learning-agent load-session`, the stdout becomes Claude's context. The output must be:
- Context-efficient (500-1000 tokens max)
- Human-readable (Claude reads it as markdown)
- Gracefully degrading (no errors if no lessons)
- Self-explanatory (Claude understands what it is)

## Current State

Current output (human mode):
```
## Session Lessons (High Severity)

1. [L12345678] Use Polars for files >100MB
   - Source: user_correction (2025-01-28)
   - Tags: performance, data

---
2 high-severity lessons loaded.
```

## Target Output

```markdown
## Lessons from Past Sessions

These lessons were captured from previous corrections and should inform your work:

1. **Use Polars for files >100MB** (performance)
   Learned: 2025-01-28 via user correction

2. **API v2 requires X-Request-ID header** (api, auth)
   Learned: 2025-01-27 via test failure

Consider these lessons when planning and implementing tasks.
```

## Requirements

### Output Format Changes

1. **Header**: `## Lessons from Past Sessions` (more descriptive)
2. **Intro**: Brief explanation of what these are
3. **Lessons**: Bold insight, tags in parens, date + source
4. **Footer**: Actionable reminder
5. **No IDs**: Remove lesson IDs (not useful to Claude)
6. **No [info] prefix**: Clean output for context injection

### Graceful Degradation

When no lessons exist:
```markdown
## Lessons from Past Sessions

No high-severity lessons captured yet. Learn more: npx learning-agent --help
```

**NOT** an error, just informational.

### Silent Failure Mode

When hook runs, errors should not pollute Claude's context:
- Embedding errors: Catch and return empty (load-session doesn't need embeddings anyway)
- File errors: Catch and return empty with warning to stderr
- Parse errors: Skip corrupted lessons (already implemented)

### Token Efficiency

Target: ~100-150 tokens per lesson, ~50 token header/footer
Max lessons: 5 (current default)
Total budget: ~800 tokens max

## Implementation Notes

### Changes to outputSessionLessonsHuman()

Location: `src/cli.ts` lines 253-270

Current:
```typescript
function outputSessionLessonsHuman(lessons: Lesson[], quiet: boolean): void {
  console.log('## Session Lessons (High Severity)\n');
  // ...
}
```

Target:
```typescript
function outputSessionLessonsHuman(lessons: Lesson[], quiet: boolean): void {
  console.log('## Lessons from Past Sessions\n');
  if (!quiet) {
    console.log('These lessons were captured from previous corrections and should inform your work:\n');
  }
  // Format each lesson compactly
  // ...
}
```

### Changes to load-session Command

- Remove `[info]` prefix when no lessons
- Make output cleaner for context injection
- Ensure exit code 0 even with no lessons

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Output format matches target (header, intro, lessons, footer)
- [ ] No lesson IDs in output
- [ ] No `[info]` prefix in output
- [ ] Graceful message when no lessons (not error)
- [ ] Exit code 0 in all cases
- [ ] Token-efficient (~800 tokens max for 5 lessons)
- [ ] Existing tests still pass
- [ ] New tests for enhanced output format

## Testing

1. Test output format with lessons
2. Test output format without lessons
3. Test `--quiet` flag still works
4. Test `--json` output unchanged
5. Verify token count is reasonable
close_reason: Closed

title: Ensure CLI always releases DB/model resources on exit
description:
## Problem
CLI commands can load SQLite and the embedding model but do not call `closeDb()` / `unloadEmbedding()` before exit, contrary to doc/RESOURCE_LIFECYCLE.md.

## Proposed Fix
Add centralized cleanup for CLI process exit:
- Register `process.on('exit'|'SIGINT'|'SIGTERM')` handlers to call `unloadEmbedding()` and `closeDb()` safely.
- Optionally wrap `program.parseAsync()` in a `try/finally` to ensure cleanup on thrown errors.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Cleanup runs on normal exit and on SIGINT/SIGTERM.
- [ ] Cleanup safe even if resources never loaded (no throw).
- [ ] No behavioral changes to command outputs.

## Tests
1. (Unit) Stub cleanup functions and assert they are called on process exit handler registration in CLI.
2. (Integration) Run a CLI command that opens DB and ensure process exits cleanly (no open handle warnings).

## Files
- src/cli.ts (cleanup registration)
close_reason: Added SIGINT/SIGTERM cleanup handlers to close database on interruption. Avoided 'exit' handler and embedding unload to prevent native module issues.

Warn them NOT to add it as an additional directory to an existing session.' Add to Common Pitfalls: 'Opening the worktree as an additional directory instead of primary (hooks and phase state will be misrouted to the main repo)'. Add a new section explaining WHY: Claude Code hooks always run with cwd=primary dir, settings.json is only read from primary dir, so all state files (.ca-phase-state.json, .ca-failure-state.json) would go to the wrong location.' title: E2: Import Graph Decoupling
description:
## Scope
Break the import chain that forces ~55 MB of node-llama-cpp into modules that only need fs.existsSync.

## In Scope
- Create model-info.ts with isModelAvailable(), MODEL_URI, MODEL_FILENAME, DEFAULT_MODEL_DIR — zero native imports
- Update barrel embeddings/index.ts to re-export from model-info.ts
- Update all consumers that only need availability checks, INCLUDING knowledge subsystem:
  - src/memory/knowledge/search.ts (direct import from nomic.js + model.js)
  - src/memory/knowledge/embed-chunks.ts (direct import from nomic.js)
  - src/memory/knowledge/embed-background.ts (import from embeddings/index.js)
  - src/memory/search/prewarm.ts
  - src/memory/search/vector.ts
  - src/memory/capture/quality.ts
- Add CI check: importing model-info.ts must not increase RSS beyond Node baseline

## Out of Scope
Dynamic import() conversion in nomic.ts/model.ts, test suite changes, runtime replacement

## Fitness Function
node -e "import('./dist/memory/embeddings/model-info.js')" RSS < 50 MB

## EARS Requirements
E3, W3, S1

## Fragile Contract
One accidental transitive native import in model-info.ts breaks Epics 3 + 5. CI check enforces this.

## Assumptions
6+ unit test files currently load node-llama-cpp (~55 MB × 4 threads = ~220 MB) that this eliminates.

title: EPIC 2: Unified Memory Schema
description:
# Extend lesson schema to unified memory items

## Goal
Evolve from lessons-only storage to a unified memory store that holds
lessons, solutions, patterns, and preferences. All searchable via the
same vector search + ranking.

## Scope
- Extend Zod schema: add solution, pattern, preference types to MemoryItem union
- JSONL backward compatibility: read old lesson format, write new unified format
- SQLite schema update for new fields and types
- Search/retrieval handles all memory types transparently
- MCP tools accept and return all memory types
- Lower capture quality bar (remove actionability gate)
- Compaction handles all types

## Exit Criteria
- [ ] Can store/retrieve all 4 memory item types
- [ ] Old lessons still readable from existing JSONL
- [ ] Vector search returns mixed types ranked by relevance
- [ ] MCP tools work with unified schema
- [ ] Quality filter uses lower bar (novelty + specificity only)
- [ ] All tests pass (existing + new)
- [ ] Full review: code, tests, docs

## Context
- Schema defined in: doc/ARCHITECTURE-V2.md (Semantic Memory section)
- Current types.ts: src/types.ts
- Current storage: src/storage/

description: Core Phase 2 feature. A subagent that runs during or after the compounding phase to synthesize lessons into actionable patterns. FLOW: (1) Read all lessons from the JSONL store, (2) Cluster similar mistakes (e.g., multiple 'forgot null check' lessons), (3) Compact clusters into generic patterns with: pattern name, description, frequency count, testable flag, suggested test approach, (4) Update a CCT config file (.claude/lessons/cct-patterns.jsonl or similar) with testable patterns, (5) Optionally update existing lessons with back-references to the generic pattern. TRIGGER: Part of the existing compound workflow (lna compound or similar). Can also be run on-demand. OUTPUT: Updated CCT config file + compacted lesson summaries. This subagent needs access to the lessons store and should use semantic similarity to cluster related mistakes.

title: Remove 90-day archival rule from compact.ts
description:
Remove the age-based archival logic from the compaction system. Lessons should not be auto-archived based on age + zero retrievals. A rarely-retrieved lesson about a rare edge case can be extremely valuable when finally triggered.

## What to remove from src/memory/storage/compact.ts

1. Delete `ARCHIVE_AGE_DAYS` constant (line 26)
2. Delete `shouldArchive()` function (lines 117-122)
3. Delete `getLessonAgeDays` import (line 15)
4. Delete `MONTH_INDEX_OFFSET` and `MONTH_PAD_LENGTH` constants (lines 29, 32)
5. Delete `getArchivePath()` function (lines 52-56)
6. Delete `ARCHIVE_DIR` constant (line 20)
7. Remove `appendFile` from the fs imports (line 10) — only used for archive writing. Keep `mkdir`, `readFile`, `rename`, `writeFile`.

## What to modify in compact()

Simplify `compact()` function (lines 130-215):
- Remove archive split logic (lines 170-180): all non-deleted items go to `toKeep`
- Remove archive-writing block (lines 182-200)
- `CompactResult.archived` stays in interface but always returns 0

## Wider fallout — ALL files that need updates

### src/memory/storage/index.ts (barrel exports, line 33)
Remove: `ARCHIVE_AGE_DAYS`, `ARCHIVE_DIR`, `getArchivePath`
Keep: `compact`, `countTombstones`, `needsCompaction`, `TOMBSTONE_THRESHOLD`, `CompactResult`

### src/commands/management-maintenance.ts
- Line 57-64: Update compact result logging. Remove archive count message. Change description.
- Line 163: Change command description from 'Compact lessons: archive old lessons and remove tombstones' to 'Compact lessons: remove tombstones and invalid records'

### src/commands/management-maintenance.ts stats command
- Line 146: The age distribution display (`<30d, 30-90d, >90d`) can stay — it's informational, not tied to archival

### Test files to update

**src/memory/storage/compact.test.ts**:
- Remove tests asserting `archived > 0` for old lessons
- Remove `createOldLesson` helper if present
- Update data invariant test: `result.lessonsRemaining` = total non-deleted unique lessons (no archive split)
- Keep: TOCTOU test, tombstone removal tests, idempotency test, atomic write test
- Remove imports of `ARCHIVE_AGE_DAYS`, `ARCHIVE_DIR`, `getArchivePath`

**src/cli/compact.test.ts**:
- Line 9: Remove `ARCHIVE_DIR` import
- Line 27: Remove assertion `expect(combined).toContain('archive old lessons')` or update to new description
- Remove any tests that verify archive file creation
- Keep: tests for `--force`, `--dry-run` flags, tombstone removal

**src/commands/management.test.ts**:
- Line 12: Remove `ARCHIVE_DIR` import if present
- Line 196+: Remove/update any compact-related test assertions about archiving

## Update module docstring

Lines 1-8: Change from 'Archiving old lessons (>90 days with 0 retrievals)' to 'Tombstone removal and JSONL rewrite'

## What to keep unchanged
- `countTombstones()`, `needsCompaction()`, `TOMBSTONE_THRESHOLD`
- `CompactResult` interface (keep `archived` field, always 0)
- `parseRawJsonlLines()`
- Atomic write pattern (temp file + rename)
close_reason: Removed ARCHIVE_AGE_DAYS, shouldArchive, getArchivePath, archive-writing block. compact() now only removes tombstones. Updated barrel exports, command description, tests.

title: Epic: Search pipeline performance & consistency
description: After deep analysis by 3 independent reviewers (architecture explorer, Opus challenger, Sonnet profiler), the search pipeline has 5 concrete improvements worth making. The dominant bottleneck is the embedding model being loaded twice per invocation (isModelUsable probe + embedText). Secondary issues: N individual SQLite queries instead of bulk read, redundant JSONL parsing, missing cache pre-warming, and Float32Array/number[] inconsistency between lesson and knowledge paths. All fixes are low-risk, require no new dependencies, and touch well-understood code paths.
notes: Phase: REVIEW COMPLETE | /implementation-reviewer APPROVED. | Next: COMPOUND

description: Capture a lesson from this session

title: Enable parse error logging in CLI commands
description:
Enable onParseError logging in CLI commands that read from JSONL:
- load-session
- list
- search (after sync)
- stats

Currently readLessons() supports onParseError callback but CLI doesn't use it, causing silent failures when users manually edit JSONL.

Example output:
  npx learning-agent load-session
  ⚠️  Line 15: Invalid JSON - unexpected token
  ⚠️  Line 22: Schema validation failed - 'severity' required for type:'full'
  No high-severity lessons found.

Related to investigation of learning_agent-660 (user manual edit silently skipped).

Implementation:
- Add onParseError callback to readLessons() calls in CLI
- Use chalk.yellow for warnings
- Only log in non-JSON output mode (suppress for --json flag)
- Keep existing behavior (continue processing valid lessons)

Acceptance criteria:
- All CLI commands that read JSONL show parse errors
- Errors include line number and message
- --json mode suppresses warnings
- Unit tests verify callback is invoked
close_reason: Superseded by compound-agent mega plan. Fresh start.
