title: REFACTOR: Split setup.ts (693 lines → ~150-200 each)
description:
## Problem

`src/commands/setup.ts` is 693 lines, exceeding the 300-line standard by 393 lines.

This violates:
- `.claude/CLAUDE.md`: 'Files < 300 lines'

## Current Structure

The file contains 4 main commands plus many helpers:
1. `init` - Initialize learning-agent in a repo (lines ~50-250)
2. `hooks` - Configure git hooks (lines ~255-400)
3. `setup claude` - Configure Claude Code integration (lines ~405-550)
4. `download-model` - Download embedding model (lines ~555-693)

Plus helper functions:
- `createDirectoryStructure`
- `findFirstTopLevelExitLine`
- `updateClaudeSettings`
- Template generation functions

## Proposed Split

```
src/commands/setup/
├── index.ts           # Re-exports registerSetupCommands (20 lines)
├── init.ts            # init command + directory structure (~180 lines)
├── hooks.ts           # hooks command + git integration (~150 lines)
├── claude.ts          # setup claude command + settings (~180 lines)
├── download-model.ts  # download-model command (~140 lines)
└── templates.ts       # Shared template strings (~100 lines)
```

## Implementation Steps

### 1. Create directory structure
```bash
mkdir -p src/commands/setup
```

### 2. Create `templates.ts`
Extract all template strings:
- AGENTS.md template
- Hook script templates
- Slash command templates

### 3. Create `init.ts`
Extract:
- `init` command and action handler
- `createDirectoryStructure` function
- Directory/file creation logic

### 4. Create `hooks.ts`
Extract:
- `hooks` command and action handler
- Git hook detection/insertion logic
- `findFirstTopLevelExitLine` function

### 5. Create `claude.ts`
Extract:
- `setup claude` command and action handler
- `updateClaudeSettings` function
- Claude settings file manipulation

### 6. Create `download-model.ts`
Extract:
- `download-model` command and action handler
- Model download progress logic

### 7. Create `index.ts`
```typescript
import type { Command } from 'commander';
import { registerInitCommand } from './init.js';
import { registerHooksCommand } from './hooks.js';
import { registerClaudeCommand } from './claude.js';
import { registerDownloadModelCommand } from './download-model.js';

export function registerSetupCommands(program: Command): void {
  registerInitCommand(program);
  registerHooksCommand(program);
  registerClaudeCommand(program);
  registerDownloadModelCommand(program);
}
```

### 8. Update imports
- `src/commands/index.ts` should import from `./setup/index.js`

## Verification

```bash
pnpm build   # Must succeed
pnpm test    # All tests must pass
pnpm lint    # No new violations

# Verify each new file is under 300 lines
wc -l src/commands/setup/*.ts
```

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `setup.ts` deleted
- [ ] 5-6 new files created in `src/commands/setup/`
- [ ] Each file < 300 lines
- [ ] All CLI commands still work identically
- [ ] All tests pass
- [ ] No functionality changes
close_reason: Split setup.ts (1022 lines) into 8 files under 300 lines each: claude-helpers.ts (130), claude.ts (142), download-model.ts (55), hooks.ts (190), index.ts (28), init.ts (255), templates.ts (281), types.ts (13). All 984 tests pass.

title: Add similarity warning to ca learn command
description:
Modify the `handleLearn()` function in `src/commands/capture.ts` to show a warning when the just-captured lesson is semantically similar to existing lessons.

## Depends on
- findSimilarLessons() function (learning_agent-vos)

## Where to modify

`src/commands/capture.ts`, function `handleLearn()` (lines 116-198). Add the similarity check AFTER line 197 (after the existing success output), before the function closes at line 198.

## What to add

A best-effort try/catch block that:
1. Checks `isModelAvailable()` from `../memory/embeddings/index.js` — skip entirely if false
2. Calls `syncIfNeeded(repoRoot)` from `../memory/storage/index.js` — ensures SQLite has the just-appended lesson
3. Calls `findSimilarLessons(repoRoot, item.insight, { excludeId: item.id })`
   IMPORTANT: Pass `item.insight` ONLY, not `trigger + insight`. Generic triggers like 'Manual capture' inflate similarity scores.
4. If results found (length > 0):
   - Print blank line
   - Print warning: 'Similar lessons found:'
   - For each result (max 3): print ID, score as percentage, truncated insight
   - Print blank line
   - Print: `Run 'npx ca clean-lessons' to review and resolve.`
5. Call `unloadEmbedding()` to release ~150MB model memory (in finally block)

## Critical constraints

- The entire block MUST be in try/catch — similarity check failure must NEVER break the learn command
- The lesson is ALREADY saved at this point (line 184) — this is informational, not a gate
- Use existing `out` helper from `./shared.js` for styled output (e.g., `out.warn()`)
- Use `chalk` (already imported at line 187) for dim formatting
- Only show top 3 matches to avoid overwhelming output
- `unloadEmbedding()` must be called in finally block (both success and error paths)

## Expected output example

```
Learned: Always use file-based persistence for cross-process hooks
ID: L12345678

Similar lessons found:
  Ld5fa1a59 (92%) Use file persistence, not module variables, for hoo...
  La1b2c3d4 (85%) Cross-process state must use filesystem, not memory...

Run 'npx ca clean-lessons' to review and resolve.
```

## Tests

In `src/commands/capture.test.ts`:
- learn command outputs similarity warning when similar lessons exist (mock findSimilarLessons)
- learn command succeeds without warning when no similar lessons found
- learn command succeeds even when embedding model unavailable (isModelAvailable returns false)
- learn command succeeds even when findSimilarLessons throws (graceful degradation)
close_reason: handleLearn now shows similarity warning post-capture. Extracted to checkSimilarityPostCapture helper. 4 new tests.

title: Split cli.test.ts by command
description:
## Purpose

Break the 4,000-line cli.test.ts into smaller files for better parallelization.

## Current State

`cli.test.ts` contains ~55 tests covering all CLI commands in one file. Vitest parallelizes at the file level, so this monolith runs sequentially.

## Target Structure

```
src/cli/
├── add.test.ts         # 'lna add' command tests
├── search.test.ts      # 'lna search' command tests
├── list.test.ts        # 'lna list' command tests
├── show.test.ts        # 'lna show' command tests
├── sync.test.ts        # 'lna sync' command tests
├── compact.test.ts     # 'lna compact' command tests
├── meta.test.ts        # --version, --help, aliases
└── cli-helpers.ts      # Shared test utilities (execCli, tempDir setup)
```

## Shared Test Utilities

Extract common patterns into `cli-helpers.ts`:
```typescript
export function execCli(args: string, cwd: string): string {
  return execSync(`node --import tsx/esm ../dist/cli.js ${args}`, {
    cwd,
    encoding: 'utf-8',
  });
}

export async function setupCliTestDir(): Promise<string> {
  return mkdtemp(join(tmpdir(), 'learning-agent-cli-'));
}
```

## Migration Strategy

1. Create `src/cli/` directory
2. Extract shared utilities first
3. Move tests by command, updating imports
4. Delete original cli.test.ts
5. Verify all tests pass

## Verification

1. Same number of tests as before (55)
2. All tests pass
3. Coverage unchanged
4. Execution faster due to parallelization
close_reason: All optimizations implemented and verified

title: Task 1: Lock file module (embed-lock.ts)
description:
Create PID-based lock file module to prevent concurrent embedding processes.

## Context
When background embedding runs after ca init/setup AND a docs/ commit triggers post-commit hook simultaneously, two embedding processes could compete. This lock file prevents that.

## Files
- NEW: src/memory/knowledge/embed-lock.ts
- NEW: src/memory/knowledge/embed-lock.test.ts

## Implementation
Lock file path: .claude/.cache/embed.lock
Lock file content: JSON { pid: number, startedAt: ISO string }

### Functions
- acquireEmbedLock(repoRoot: string): LockResult
  - Returns { acquired: true, release: () => void } or { acquired: false, holder: number }
  - Use writeFileSync with 'wx' flag (exclusive create) for atomicity
  - On EEXIST: read PID, check staleness via process.kill(pid, 0)
  - If stale (process dead): overwrite lock. If alive: return acquired: false
  - release() calls unlinkSync on lock file

- isEmbedLocked(repoRoot: string): boolean
  - Check if lock file exists with live PID

### Type
interface LockResult = { acquired: true; release: () => void } | { acquired: false; holder: number }

## Tests (TDD - write first)
1. acquireEmbedLock creates lock file with current PID
2. Double acquire returns acquired: false with holder PID
3. release() removes lock file
4. After release, acquire succeeds again
5. Stale lock (dead PID 999999) is overridden on acquire
6. isEmbedLocked returns true when locked, false when not
7. Lock file directory created if missing

## Dependencies
None (Layer 0). Uses only node:fs, node:path, node:process.

## Patterns to follow
- File-based cross-process state pattern from src/setup/hooks-failure-tracker.ts (.ca-failure-state.json)
- mkdirSync(dir, { recursive: true }) for directory creation
- No external dependencies
close_reason: Closed

title: setup claude should default to project-local, not global
description:
'setup claude' installs to ~/.claude/settings.json (global) by default. This affects ALL projects, not just the one being initialized.

**Observed behavior:**
```
(base) MacBookPro:own_website Nathan$ npx learning-agent setup claude
[ok] Claude Code hooks installed
  Location: ~/.claude/settings.json   <-- GLOBAL!
```

**Expected behavior:**
- Default should be project-local: .claude/settings.json
- Global should require explicit --global flag
- Or at minimum, warn user that this affects all projects

**Rationale:**
- User expects 'init' and 'setup' to affect current project only
- Global hooks load lessons from wrong projects
- Could confuse Claude with irrelevant lessons

**Proposed solution:**
- Change default to project-local
- Add --global flag (currently it's --project for local)
- Swap the semantics: default=local, --global=global
notes:
## Tests to Implement

### Unit Tests
- [ ] Default (no flags) -> installs to .claude/settings.json (project)
- [ ] --global flag -> installs to ~/.claude/settings.json
- [ ] Output shows correct location (project vs global)
- [ ] getClaudeSettingsPath(false) returns project path (BREAKING CHANGE)

### Integration Tests
- [ ] init + setup claude -> both in project directory
- [ ] Global install doesn't affect other projects
- [ ] Project install only affects current project

### Edge Cases
- [ ] Project .claude/ doesn't exist -> create it
- [ ] Running from subdirectory -> finds repo root
- [ ] No git repo -> error or use cwd?
- [ ] Both project and global have hooks -> which takes precedence? (Claude behavior)
- [ ] --uninstall with wrong scope -> helpful error
close_reason: Closed

title: Optimize test pipeline: parallelization, grouping, and ergonomics
description: Improve overall test execution speed and developer experience. (1) Better parallelization: profile current bottlenecks, optimize thread pool config, identify serialization points. (2) Smarter grouping: make test:segment and test:critical more useful with well-defined test categories (unit/integration/e2e, fast/slow, by module). (3) Ergonomic subset running: easy commands to run tests for a specific module or feature area. (4) Remove bottlenecks: identify and fix slow tests, unnecessary setup/teardown. No hard speed target -- just measurably faster than current baseline.

title: EPIC 6: Work Phase
description:
# Implement /compound:work workflow

## Goal
Third workflow phase: execute the plan using agent teams with adaptive TDD.
Lead delegates beads tasks to worker agents. TDD pairs for non-trivial tasks.

## Scope
- work.md command: workflow instructions for Claude
- work/SKILL.md: TDD workflow, team structure, execution patterns
- Agent definitions: test-writer.md, implementer.md
- Adaptive TDD pair model:
  - Trivial: single agent, no TDD
  - Simple: sequential (test-writer finishes, hands to implementer)
  - Complex: iterative (ping-pong between test-writer and implementer)
- Lead in delegate mode: coordinates, does not code
- Memory injection per agent: each agent gets relevant items for their task
- Beads task lifecycle: claim -> in_progress -> close

## Exit Criteria
- /compound:work invokable as slash command
- Spawns agent team based on plan/beads tasks
- TDD pairs work for simple tasks (sequential handoff)
- TDD pairs work for complex tasks (iterative)
- Lead delegates effectively (no coding by lead)
- Each agent receives relevant memory items
- Beads tasks updated through lifecycle
- Integration tests verify the workflow

## Context
- Architecture: doc/ARCHITECTURE-V2.md (Phase 3: Work)
- Agent model: Team with TDD pairs (adaptive)
- Agent teams docs: https://code.claude.com/docs/en/agent-teams
- Depends on: plan phase output (beads tasks)
close_reason: Closed

title: Release v0.2.0: Claude Code Hooks Integration
description:
## Overview

Prepare learning-agent v0.2.0 for npm publication. This release introduces Claude Code hooks integration for automatic lesson injection.

## Release Highlights

- **setup claude** command for automatic hook installation
- **Enhanced init** with pre-commit hooks
- **Session-end protocol** in AGENTS.md template
- Improved output formatting for context injection

## Pre-Release Checklist

### 1. Code Quality
- [ ] All tests pass (`pnpm test`)
- [ ] Lint passes (`pnpm lint`)
- [ ] No skipped tests
- [ ] No console.log debugging statements
- [ ] No TODO/FIXME comments in release code

### 2. Documentation
- [ ] README.md updated with new features
- [ ] CHANGELOG.md updated for v0.2.0
- [ ] AGENTS.md (project) reflects current state
- [ ] JSDoc on all public APIs
- [ ] CLI --help text accurate

### 3. Package Verification
- [ ] package.json version bumped to 0.2.0
- [ ] `pnpm pack --dry-run` shows correct files
- [ ] No dev files in tarball (tests, .claude, etc.)
- [ ] All dependencies correctly listed
- [ ] Peer dependencies documented

### 4. Build Verification
- [ ] Clean build succeeds (`rm -rf dist && pnpm build`)
- [ ] Type declarations generated (dist/*.d.ts)
- [ ] CLI executable works (`node dist/cli.js --version`)
- [ ] ESM imports work

### 5. Integration Testing
- [ ] Fresh install in test repo works
- [ ] `npx learning-agent init` works
- [ ] `npx learning-agent setup claude` works (when implemented)
- [ ] Hooks fire correctly in Claude Code

### 6. Final Steps
- [ ] Version bump committed
- [ ] Git tag created (v0.2.0)
- [ ] `pnpm publish --dry-run` succeeds
- [ ] Publish to npm
- [ ] Push tags to GitHub
- [ ] GitHub release created with notes

## Blockers

This release depends on completing:
- learning_agent-p4d: setup claude command
- learning_agent-793: Enhanced load-session output
- learning_agent-6ve: AGENTS.md template update

## Timeline

Complete all feature work first, then run through checklist.
close_reason: v0.2.0 released to npm (with bugs - see learning_agent-egt for fixes)

title: Add pre-commit hook with lesson reminder for Claude
description:
Implement a pre-commit hook that reminds Claude to consider capturing lessons before committing.

## Background

Based on beads framework analysis, hooks are implemented as:
1. Thin shell scripts in .git/hooks/ that call the main CLI
2. Scripts are installed during 'init' command
3. Hook runs CLI with appropriate arguments, CLI outputs prompt/reminder

## Requirements

1. **Hook installation**: During 'npx learning-agent init', install pre-commit hook shim
2. **Prompt reminder**: Hook outputs a prompt like: 'Before committing, have you captured any valuable lessons from this session? Consider corrections, mistakes, or insights worth remembering.'
3. **Non-blocking**: Hook should NOT block the commit - just output reminder text
4. **Idempotent**: Re-running init should not duplicate hooks
5. **Claude decides**: The prompt is for Claude to decide if lessons are needed

## Implementation Pattern (from beads)

```bash
#!/bin/sh
# .git/hooks/pre-commit (installed by init)
npx learning-agent hooks run pre-commit
```

## CLI Addition

Add 'hooks run <hook-name>' command that outputs the appropriate prompt.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] 'init' command installs pre-commit hook
- [ ] Hook is executable
- [ ] Hook outputs lesson reminder prompt
- [ ] Re-running init does not duplicate hook
- [ ] 'hooks run pre-commit' command works standalone
close_reason: Implemented pre-commit hook with TDD, all tests pass

title: Commit gate: Redesign pre-commit prompt with checklist format
description:
## Overview
Improve the pre-commit hook prompt to use a checklist format that forces Claude to explicitly reflect on lessons before proceeding.

## Current Prompt (too easy to dismiss)
```
Before committing, have you captured any valuable lessons from this session?
Consider: corrections, mistakes, or insights worth remembering.

To capture a lesson:
  npx lna capture --trigger "what happened" --insight "what to do" --yes
```

## New Prompt (checklist gate)
```
╔══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╗
║                    LESSON CAPTURE CHECKPOINT                 ║
╠══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╣
║ STOP. Before this commit completes, review:                  ║
║                                                              ║
║ [ ] Did the user correct me during this session?             ║
║ [ ] Did I discover something unexpected about this codebase? ║
║ [ ] Did a test fail → fix → pass cycle reveal an insight?    ║
║ [ ] Did I make an assumption that was wrong?                 ║
║                                                              ║
║ If ANY checkbox applies, capture the lesson NOW:             ║
║   npx lna learn "<insight>" --trigger "<what happened>"    ║
║                                                              ║
║ If NONE apply, you may proceed.                              ║
╚══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╝
```

## Files to Modify
- src/commands/setup/templates.ts - Update PRE_COMMIT_MESSAGE constant

## Testing
- Unit test: Message format validation
- Visual inspection: Ensure message renders correctly in terminal

## Rationale
The checklist format:
1. Forces Claude to actively consider each scenario
2. Provides concrete criteria (not vague 'anything worth remembering')
3. Uses visual framing (box) to signal importance
4. Clear instruction at the end
close_reason: Closed

title: Add 72h TTL to .ca-phase-state.json reads
description: File: src/commands/phase-check.ts, function getPhaseState() (lines 103-113). After parsing and validating the state, add a staleness check: if Date.now() - Date.parse(state.started_at) > 72 * 60 * 60 * 1000, call cleanPhaseState(repoRoot) and return null. This prevents stale phase state from interrupted LFG sessions persisting indefinitely. The 72h window (vs 24h) covers multi-day epics that pause overnight. Pattern already exists in hooks.ts readFailureState() (1h TTL at line 183). Acceptance: (1) TTL check added, (2) stale state auto-cleaned, (3) test for TTL expiry, (4) test that 71h state is still valid.

title: Create ca watch command for live trace pretty-printing
description:
## What
New CLI command: ca watch [--epic <id>] that tails the latest trace JSONL file and pretty-prints events in a human-readable, colored format.

## Why
The trace JSONL is machine-readable but not human-scannable. The watch command makes the micro log useful by formatting events into a compact, colored timeline that a user can observe in a second terminal while the loop runs.

## Implementation details
- New file: src/commands/watch.ts
- Register in src/commands/index.ts (or wherever commands are wired up)
- Behavior:
  1. Find the latest trace file: ls -t agent_logs/trace_*.jsonl | head -1 (or follow a .latest symlink)
  2. If --epic provided, find the specific trace file for that epic
  3. tail -f the file, parsing each JSON line and formatting it
- Event formatting rules:
  - tool_use events: [HH:MM:SS] TOOL  <tool_name> -> <key_input>  OK/FAIL <duration>
  - text_delta events: aggregate into prompts, show [HH:MM:SS] PROMPT '<truncated>' (<token_count> tokens)
  - API request events: [HH:MM:SS] TOKENS Turn N: X in / Y out ($Z.ZZ)
  - Epic markers: [HH:MM:SS] MARKER EPIC_COMPLETE (highlighted/bold)
  - Phase transitions: [HH:MM:SS] PHASE plan -> work
- Colors: green for OK, red for FAIL, yellow for MARKER, dim for TOKENS
- Graceful handling: if no trace file exists yet, wait and retry every 2s with a message
- Exit cleanly on Ctrl+C

## Acceptance criteria
- ca watch starts tailing the latest trace file
- ca watch --epic <id> tails a specific epic's trace
- Events are formatted in the compact timeline format shown in the epic description
- Colors work in standard terminals
- Clean exit on Ctrl+C or when the trace file ends
close_reason: watch.ts created: formatStreamEvent, findLatestTraceFile, registered in CLI

title: Move real embedding tests out of thread pool to singleFork
description: embed-chunks.test.ts and indexing.test.ts run in the unit workspace (threads, shared heap). When the model IS available, their .skipIf(skipEmbedding) embedding tests execute real embedText()/embedChunks() which loads ~150MB native memory. This causes SIGABRT during worker cleanup (pre-existing node-llama-cpp dispose bug). Options: (1) mock embedText in these files and keep real embedding tests only in singleFork pool, or (2) move the embedding-dependent test blocks to files under src/memory/embeddings/ which already use singleFork. This is a follow-up to commit 6b14b61 which fixed the 63GB leak from module-evaluation-time probes.

title: REFACTOR: Split management.ts (759 lines → ~150-200 each)
description:
## Problem

`src/commands/management.ts` is 759 lines, exceeding the 300-line standard by 459 lines.

This violates:
- `.claude/CLAUDE.md`: 'Files < 300 lines'
- `doc/standards/typescript-best-practices.md`: Module structure standards

## Current Structure

The file contains 9 distinct commands:
1. `wrong` - Mark lesson as incorrect (lines ~30-85)
2. `validate` - Restore invalidated lesson (lines ~90-175)
3. `compact` - Compact JSONL storage (lines ~180-260)
4. `stats` - Show lesson statistics (lines ~265-350)
5. `rebuild` - Rebuild SQLite index (lines ~355-400)
6. `export` - Export lessons to file (lines ~405-480)
7. `import` - Import lessons from file (lines ~485-590)
8. `show` - Show single lesson (lines ~595-680)
9. `update` - Update lesson fields (lines ~685-730)
10. `delete` - Delete a lesson (lines ~735-759)

## Proposed Split

```
src/commands/management/
├── index.ts           # Re-exports registerManagementCommands (20 lines)
├── invalidation.ts    # wrong, validate commands (~150 lines)
├── compaction.ts      # compact, rebuild, stats commands (~200 lines)
├── transfer.ts        # export, import commands (~180 lines)
└── crud.ts            # show, update, delete commands (~200 lines)
```

## Implementation Steps

### 1. Create directory structure
```bash
mkdir -p src/commands/management
```

### 2. Create `invalidation.ts`
Extract:
- `wrong` command and action handler
- `validate` command and action handler
- Shared helper: field copying logic (if any)

### 3. Create `compaction.ts`
Extract:
- `compact` command and action handler
- `rebuild` command and action handler
- `stats` command and action handler

### 4. Create `transfer.ts`
Extract:
- `export` command and action handler
- `import` command and action handler

### 5. Create `crud.ts`
Extract:
- `show` command and action handler
- `update` command and action handler
- `delete` command and action handler
- `wasLessonDeleted` helper function

### 6. Create `index.ts`
```typescript
import type { Command } from 'commander';
import { registerInvalidationCommands } from './invalidation.js';
import { registerCompactionCommands } from './compaction.js';
import { registerTransferCommands } from './transfer.js';
import { registerCrudCommands } from './crud.js';

export function registerManagementCommands(program: Command): void {
  registerInvalidationCommands(program);
  registerCompactionCommands(program);
  registerTransferCommands(program);
  registerCrudCommands(program);
}
```

### 7. Update imports
- `src/commands/index.ts` should import from `./management/index.js`

## Verification

```bash
pnpm build   # Must succeed
pnpm test    # All tests must pass
pnpm lint    # No new violations

# Verify each new file is under 300 lines
wc -l src/commands/management/*.ts
```

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `management.ts` deleted
- [ ] 4 new files created in `src/commands/management/`
- [ ] Each file < 300 lines
- [ ] All CLI commands still work identically
- [ ] All tests pass
- [ ] No functionality changes

title: Move agent role definitions from .claude/agents/ to .claude/skills/compound/agents/ as role skills
description:
## Problem
Agent role definitions (.claude/agents/compound/*.md) only work as subagents via the Task tool. When Claude creates an AgentTeam (TeamCreate), teammates are spawned as generic 'general-purpose' agents and cannot inherit from .claude/agents/ files. This is a confirmed Claude Code limitation (GitHub issue #24316).

## Solution
Move agent role definitions to .claude/skills/compound/agents/<name>/SKILL.md. Skills are automatically available to ALL Claude Code sessions — including agent team teammates. When the lead spawns a teammate, it just says 'You are the test-writer. Follow the test-writer skill. Your task: X.' The teammate discovers the skill natively.

## Benefits
- Single source of truth for agent roles (no dual maintenance)
- Works for BOTH subagents and agent teams (skills are universal)
- Forward-compatible: when #24316 ships, .claude/agents/ files can reference these skills via 'skills:' frontmatter
- Teammates get role knowledge automatically — no prompt embedding needed

## Target Directory Structure
```
.claude/skills/compound/
  brainstorm/SKILL.md          <- phase skill (existing, unchanged)
  plan/SKILL.md                <- existing, unchanged
  work/SKILL.md                <- existing, unchanged
  review/SKILL.md              <- existing, unchanged
  compound/SKILL.md            <- existing, unchanged
  agents/
    test-writer/SKILL.md       <- NEW: role skill (from agents-workflow.ts)
    implementer/SKILL.md       <- NEW: role skill
    context-analyzer/SKILL.md  <- NEW: role skill
    lesson-extractor/SKILL.md  <- NEW: role skill
    pattern-matcher/SKILL.md   <- NEW: role skill
    solution-writer/SKILL.md   <- NEW: role skill
    repo-analyst/SKILL.md      <- NEW: role skill (from agents-review.ts)
    memory-analyst/SKILL.md    <- NEW: role skill
    security-reviewer/SKILL.md <- NEW: role skill
    architecture-reviewer/SKILL.md <- NEW
    performance-reviewer/SKILL.md  <- NEW
    test-coverage-reviewer/SKILL.md <- NEW
    simplicity-reviewer/SKILL.md   <- NEW
    compounding/SKILL.md       <- NEW: role skill (from agents-phase11.ts)
    audit/SKILL.md             <- NEW: role skill
    doc-gardener/SKILL.md      <- NEW: role skill
    cct-subagent/SKILL.md      <- NEW: role skill
    drift-detector/SKILL.md    <- NEW: role skill
```

Note: external-reviewer-gemini and external-reviewer-codex stay as .claude/agents/ files because they are actual subagent definitions with special tool/model config that skills cannot express.

## Skill Format for Agent Roles
Each role skill follows this format:
```markdown
---
name: <Role Name>
description: <When this role applies — Claude uses this for auto-discovery>
---

# <Role Name>

## Role
<What this agent does — one paragraph>

## Instructions
<Numbered steps — what to do when assigned a task>

## Collaboration
<How to communicate with teammates via SendMessage>

## Output Format
<What to report back>
```

Key differences from current agent .md files:
- NO 'model:' in frontmatter (skills don't set model — the lead chooses at spawn time)
- NO 'tools:' in frontmatter (skills don't restrict tools — that is a subagent-only feature)
- YES 'description:' (required for skill auto-discovery)
- Keep Collaboration section (critical for team coordination)
- All npx ca search/learn references (already done in v1.2.6)

## Implementation Steps

### 1. Create new template file: src/setup/templates/agent-skills.ts
- Export AGENT_ROLE_SKILLS: Record<string, string> — maps 'agent-name' to skill content
- Convert each agent from agents-workflow.ts, agents-review.ts, agents-phase11.ts
- Conversion: strip model/tools from frontmatter, keep name/description, keep body as-is
- 18 agent role skills total (exclude 2 external reviewers)

### 2. Update src/setup/templates/index.ts
- Export AGENT_ROLE_SKILLS from agent-skills.ts

### 3. Update src/setup/primitives.ts
- Add installAgentRoleSkills() function:
  - Writes to .claude/skills/compound/agents/<name>/SKILL.md
  - Same pattern as installPhaseSkills() but nested under agents/
- Keep installAgentTemplates() for now (external reviewers still need .claude/agents/)
  - Filter AGENT_TEMPLATES to only install external reviewers
  - OR: create a separate EXTERNAL_AGENT_TEMPLATES export (already exists!)

### 4. Update src/setup/init.ts and src/setup/setup-all.ts
- Call installAgentRoleSkills() during setup
- Update user-facing output messages

### 5. Update commands.ts — work.md, review.md, compound.md
- Where commands reference agent roles, add: 'Teammates should follow their role skill in .claude/skills/compound/agents/'
- Example for work.md step 5: 'Make a task list and deploy an AgentTeam. Teammates follow their role skills: test-writer writes failing tests, implementer makes them pass.'

### 6. Update skills.ts — work, review, compound phase skills
- Same references to agent role skills

### 7. Remove agent templates that are now skills
- agents-workflow.ts: Remove all 6 templates (now skills)
- agents-review.ts: Remove all 7 templates (now skills)
- agents-phase11.ts: Remove all 5 templates (now skills)
- agents.ts: Only re-export EXTERNAL_AGENT_TEMPLATES
- installAgentTemplates() in primitives.ts: only install external reviewer agents

### 8. Update test files
- Create src/setup/templates/agent-skills.test.ts for new skill templates
  - Test each skill has YAML frontmatter with name + description
  - Test each skill has Role, Instructions, Output Format sections
  - Test character limits if needed (skills currently have 4000 char limit — may need to raise)
- Update agents tests:
  - agents-workflow.test.ts → remove or convert to test agent-skills
  - agents-review.test.ts → remove or convert
  - agents-phase11.test.ts → remove or convert
  - agents-external.test.ts → keep (external reviewers stay as agents)
- Update phase tests that assert on agent references

### 9. Verify the full pipeline
- pnpm test — all pass
- pnpm lint — zero violations
- Test ca setup in a clean directory — verify skills are installed correctly
- Verify .claude/skills/compound/agents/ directory structure is correct

## Character Limit Consideration
Current phase skills have a 4000 char limit. Agent role skills are smaller (most are 500-1500 chars), so this is not an issue. But verify each stays under the limit.

## What NOT to Change
- Phase skills (brainstorm, plan, work, review, compound) — stay as-is in skills.ts
- External reviewer agents (gemini, codex) — stay as .claude/agents/ because they need model/tools config
- The actual CLI code (src/cli/) — no changes
- MCP server code (src/mcp/) — no changes

## TDD Approach
1. Write agent-skills.test.ts first (structure, frontmatter, sections)
2. Verify tests fail (file doesn't exist yet)
3. Create agent-skills.ts with converted content
4. Verify tests pass
5. Update primitives.ts and setup files
6. Update commands/skills references
7. Remove old agent template files and update their tests
8. Full pnpm test && pnpm lint
notes:
REFINED APPROACH (per user):
- ALL 18 agents → role skill in .claude/skills/compound/agents/<name>/SKILL.md
- 6 subagents (repo-analyst, memory-analyst, audit, doc-gardener, cct-subagent, drift-detector) → ALSO keep thin .claude/agents/compound/<name>.md wrapper pointing to skill
- 12 AgentTeam members → skills ONLY (no agent file)
- 2 external reviewers → agents ONLY (no skill, unchanged)
- Each role skill has ## Deployment section: 'AgentTeam member' or 'Subagent'
- Thin agent wrapper format: frontmatter (name, description, model) + 'Follow the <name> role skill'
close_reason: Agent roles moved to skills, commands consolidated. 86 files, 1988 tests pass, lint clean.

title: EPIC 8: Compound Phase
description:
# Implement /compound:compound workflow

## Goal
Fifth workflow phase: capture what was learned. Analyze the completed work,
extract knowledge, store as memory items. Close the compound loop.

## Scope
- compound.md command: workflow instructions for Claude
- compound/SKILL.md: what to capture, quality filters, item schema
- Compound agent definitions:
  - context-analyzer.md: summarize what happened (plan + diff + tests)
  - lesson-extractor.md: identify mistakes, corrections, discoveries
  - pattern-matcher.md: find recurring patterns across sessions
  - solution-writer.md: formulate structured memory items
- Quality filter (lower bar):
  - Novelty check (>0.85 similarity = skip)
  - Basic specificity check
  - NO actionability gate (capture more, prune later)
- Memory item storage: trigger + insight + tags + severity + evidence
- Supersedes/related linking: connect new items to existing knowledge
- User confirmation for high-severity items only

## Exit Criteria
- /compound:compound invokable as slash command
- Spawns analysis agent team (3-4 agents)
- Agents analyze recent work context (diff, tests, plan)
- Quality filter prevents duplicates but allows broad capture
- Memory items stored with proper linking
- High-severity items prompt for user confirmation
- Integration tests verify the workflow

## Context
- Architecture: doc/ARCHITECTURE-V2.md (Phase 5: Compound)
- This phase PRODUCES memory items - the compound loop closes here
- Capture strategy: lower bar, prune later
- Current quality filter: src/capture/quality.ts

title: BUG: Pre-commit hook appended after exit statements
description:
When pre-commit hook is appended to existing hooks, it's placed AFTER exit statements, meaning it never actually runs.

**Observed behavior:**
User's existing pre-commit hook ends with 'exit 0' or 'exit 1'. The learning-agent block is appended at the very end:
```bash
# ... existing hook code ...
exit 0
fi

# Learning Agent pre-commit hook (appended)
npx learning-agent hooks run pre-commit
```

The learning-agent code is unreachable because the script already exited.

**Expected behavior:**
- Detect existing exit statements and insert before them
- Or wrap the existing hook and ensure our code runs
- Or use a pre-commit framework (husky, etc.)

**Root cause:**
- installPreCommitHook() just appends to file content
- No parsing of existing hook logic

**Impact:** P1 - Pre-commit reminders never shown when user has existing hooks
notes:
## Tests to Implement

### Unit Tests
- [ ] Hook inserted BEFORE 'exit 0' statement
- [ ] Hook inserted BEFORE 'exit 1' statement  
- [ ] Hook inserted BEFORE 'exit $?' statement
- [ ] Multiple exit statements -> inserted before ALL of them
- [ ] No exit statement -> simple append works

### Integration Tests
- [ ] Existing hook with exit still runs our code
- [ ] Existing hook logic preserved and functional
- [ ] Hook output visible during commit

### Edge Cases
- [ ] Exit inside function definition -> don't insert there
- [ ] Exit in heredoc/string -> don't insert there
- [ ] Exit with variable: 'exit $status' 
- [ ] Conditional exit: 'if [ cond ]; then exit 1; fi'
- [ ] Exit at multiple indent levels
- [ ] Hook file has no shebang -> add one?
- [ ] Hook file is binary/not shell -> skip gracefully
close_reason: Closed

title: Refactor src/cli.ts to reduce complexity
description:
The `src/cli.ts` file has grown to approximately 2000 lines, becoming a "God Class" that violates the Single Responsibility Principle. It currently handles:
- Argument parsing (Commander.js configuration)
- Business logic orchestration
- Output formatting
- File I/O and System interactions
- Hook management

**Problems:**
- **Cognitive Load**: Hard to navigate and understand for new contributors.
- **Testability**: Logic coupled with CLI presentation is harder to unit test in isolation.
- **Merge Conflicts**: High activity file, prone to conflicts.

**Proposal:**
1.  **Extract Commands**: Move each command action into a dedicated file in `src/commands/` (e.g., `src/commands/init.ts`, `src/commands/capture.ts`).
2.  **Shared Utils**: Consolidate shared logic (like `getRepoRoot`, formatting) in `src/cli-utils.ts` or `src/lib/`.
3.  **Thin Entry Point**: `src/cli.ts` should only define the Commander configuration and dispatch to handlers.

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- `src/cli.ts` is significantly smaller (< 200 lines).
- Each command has its own module.
- All tests pass.
close_reason: Refactored cli.ts from ~2000 lines to ~100 lines. All 18 commands extracted to src/cli/commands/. Shared utilities in src/cli/shared.ts. All 648 tests pass. Lint passes.

title: Create Claude Plugin for learning-agent
description:
Create a Claude Plugin following beads pattern.

## Structure
```
.claude-plugin/
├── plugin.json              # Manifest with hooks
└── commands/                # Slash commands
    ├── learn.md             # /learn
    ├── list.md              # /list
    ├── check-plan.md        # /check-plan
    ├── prime.md             # /prime
    ├── show.md              # /show
    ├── wrong.md             # /wrong
    └── stats.md             # /stats
```

## plugin.json
```json
{
  "name": "learning-agent",
  "description": "Session memory for Claude Code - capture and retrieve lessons",
  "version": "0.2.3",
  "author": {
    "name": "Nathan Delacrétaz",
    "url": "https://github.com/Nathandela"
  },
  "repository": "https://github.com/Nathandela/learning_agent",
  "license": "MIT",
  "hooks": {
    "SessionStart": [{
      "matcher": "",
      "hooks": [
        { "type": "command", "command": "npx lna prime" },
        { "type": "command", "command": "npx lna load-session" }
      ]
    }],
    "PreCompact": [{
      "matcher": "",
      "hooks": [
        { "type": "command", "command": "npx lna prime" }
      ]
    }]
  }
}
```

## Implementation
1. Create plugin structure in package (included in npm publish)
2. Update `lna init` to copy plugin to project's .claude-plugin/
3. Or: Use global plugin installation via `lna setup claude`

## Dependencies
- Depends on: learning_agent-8lp (/learn command)
- Depends on: learning_agent-gpv (prime command)

## Reference
Pattern: steveyegge/beads/claude-plugin/

title: Evaluate and clamp ranking boost multiplier
description: PRODUCT DECISION: src/search/ranking.ts:65-69. Multiplicative boost: severity(1.5) * recency(1.2) * confirmation(1.3) = max 2.34x. A lesson with moderate similarity (0.4) but all boosts scores 0.936, outranking an excellent match (0.9) with no boosts. Spec defines this formula but extreme cases may produce surprising results. Evaluate: Should we clamp max combined boost (e.g. min(combined, 1.8))? Or use additive approach: vectorSimilarity * (1 + severityDelta + recencyDelta + confirmationDelta)? Run tests with real lesson data to decide.

title: Epic: v0.2.4 - Field Testing Fixes
description:
# Epic: v0.2.4 - Hybrid Memory System Redesign

## Vision

Redesign the lesson system to combine the best of **Beads** (trusted hook injection) and **Moltbot** (tool-based memory lookup). The goal is to make Claude **fluent and proactive** with lessons, not just a passive recipient of injected context.

## Problem Statement

Field testing v0.2.3 revealed a core issue: **Claude doesn't trust or effectively use auto-injected lessons** (issue lkij). The current "push" model (inject lessons at session start and plan time) doesn't align with how Claude naturally works.

## Solution: Hybrid Approach

### 1. Keep Trusted Injection (Beads-style)
- SessionStart + PreCompact hooks inject **usage guidelines** + **top high-severity lessons**
- Uses Beads' trust language patterns ("MUST", "CRITICAL", explicit prohibitions)
- Teaches Claude HOW to use the system, not just dumps lessons

### 2. Add Native MCP Tools (Moltbot-style)
- `lesson_search` tool with "Mandatory recall" language in description
- `lesson_capture` tool for saving lessons when mistakes happen
- Claude sees these as first-class tools, not Bash commands to remember

### 3. Remove Plan-Time Hook
- Old `check-plan` hook is removed
- Replaced by tool-based search that Claude uses when needed
- More natural, less noisy

### 4. Add Commit Reminder
- PreCommit hook reminds Claude to capture lessons learned
- Complements reactive capture (when mistakes happen)

## Architecture

```
learning-agent/
├── src/
│   ├── cli.ts              # Commander.js (lna command)
│   ├── mcp.ts              # MCP server (~100 lines, thin wrapper)
│   ├── commands/
│   │   ├── setup.ts        # One-shot setup (MCP + hooks + init + model)
│   │   ├── prime.ts        # Prime injection (guidelines + top lessons)
│   │   └── remind-capture.ts
│   ├── search/             # Shared search logic (used by CLI + MCP)
│   ├── capture/            # Shared capture logic (used by CLI + MCP)
│   └── storage/            # JSONL + SQLite
```

## MCP Server Exposure

| Name | Type | Description |
|------|------|-------------|
| `lesson_search` | Tool | "Mandatory recall: search lessons BEFORE architectural decisions, after corrections, or when repeating patterns" |
| `lesson_capture` | Tool | "Capture a lesson after: user corrections, test fix cycles, or discovering project-specific knowledge" |
| `lessons://prime` | Resource | Prime context (guidelines + top lessons) for tools that read resources |

## Hook Configuration

| Hook | Command | Purpose |
|------|---------|---------|
| SessionStart | `lna prime` | Inject guidelines + top lessons + CLI reference |
| PreCompact | `lna prime` | Re-inject before compaction (context survival) |
| PreCommit | `lna remind-capture` | Prompt to capture lessons before committing |

## One-Shot Setup (`lna setup`)

Single command configures everything:
1. Register MCP server in `.claude/settings.local.json`
2. Configure all hooks in `.claude/settings.local.json`
3. Initialize `.claude/lessons/index.jsonl`
4. Download embedding model to `~/.cache/learning-agent/`

## Prime Output (What Claude Sees)

```markdown
# Learning Agent Active

> Context Recovery: Run \`lna prime\` after compaction

## Mandatory Recall
You MUST use \`lesson_search\` BEFORE:
- Architectural decisions or complex planning
- Patterns you've implemented before in this repo
- After corrections ("actually...", "wrong", "use X instead")

## Capture Lessons
Use \`lesson_capture\` AFTER:
- User corrects you
- Test fail → fix → pass cycles
- You discover project-specific knowledge

## Commands (for manual use)
- \`lna search "query"\` - Find relevant lessons
- \`lna learn "insight"\` - Save new lesson
- \`lna list\` - Show recent lessons

## High-Severity Lessons
[Top 3-5 lessons auto-loaded here]
```

## Migration

- **Clean break**: New `lna setup` replaces old hooks
- **Lessons preserved**: Existing index.jsonl remains compatible
- **Old hooks removed**: `load-session`, `check-plan` replaced

## Research Sources

- **Beads** (github.com/steveyegge/beads): Hook injection patterns, trust language, CLI documentation format
- **Moltbot/Openclaw** (github.com/openclaw/openclaw): Tool-based memory, mandatory recall language, two-step retrieval

## Success Criteria

1. Claude proactively uses `lesson_search` when making complex decisions
2. Claude captures lessons when corrected without being asked
3. Setup is one command, works out of the box
4. Context cost stays under 2K tokens for prime injection

title: Integration test: fresh install in clean repo
description:
## Overview

Test the complete user journey in a fresh repository to catch any integration issues.

## Test Environment Setup

```bash
# Create test directory
mkdir /tmp/learning-agent-test
cd /tmp/learning-agent-test

# Initialize git repo
git init
git config user.email "test@test.com"
git config user.name "Test User"

# Create minimal package.json
echo '{"name": "test-project", "version": "1.0.0"}' > package.json
```

## Test Scenarios

### 1. Fresh Install
```bash
# Install from local tarball (or npm after publish)
npm install /path/to/learning-agent-0.2.0.tgz

# Verify CLI works
npx learning-agent --version
npx learning-agent --help
```

### 2. Init Command
```bash
npx learning-agent init

# Verify:
# - .claude/lessons/index.jsonl exists
# - AGENTS.md created with Learning Agent section
# - .git/hooks/pre-commit exists and is executable
```

### 3. Setup Claude (when implemented)
```bash
npx learning-agent setup claude --dry-run

# Verify shows correct settings path
```

### 4. Lesson Capture
```bash
npx learning-agent learn "Test lesson" --trigger "Testing" --yes

# Verify:
npx learning-agent list  # Shows lesson
npx learning-agent search "test"  # Finds lesson
```

### 5. Session Commands
```bash
npx learning-agent load-session
npx learning-agent load-session --json
```

### 6. Pre-commit Hook
```bash
# Make a commit
echo "test" > test.txt
git add test.txt
git commit -m "Test commit"

# Verify hook output appeared
```

## Checklist

- [ ] Fresh install works
- [ ] All CLI commands execute
- [ ] Init creates expected files
- [ ] Lesson capture and retrieval works
- [ ] Pre-commit hook fires
- [ ] No error messages or warnings
- [ ] JSON output is valid JSON

## Cleanup

```bash
rm -rf /tmp/learning-agent-test
```
close_reason: Integration test passed: init, learn, list, search, load-session, stats, show, pre-commit hook all work

title: Implement agent-compliant linter rules for the codebase
description:
## Context

Research from three sources (Anthropic C compiler paper, OpenAI Harness Engineering paper, 
Agentic AI Codebase Guide — all in docs/research/) converges on one key finding:

> "Documentation alone doesn't keep a fully agent-generated codebase coherent." 
> — OpenAI Harness Engineering (Feb 2026)

Our codebase has comprehensive documentation (CLAUDE.md, docs/standards/, docs/verification/) 
that describes ~58 rules/constraints, but **almost nothing is mechanically enforced**. 
The current ESLint config (eslint.config.js) has only 3 basic rules (no-var, prefer-const, 
no-else-return). The custom rules engine (src/rules/) exists but .claude/rules.json is empty.

This is the exact gap OpenAI warns about — rules that exist only as documentation drift and 
become invisible to agents. Agents need **mechanical feedback loops**, not documentation they 
might not read.

## What to implement

### Phase 1: ESLint foundation (existing plugin rules)

Enhance eslint.config.js with rules from existing plugins:

**Security (Inviolable)**:
- `@typescript-eslint/no-explicit-any` — ban any types
- `@typescript-eslint/explicit-module-boundary-types` — types on all exports
- Custom rule or no-restricted-syntax: no template literals in .prepare()/.run()/.exec()/.all()

**Code quality (Inviolable/Strong Default)**:
- `max-lines-per-function: ["warn", { max: 50, skipBlankLines: true, skipComments: true }]`
- `max-lines: ["warn", { max: 300, skipBlankLines: true, skipComments: true }]`
- `max-depth: ["error", 3]` — enforce early returns over deep nesting
- `@typescript-eslint/no-magic-numbers` — with ignore list [0, 1, -1]

**Test quality**:
- `eslint-plugin-vitest/no-focused-tests` — no .only in tests
- `eslint-plugin-vitest/no-disabled-tests` — no .skip in tests

**Import hygiene**:
- `eslint-plugin-import/no-cycle` — no circular imports
- `eslint-plugin-import/no-commonjs` — enforce ESM

### Phase 2: Custom ESLint rules (agent-compliant)

Create `tools/eslint-rules/` with custom rules following the OpenAI pattern — 
**error messages must include remediation instructions** for agents:

1. **no-sql-interpolation**: Detect template literals passed to SQLite methods.
   Error: "SQL injection risk: Use parameterized query with ? placeholders instead of 
   template literal. See: docs/standards/typescript-best-practices.md#database-sqlite"

2. **no-mock-module-under-test**: In test files, detect vi.mock() where the mocked 
   path matches the test file's corresponding source file.
   Error: "Do not mock the module being tested — test real behavior. Mock only 
   external dependencies. See: docs/standards/anti-patterns.md"

3. **no-utils-helpers-dirs**: Flag files in directories named utils/, helpers/, shared/, 
   common/, or misc/.
   Error: "Utils/helpers directories indicate unclear responsibility. Move this function 
   to the module that owns its concern. See: docs/standards/code-organization.md"

4. **enforce-barrel-exports**: Verify cross-module imports go through index.ts, 
   not internal files.
   Error: "Import from the module's public API (index.ts), not internal files. 
   See: docs/standards/code-organization.md#module-design"

### Phase 3: Structural tests

Add Vitest-based structural tests in `src/__structural__/` that verify project invariants:

- Every src/ subdirectory with .ts files has an index.ts
- index.ts files only contain re-exports (no implementation logic)
- No files/dirs named utils, helpers, shared, common, misc
- .claude/.cache/ is in .gitignore
- package.json has "type": "module"
- No .skip or .only in committed test files (belt-and-suspenders with ESLint)

## Design principles (from the research)

1. **Error messages are agent instructions**: Every lint error should tell the agent 
   exactly what to do and where to find more context. The error IS the remediation.
2. **Mechanical > documentary**: Rules in code beat rules in docs. Promote docs → code 
   whenever possible.
3. **Progressive enforcement**: Start with warnings, escalate to errors once the 
   codebase is clean.
4. **Context window friendly**: Lint output should be concise and grep-friendly 
   (Anthropic paper insight).

## Dependencies

- pnpm add -D eslint-plugin-vitest eslint-plugin-import (Phase 1)
- No external deps for custom rules (Phase 2)
- No external deps for structural tests (Phase 3)

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] eslint.config.js enforces all Phase 1 rules
- [ ] pnpm lint passes with zero violations (fix existing code if needed)
- [ ] Custom rules in tools/eslint-rules/ with agent-targeted error messages
- [ ] Structural tests pass in pnpm test
- [ ] Existing test suite still passes (no regressions)
close_reason: Closed

title: P3: Fix overstated e2e claims in test comments
description: compound-cycle.test.ts:4 and lfg-phase.test.ts:5 claim 'full cycle e2e' but tests are structural template validation + memory pipeline, not true orchestrated full-cycle execution. Update comments to accurately reflect test scope.
close_reason: Updated compound-cycle.test.ts header to accurately describe test scope as MCP pipeline validation, not full e2e

title: Harden custom ESLint rules: barrel-exports bypass, path portability, mock detection
description:
## Context
Implementation review found 3 bugs in custom ESLint rules (findings 3, 4, 5).

## Finding 3 (High): enforce-barrel-exports bypass via extensionless imports
**File**: tools/eslint-rules/rules/enforce-barrel-exports.js:42
**Bug**: `isBarrelImport()` treats any import whose last segment has no dot as a barrel import. Deep extensionless imports like `../memory/storage/sqlite/search` slip through.
**Fix**: Count path segments after the target module root. Only allow single-segment extensionless imports (e.g. `../memory`). Multi-segment extensionless paths (e.g. `../memory/storage/sqlite/search`) must be flagged.

## Finding 4 (Medium): Path portability (Windows)
**Files**: enforce-barrel-exports.js:9 (`/src/` substring), no-utils-helpers-dirs.js:27 (`.split('/')`)
**Bug**: Hardcoded forward-slash path operations fail on Windows.
**Fix**: Use `path.normalize()`, `path.sep`, and `path.relative()` before segment checks in all 3 path-sensitive rules.

## Finding 5 (Medium): no-mock-module-under-test false positives
**File**: tools/eslint-rules/rules/no-mock-module-under-test.js:55-57
**Bug**: Compares only basenames. `vi.mock('../other-dir/search')` in `search.test.ts` falsely matches because basenames collide.
**Fix**: Resolve the mocked relative path against the test file directory and compare normalized absolute candidate paths.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] enforce-barrel-exports correctly flags extensionless deep imports
- [ ] All 3 path-sensitive rules use path.normalize/path.sep
- [ ] no-mock-module-under-test resolves full paths, not just basenames
- [ ] Updated RuleTester tests cover all new edge cases
- [ ] Existing 58 rule tests still pass
close_reason: Closed

title: REFACTOR: Split sqlite.ts (613 lines → ~200-250 each)
description:
## Problem

`src/storage/sqlite.ts` is 613 lines, exceeding the 300-line standard by 313 lines.

This violates:
- `.claude/CLAUDE.md`: 'Files < 300 lines'

## Current Structure

The file contains several distinct concerns:
1. **Schema definition** (~100 lines of SQL)
2. **Connection management** - openDb, closeDb (~80 lines)
3. **Embedding cache** - getCachedEmbedding, setCachedEmbedding, collectCachedEmbeddings (~100 lines)
4. **Index operations** - rebuildIndex, syncIfNeeded (~150 lines)
5. **Search/Query** - searchKeyword, rowToLesson (~100 lines)
6. **Metadata** - getLastSyncMtime, setLastSyncMtime (~50 lines)

## Proposed Split

```
src/storage/sqlite/
├── index.ts           # Re-exports all public functions (30 lines)
├── schema.ts          # SCHEMA_SQL constant + createSchema (~120 lines)
├── connection.ts      # openDb, closeDb, db singleton (~100 lines)
├── cache.ts           # Embedding cache operations (~120 lines)
├── sync.ts            # rebuildIndex, syncIfNeeded (~180 lines)
└── search.ts          # searchKeyword, rowToLesson (~100 lines)
```

## Implementation Steps

### 1. Create directory structure
```bash
mkdir -p src/storage/sqlite
```

### 2. Create `schema.ts`
Extract:
- `SCHEMA_SQL` constant (the entire CREATE TABLE block)
- `createSchema(db)` function
- Any schema-related constants

### 3. Create `connection.ts`
Extract:
- `let db: DatabaseType | null = null` singleton
- `let dbIsInMemory = false` flag
- `openDb(repoRoot, options)` function
- `closeDb()` function
- `DB_PATH` constant

### 4. Create `cache.ts`
Extract:
- `contentHash(text)` function
- `getCachedEmbedding(lessonId)` function
- `setCachedEmbedding(lessonId, embedding, contentHash)` function
- `collectCachedEmbeddings(repoRoot)` function

### 5. Create `sync.ts`
Extract:
- `rebuildIndex(repoRoot)` function
- `syncIfNeeded(repoRoot)` function
- `getLastSyncMtime()` function
- `setLastSyncMtime(mtime)` function

### 6. Create `search.ts`
Extract:
- `searchKeyword(repoRoot, query, limit)` function
- `rowToLesson(row)` function (internal helper)
- `LessonRow` interface

### 7. Create `index.ts`
```typescript
// Re-export public API only
export { DB_PATH, openDb, closeDb } from './connection.js';
export { contentHash, getCachedEmbedding, setCachedEmbedding, collectCachedEmbeddings } from './cache.js';
export { rebuildIndex, syncIfNeeded, getLastSyncMtime, setLastSyncMtime } from './sync.js';
export { searchKeyword } from './search.js';
// Note: rowToLesson is internal, not exported
```

### 8. Update parent index
- `src/storage/index.ts` should import from `./sqlite/index.js`

## Dependency Order

Files should be created in this order due to dependencies:
1. `schema.ts` (no deps)
2. `connection.ts` (imports schema)
3. `cache.ts` (imports connection)
4. `search.ts` (imports connection)
5. `sync.ts` (imports connection, schema, search)
6. `index.ts` (imports all)

## Verification

```bash
pnpm build   # Must succeed
pnpm test    # All tests must pass
pnpm lint    # No new violations

# Verify each new file is under 300 lines
wc -l src/storage/sqlite/*.ts
```

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `sqlite.ts` deleted
- [ ] 6 new files created in `src/storage/sqlite/`
- [ ] Each file < 300 lines
- [ ] All storage operations still work identically
- [ ] All tests pass
- [ ] No functionality changes
close_reason: Split sqlite.ts (644 lines) into 8 focused modules under src/storage/sqlite/, all under 300 lines. Backward-compatible shim maintained at sqlite.ts.

title: Pre-commit hook doesn't recall relevant lessons
description:
**Clarification:** This is about whether the pre-commit hook should dynamically query lessons based on staged files, similar to how session-start loads high-severity lessons.

**Current behavior:**
Pre-commit hook outputs a static reminder message (PRE_COMMIT_MESSAGE constant).

**Proposed behavior:**
Hook could:
1. Get list of staged files
2. Query lessons related to those files/topics
3. Inject relevant lessons into Claude's context

**User expectation:**
```
> Now, try to commit something, and tell me if you received some lessons there
⏺ No, I did not receive any lessons or recalls during the commit process.
```

**Considerations:**
- Would slow down every commit (embedding lookup)
- May be noisy if many lessons match
- Pre-commit hook can't easily inject into Claude context (no stdin)
- Better approach might be: Claude manually calls 'check-plan' with commit message

**Decision needed:** Is this feature worth implementing, or is the current static reminder sufficient?

**Alternative:** Improve the static reminder to suggest running 'check-plan' manually
notes:
## Tests to Implement (if feature approved)

### Unit Tests
- [ ] Hook extracts staged file list
- [ ] Hook calls retrieveForPlan with file context
- [ ] Hook formats lessons for output

### Integration Tests
- [ ] Commit with staged Python files -> Python-related lessons shown
- [ ] Commit with no relevant lessons -> graceful message
- [ ] Hook output is captured by Claude

### Edge Cases
- [ ] Many files staged -> reasonable subset used for query
- [ ] No model available -> skip lesson query, show static message
- [ ] Query timeout -> don't block commit

### Performance Tests
- [ ] Hook completes in <500ms for typical commit
- [ ] Large repos don't cause slowdown
close_reason: Not needed - just want static reminder to create lessons

title: FEATURE: Age-based temporal validity (Beads-style)
description:
## Problem
Lessons can become stale over time. The current 30-day recency boost is too crude - it penalizes old lessons but doesn't flag them for review.

## Research Findings (from Beads)
Beads uses a two-tier compaction strategy:
- Tier 1 (30+ days): Basic summarization
- Tier 2 (90+ days): Aggressive archival
- Tracks: compaction_level, compacted_at, original_size
- Dependency-aware: won't compact if dependents still open

## Proposed Solution
1. Add schema fields: `compactionLevel`, `compactedAt`, `lastRetrieved`
2. In `load-session` and `check-plan`, flag lessons >90 days old with warning
3. In `lna stats`, show count of lessons by age bracket
4. Optional: AI summarization for Tier 1 (defer to future version)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Schema includes `compactionLevel` (0=active, 1=flagged, 2=archived)
- [ ] `load-session` shows '⚠️ Old lesson (90+ days)' for flagged lessons
- [ ] `stats` command shows age distribution
- [ ] Tests cover age calculation and flagging logic
close_reason: Closed

title: Eliminate double model initialization in search path
description: isModelUsable() in model.ts loads the full 278MB EmbeddingGemma model, creates an embedding context, probes it, then DISPOSES everything. Immediately after, embedText() calls getEmbedding() which loads the model AGAIN via the singleton. This wastes 800-2000ms per CLI invocation. Fix: use isModelAvailable() (file-check only) for preflight, let the singleton in getEmbedding() handle the actual initialization, and catch/surface errors at the call site in retrieval.ts. The cachedUsability pattern can remain as a fast-path after first success. Key files: src/memory/embeddings/model.ts, src/commands/retrieval.ts, src/memory/search/vector.ts.

title: Embedding Memory Pressure Remediation
description: Meta-epic: Decompose and fix memory pressure from native C++ addons (node-llama-cpp, better-sqlite3) in test suite and CLI. 4 independent reviews analyzed. 6 epics spanning quick fixes to partial Go migration. See docs/embedding_memory_pressure/ for full analysis. Processing order: Epic 1 → [Epic 2 + Epic 4 parallel] → Epic 3 (after 2) → Epic 5 (after 4, gate B) → Epic 6 (after 5). Gates: A (after E1: is Tier 2 needed?), B (after E4: Go or lighter runtime?), C (after E3: is Tier 3 needed?).

title: FIX: init command missing Claude hooks integration (40 failing tests)
description:
## Problem

Tests in `src/cli/init.test.ts` (lines 604-935) expect the `init` command to:
1. Have `--skip-claude` / `--skip-claude-hooks` CLI option
2. Include `claudeHooks: boolean` field in JSON output
3. Integrate with `.claude/settings.json` to install SessionStart hooks

But the actual `init` command (setup.ts lines 719-726) does NOT implement any of these features.

## Impact

- 40 tests failing in full test suite
- 24 tests failing in init.test.ts alone
- Tests pass only when filtered to skip Claude hooks tests

## Root Cause

Tests were written for a feature that was never implemented. The `setup claude` command handles settings.json hooks separately, but `init` was expected to call this functionality as part of the initialization flow.

## Solution Options

### Option A: Implement the feature (recommended)
Make `init` call the same logic as `setup claude` to install hooks automatically:
1. Add `--skip-claude` option to init command
2. Call Claude hooks installation from init
3. Add `claudeHooks` field to JSON output
4. Handle errors gracefully (don't fail init if hooks fail)

### Option B: Remove the tests
If the feature was deprioritized, remove the tests that expect it:
- Delete tests in 'Claude hooks integration (v0.2.1)' describe block
- Update any references to expected behavior

## Related

- `setup claude` command already implements settings.json hook management
- This is NOT related to SQLite graceful degradation work (beads-2f0)
- Tests are pre-existing failures on main branch
close_reason: Fixed: init now integrates Claude hooks, all 984 tests pass

title: Improve Test DX: Execute integration tests against source
description:
Current integration tests in `src/cli.test.ts` rely on the built artifact (`dist/cli.js`).

**Problems:**
- **False Positives/Negatives**: Tests fail or pass based on the *last build*, not the *current code*.
- **Developer Friction**: Requires running `pnpm build` before every test run, slowing down the TDD loop.
- **Confusion**: Debugging is harder as stack traces point to `dist` files.

**Proposal:**
1.  Update test helpers (`runCli`, etc.) to execute `src/cli.ts` directly using `tsx` or `ts-node`.
2.  Ensure `vitest` environment handles the execution correctly.
3.  Remove strict dependency on `dist/` for tests (except perhaps for a final "packaging" test).

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- `pnpm test` passes without a prior `pnpm build`.
- Tests reflect changes in `src/` immediately.
close_reason: Tests now run against src/cli.ts using tsx - no build required

title: Fix: ca setup does not install git pre-commit hook
description:
PROBLEM: ca setup (in src/setup/all.ts runSetup()) does not call installPreCommitHook(). Only ca init installs the git pre-commit hook. Users who use ca setup as their primary setup path miss the lesson capture reminder on commits.

EVIDENCE: src/setup/all.ts — runSetup() calls configureClaudeSettings(), createPluginManifest(), installAgentTemplates(), installWorkflowCommands(), installPhaseSkills(), installAgentRoleSkills(). No call to installPreCommitHook().

IMPACT: Low — the pre-commit hook is a soft reminder only (prints a message, never blocks). But it's a gap in the setup path.

FIX: Add installPreCommitHook(repoRoot) call to runSetup() in all.ts, gated behind the same skip-hooks flag pattern used in ca init.

title: Fix: ca init should install all 5 Claude Code hooks
description:
PROBLEM: ca init calls addCompoundAgentHook() (singular) which only installs SessionStart into settings.json. The other 4 hooks (PreCompact, UserPromptSubmit, PostToolUseFailure, PostToolUse) are defined in templates.ts and declared in plugin.json but never written to settings.json by ca init.

EVIDENCE: src/setup/claude-helpers.ts:80-89 (addCompoundAgentHook) vs lines 95-143 (addAllCompoundAgentHooks). src/setup/init.ts:83 calls installClaudeHooksForInit() which calls the singular version.

IMPACT: Most users run ca init as their entry point. They only get SessionStart/prime. The UserPromptSubmit correction/planning reminders never fire. The PostToolUse hooks never fire.

FIX: Change installClaudeHooksForInit() to call addAllCompoundAgentHooks() instead of addCompoundAgentHook(). Update the existing-hook detection to handle all 5 types. Update tests accordingly.

title: Task 7: Background embedding after init/setup
description:
Spawn a detached background process to embed knowledge chunks after ca init/ca setup completes, with user feedback and proper lifecycle management.

## Context
After ca init or ca setup finishes (model downloaded, docs indexed), embedding 400 chunks takes 25-70s. Instead of blocking, spawn a background process that embeds while the user starts working. Print a non-blocking message: 'Embedding in progress (background). You can start working.' and write status to embed-status.json for later feedback.

## Files
- NEW: src/memory/knowledge/embed-background.ts
- NEW: src/memory/knowledge/embed-background.test.ts
- MODIFY: src/setup/all.ts (spawn after setup)
- MODIFY: src/setup/init.ts (spawn after init)

## Implementation

### embed-background.ts

#### Types:
interface SpawnEmbedResult {
  spawned: boolean;
  reason?: string;  // why not spawned (lock held, no chunks, model unavailable)
  pid?: number;
}

#### spawnBackgroundEmbed(repoRoot: string): SpawnEmbedResult
1. Check isEmbedLocked(repoRoot) — if locked, return { spawned: false, reason: 'Embedding already in progress' }
2. Check isModelAvailable() — if not, return { spawned: false, reason: 'Model not available' }
3. Check getUnembeddedChunkCount(repoRoot) — if 0, return { spawned: false, reason: 'All chunks already embedded' }
4. Spawn: child_process.spawn('node', [cliPath, 'embed-worker', repoRoot], { detached: true, stdio: 'ignore' })
5. child.unref()
6. Return { spawned: true, pid: child.pid }

Note: cliPath = resolve the dist/cli.js path relative to import.meta.url

#### runBackgroundEmbed(repoRoot: string): Promise<void>
Worker entry point, called by the embed-worker CLI command:
1. const lock = acquireEmbedLock(repoRoot) — if !acquired, exit quietly
2. writeEmbedStatus(repoRoot, { state: 'running', startedAt: new Date().toISOString() })
3. try:
   a. const count = getUnembeddedChunkCount(repoRoot)
   b. const result = await embedChunks(repoRoot, { onlyMissing: true })
   c. writeEmbedStatus(repoRoot, { state: 'completed', chunksEmbedded: result.chunksEmbedded, completedAt: new Date().toISOString(), durationMs: result.durationMs })
4. catch (err):
   writeEmbedStatus(repoRoot, { state: 'failed', error: err.message })
5. finally:
   unloadEmbedding()
   closeKnowledgeDb()
   lock.release()

### Hidden CLI subcommand (embed-worker)
Register in src/commands/knowledge-index.ts or src/cli.ts:
program
  .command('embed-worker <repoRoot>')
  .description(false)  // hidden command
  .action(async (repoRoot: string) => {
    await runBackgroundEmbed(repoRoot);
  });

### Changes to src/setup/all.ts (runSetup)
After model download step (around line ~193) and after calling indexDocs if docs/ exists:
1. const docsPath = join(repoRoot, 'docs');
2. if (existsSync(docsPath)):
   a. await indexDocs(repoRoot) — text-only index (fast, ~1-2s)
   b. const spawnResult = spawnBackgroundEmbed(repoRoot)
   c. if (spawnResult.spawned):
      out.info('Embedding in progress (background). You can start working.')
      out.info('Run `npx ca knowledge <query>` to search docs.')
   d. else:
      out.info('Embedding: ' + spawnResult.reason)

### Changes to src/setup/init.ts (initAction)
Same pattern as all.ts, after model download step:
1. Check if docs/ exists
2. If yes: indexDocs + spawnBackgroundEmbed with same messaging

## Key functions to reuse (DO NOT reimplement)
- acquireEmbedLock / isEmbedLocked from ./embed-lock.ts (Task 1)
- writeEmbedStatus / readEmbedStatus from ./embed-status.ts (Task 2)
- embedChunks / getUnembeddedChunkCount from ./embed-chunks.ts (Task 3)
- isModelAvailable from src/memory/embeddings/model.ts
- unloadEmbedding from src/memory/embeddings/nomic.ts
- closeKnowledgeDb from src/memory/storage/sqlite-knowledge/connection.ts
- indexDocs from src/memory/knowledge/indexing.ts

## Tests (TDD - write first)

### Unit tests (mock child_process.spawn):
1. spawnBackgroundEmbed returns { spawned: false } when lock held
2. spawnBackgroundEmbed returns { spawned: false } when model unavailable
3. spawnBackgroundEmbed returns { spawned: false } when no unembedded chunks
4. spawnBackgroundEmbed calls spawn with correct args when preconditions met

### Integration tests (mock embedChunks):
5. runBackgroundEmbed acquires lock, writes running status, calls embedChunks, writes completed status, releases lock
6. runBackgroundEmbed writes failed status on embedChunks error
7. Lock is released even on error (verify with isEmbedLocked)
8. Status file reflects final state after run

## Dependencies
Depends on Tasks 1 (lock), 2 (status), 3 (embedChunks), 5 (CLI command), 6 (init model download).
close_reason: Closed

title: check-plan should hard-fail on embedding errors
description:
## Problem
`check-plan` currently catches all errors and prints "No relevant lessons", which violates the spec (embeddings unavailable must hard-fail) and hides real failures.

## Required Behavior
- If embeddings/model are unavailable or resolve fails, `check-plan` must exit non-zero and show a clear error instructing `learning-agent download-model`.
- If there are zero relevant lessons, still exit 0 and print the normal "No relevant lessons" message.
- JSON mode should return a structured error payload and exit non-zero on embedding failure.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Embedding/model failure results in non-zero exit and actionable error message.
- [ ] No blanket catch that masks errors.
- [ ] Empty results still exit 0 with friendly message.
- [ ] JSON output includes `{ error: "..." }` on failure (and exit non-zero).

## Tests
1. When embeddings unavailable, `check-plan` exits non-zero and includes "download-model" in output.
2. When embeddings available, `check-plan` works as before.
3. JSON mode returns error payload on failure.

**Note**: Make the tests conditional on `isModelAvailable()` to avoid CI failures.

## Files
- src/cli.ts (check-plan command)
- src/cli.test.ts (conditional test expectations)
close_reason: check-plan now hard-fails on embedding errors with actionable download-model message

## Core Rules

title: Task 3: Core embedChunks() function
description:
Create the core function that embeds all unembedded chunks in the knowledge database.

## Context
This is the workhorse function. It reads chunks from knowledge.sqlite that have no embedding, calls embedText() for each, and stores the result via setCachedChunkEmbedding(). It's called by indexDocs() (eager), by the background worker, and by the CLI --embed flag. Separated from indexing so it can be called independently.

## Files
- NEW: src/memory/knowledge/embed-chunks.ts
- NEW: src/memory/knowledge/embed-chunks.test.ts

## Implementation

### Functions
- getUnembeddedChunkCount(repoRoot: string): number
  - SQL: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM chunks WHERE embedding IS NULL
  - Uses openKnowledgeDb(repoRoot)

- embedChunks(repoRoot: string, options?: EmbedChunksOptions): Promise<EmbedChunksResult>
  - Query: SELECT id, text, content_hash FROM chunks WHERE embedding IS NULL
  - For each chunk: call embedText(chunk.text) -> number[768]
  - Call setCachedChunkEmbedding(repoRoot, chunk.id, new Float32Array(vector), chunk.content_hash)
  - Track chunksEmbedded count
  - Caller manages model lifecycle (getEmbedding/unloadEmbedding)

### Types
interface EmbedChunksOptions {
  onlyMissing?: boolean;  // default true — only embed chunks with no embedding
}

interface EmbedChunksResult {
  chunksEmbedded: number;
  chunksSkipped: number;
  durationMs: number;
}

## Key functions to reuse (DO NOT reimplement)
- embedText(text) from src/memory/embeddings/nomic.ts — returns number[768]
- setCachedChunkEmbedding(repoRoot, id, embedding, hash) from src/memory/storage/sqlite-knowledge/cache.ts
- openKnowledgeDb(repoRoot) from src/memory/storage/sqlite-knowledge/connection.ts

## Tests (TDD - write first)

### Unit tests (no model needed):
1. getUnembeddedChunkCount returns 0 for empty DB
2. getUnembeddedChunkCount returns correct count after upserting chunks without embeddings
3. getUnembeddedChunkCount returns 0 when all chunks have embeddings (upsert with embeddings map)

### Embedding tests (use it.skipIf(skipEmbedding)):
4. embedChunks embeds all unembedded chunks and returns correct stats
5. After embedChunks, getUnembeddedChunkCount returns 0
6. embedChunks with onlyMissing:true skips already-embedded chunks
7. Verify stored embedding is valid Float32Array of 768 dimensions

### Test setup pattern (from search.test.ts):
- openKnowledgeDb(repoRoot, { inMemory: true }) in beforeEach
- upsertChunks(repoRoot, chunks) to seed data
- closeKnowledgeDb() in afterEach
- unloadEmbedding() in afterAll for embedding tests
- shouldSkipEmbeddingTests() + isModelAvailable() + isModelUsable() for skip logic
- makeChunk() helper: { id, filePath, startLine, endLine, contentHash: chunkContentHash(text), text, updatedAt }

## Dependencies
Depends on Task 1 (lock) and Task 2 (status) being available as imports, but does NOT directly call them — those are used by the background worker (Task 7) which wraps embedChunks().

title: E5: Go Embedding Implementation
description:
## Scope
Full implementation of Go embedding binary + TypeScript integration, with fallback to node-llama-cpp.

## Sub-phases
### 5a — Go Binary
- Complete Go binary with batch embedding support, error handling
- stdin/stdout JSON protocol: text in → Float32Array JSON out
- Exit codes: 0=success, 1=model not found, 2=runtime error
- Own Go test suite

### 5b — TypeScript Integration
- TypeScript subprocess wrapper (embedTextViaGo, embedTextsViaGo)
- Fallback to node-llama-cpp when Go binary absent
- Provider selection layer in nomic.ts or new provider.ts
- Migration of callers (transparent — same embedText/embedTexts API)
- Cross-platform build/distribution pipeline
- Cross-provider property tests (vector compatibility)

## Out of Scope
Broader CLI migration, SQLite migration, full language rewrite

## EARS Requirements
E4, E5, S4, O2

## Key Interface Contracts
- Binary discovery: PATH, node_modules/.bin/, or env var
- Lifecycle change: Go binary is stateless per invocation — unloadEmbedding() becomes no-op
- Cache key must include provider version; invalidate all caches on provider change
- model-info.ts (from Epic 2) provides canonical model path

## Assumptions
- Epic 4 spike recommends Go (Gate B passed)
- Epic 2 model-info.ts available for model path discovery
close_reason: Cancelled per E4 Gate B decision: Go CGo bindings failed to build, Transformers.js achieves 95% memory reduction (22.7MB vs 431MB) without Go dependency. New epic will implement Transformers.js migration instead.

title: Fix embedding singleton race condition
description: CRITICAL BUG: src/embeddings/nomic.ts:54-66. Two concurrent getEmbedding() calls before init completes both pass the null guard and load the model twice. Results: ~300MB RAM (doubled), memory leak (first context overwritten, never disposed), potential native addon crash. In MCP context, concurrent lesson_search calls during planning trigger this. Fix: Add a pending promise guard (let pendingInit: Promise | null = null). When getEmbedding() is called, if pendingInit already set, return it. Otherwise set it and proceed. Clear on failure. ~5 lines change.

title: Fix git hook message: Distinguish 'Already installed' from 'Not a git repo'
description:
## Overview
The `npx lna init` command shows ambiguous message 'Git hooks: Already installed or not a git repo' which is confusing.

## Current Behavior
```
(base) MacBookPro:test_repo Nathan$ npx lna init
[ok] Learning agent initialized
  ...
  Git hooks: Already installed or not a git repo
```

The user IS in a git repo, but can't tell if the hook was installed or skipped.

## Root Cause
`installPreCommitHook()` returns `false` for BOTH cases:
1. Hook already has our marker (already installed)
2. Not a git repo (no .git directory)

The init command can't distinguish between these cases.

## Fix
Change `installPreCommitHook()` return type from `boolean` to a discriminated union:

```typescript
type HookInstallResult = 
  | { status: 'installed' }
  | { status: 'already_installed' }
  | { status: 'not_git_repo' }
  | { status: 'appended' };  // For existing hooks we appended to
```

Then update init.ts to show appropriate messages:
- 'Git hooks: Installed'
- 'Git hooks: Already installed'
- 'Git hooks: Skipped (not a git repository)'
- 'Git hooks: Appended to existing pre-commit hook'

## Files to Modify
- src/commands/setup/hooks.ts - Change return type and logic
- src/commands/setup/init.ts - Update message display
- src/commands/setup.test.ts - Update tests for new return type

## Testing
- Test: Returns 'installed' for fresh install
- Test: Returns 'already_installed' when marker present
- Test: Returns 'not_git_repo' when no .git directory
- Test: Returns 'appended' when existing hook without marker
close_reason: Closed

Fix: extend the background embed infrastructure (or add a parallel invocation) to pre-embed all lessons after ca init, ca rebuild, or when syncIfNeeded detects a stale index. This is a one-time cost that eliminates all cache misses from the search hot path. Key files: src/memory/knowledge/embed-background.ts, src/memory/search/vector.ts, src/setup/init.ts.
