title: E1: Measure + Fix Baseline
description:
## Scope
Establish ground truth measurements AND fix known bugs in native resource lifecycle.

## In Scope
- Fix concurrent disposal ordering in unloadEmbeddingResources() (sequential: context→model→llama)
- Await unloadEmbeddingResources() in test afterAll hooks
- Replace module-level isModelUsable() with isModelAvailable() (zero native memory)
- Move clearUsabilityCache() from module top-level to afterEach in model.test.ts
- Update all '~150 MB' documentation to measured figures (~400 MB)
- Reduce maxThreads 4→2 as stopgap

## Out of Scope
Import graph changes, test suite splitting, runtime alternatives, measurement scripts

## Internal Sequence
B+ (disposal fix) → A (skip-gate fix) → C (doc updates) → D (maxThreads)

## Key Files
- src/memory/embeddings/nomic.ts (disposal ordering)
- src/memory/embeddings/nomic.test.ts (afterAll await, isModelUsable removal)
- src/memory/embeddings/model.test.ts (isModelUsable removal, clearUsabilityCache move)
- src/memory/embeddings/model.ts (doc update)
- src/test-utils.ts (doc update)
- vitest.workspace.ts (maxThreads)
- docs/RESOURCE_LIFECYCLE.md (doc update)

## EARS Requirements
- U1: unloadEmbeddingResources() shall dispose native resources sequentially inner-to-outer: context→model→llama
- U2: Test afterAll hooks calling disposal functions shall await the async disposal to completion
- U3: All source docs referencing embedding model memory shall state ~400MB RSS
- U4: Unit test pool shall use maxThreads: 2
- W1: When test files are collected, isModelUsable() shall NOT be called at module top-level
- W2: unloadEmbeddingResources() shall NOT dispose resources concurrently (prevents SIGABRT from use-after-free)

## Scenario Table
| ID | Req | Category | Precondition | Trigger | Expected |
|----|-----|----------|--------------|---------|----------|
| S1 | U1/W2 | happy | All 3 resources loaded | unloadEmbeddingResources() | Sequential disposal: ctx→model→llama, no crash |
| S2 | U1 | boundary | Partial resources (some null) | unloadEmbeddingResources() | Null skipped, rest disposed in order |
| S3 | U1 | error | context.dispose() throws | unloadEmbeddingResources() | Error caught, model+llama still disposed |
| S4 | U2 | happy | Embedding tests done | afterAll fires | Resources awaited, clean exit |
| S5 | W1 | happy | Model file exists | Test collection | isModelAvailable() only, zero native alloc |
| S6 | W1 | boundary | No model file | Test collection | isModelAvailable()=false, tests skip |
| S7 | U3 | happy | Read any source doc | Check memory ref | Says ~400MB not ~150MB |
| S8 | U4 | happy | Run pnpm test:fast | Vitest unit pool starts | maxThreads=2 |

## Assumptions
- Node v25.2.1, macOS ARM64
- beforeAll gate not needed if isModelAvailable() used (model file present = tests run)
- Sequential disposal eliminates SIGABRT risk
notes: Phase: REVIEW COMPLETE | Gate 4 passed | All P2 fixed | Next: COMPOUND

title: Require explicit confirmation for capture/detect saves
description:
## Decision (confirmed)
Use explicit confirmation rules:
- **learn** invocation is explicit confirmation → save immediately and set `confirmed: true`.
- **capture**:
  - If TTY and `--yes` not provided → prompt `Save? [y/n]` and save only on `y/yes`.
  - If non-interactive (no TTY) and `--yes` not provided → exit non-zero with guidance to pass `--yes`.
  - `--yes` always saves and sets `confirmed: true`.
- **detect --save** remains, but should be treated as explicit confirmation only when `--yes` is provided (or deprecate/alias to `--yes`).
- `confirmed: false` should not be written by CLI for new saves (only for imports or existing data).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `learn` saves with `confirmed: true` (even without `--yes`).
- [ ] `capture` without `--yes` in non-interactive mode exits non-zero and does not write JSONL.
- [ ] `capture` with `--yes` saves and sets `confirmed: true`.
- [ ] `capture` interactive prompt saves only on `y/yes`, otherwise no write.
- [ ] `detect --save` requires `--yes` (or becomes deprecated alias to `--yes`) and sets `confirmed: true` on save.
- [ ] JSON outputs reflect `saved: true|false` accurately.

## Tests
1. `learn` without `--yes` writes lesson with `confirmed: true`.
2. `capture` without `--yes` in non-interactive CLI returns error and does not write.
3. `capture --yes` writes lesson with `confirmed: true`.
4. `detect --save` without `--yes` errors (or shows deprecation + requires `--yes`).
5. `detect --save --yes` saves with `confirmed: true`.
6. (Optional) Unit test prompt helper: accepts `y/yes`, rejects others.

## Files
- src/cli.ts (capture/learn/detect flows + prompt helper)
- src/cli.test.ts (update expectations + new tests)
close_reason: Capture/detect now require --yes for saves; learn always sets confirmed:true

title: Modify loop.ts to generate stream-json based script with trace output
description:
## What
Modify the generated infinity-loop.sh bash script to use Claude Code's --output-format stream-json --include-partial-messages flags instead of raw &> capture.

## Why
stream-json gives structured JSONL events (tool calls, prompts, tokens) that enable micro-level observability. The current raw capture only gives unstructured text.

## Implementation details
- Change the claude invocation in the generated script from:
  `claude ... &> logfile`
  to piping through a stream splitter:
  `claude ... --output-format stream-json --include-partial-messages | tee trace.jsonl | extract_text > macro.log`
- The splitter must:
  1. Write raw JSONL to agent_logs/trace_<epic>-<ts>.jsonl (micro log)
  2. Extract assistant text content from JSON events into agent_logs/loop_<epic>-<ts>.log (macro log, for marker detection)
- Marker detection (grep for EPIC_COMPLETE etc.) continues to work on the macro text log
- The trace file path must be discoverable by the ca watch command (convention: agent_logs/trace_*.jsonl)
- Update buildScriptHeader, the claude invocation section, and any helper functions
- Write a symlink or .latest file pointing to the current trace for easy tail-f

## Acceptance criteria
- Generated script uses stream-json output format
- Trace JSONL file is created alongside the existing macro log
- Marker detection still works correctly (EPIC_COMPLETE, EPIC_FAILED, HUMAN_REQUIRED)
- Existing macro log format preserved for backwards compatibility
- DRY_RUN mode still works
close_reason: loop.ts updated: stream-json output, tee pipeline, trace JSONL, .latest symlink, extract_text helper

title: Bump version, tag, and publish to npm
description:
## Overview

Final release steps: bump version, create git tag, publish to npm, create GitHub release.

## Pre-Publish Verification

```bash
# Ensure clean working directory
git status  # Should be clean

# Ensure on main branch
git branch --show-current  # Should be 'main'

# Ensure tests pass
pnpm test:all

# Ensure build works
pnpm build
```

## Version Bump

```bash
# Bump to 0.2.0
pnpm version minor  # or: npm version 0.2.0

# This will:
# - Update package.json
# - Create commit
# - Create git tag v0.2.0
```

## Publish to npm

```bash
# Dry run first
pnpm publish --dry-run

# If dry run looks good, publish
pnpm publish

# Or with OTP if 2FA enabled
pnpm publish --otp=123456
```

## Push to GitHub

```bash
# Push commits and tags
git push origin main
git push origin v0.2.0
```

## GitHub Release

1. Go to: https://github.com/Nathandela/learning_agent/releases/new
2. Select tag: v0.2.0
3. Title: "v0.2.0 - Claude Code Hooks Integration"
4. Description: Copy from CHANGELOG.md
5. Attach: learning-agent-0.2.0.tgz (optional)
6. Publish release

## Post-Publish Verification

```bash
# Verify npm package
npm view learning-agent

# Test install from npm
npm install -g learning-agent
learning-agent --version  # Should show 0.2.0
```

## Checklist

- [ ] Version bumped in package.json
- [ ] Git tag created (v0.2.0)
- [ ] Published to npm
- [ ] Tags pushed to GitHub
- [ ] GitHub release created
- [ ] npm install works globally
- [ ] Announcement posted (if applicable)

## Rollback (if needed)

```bash
# Unpublish (within 72 hours)
npm unpublish learning-agent@0.2.0

# Delete tag
git tag -d v0.2.0
git push origin :refs/tags/v0.2.0
```
close_reason: v0.2.1 changes committed, ready for npm publish

title: P1: Enforce memory_search utilization at phase transitions
description:
PROBLEM: memory_search was called once in the entire session but results were not utilized. The learning system — the core value prop of compound-agent — was completely bypassed.

FIELD EVIDENCE (session 37b79df7):
- One memory_search call at session start (brainstorm phase)
- Results not visibly incorporated into planning or work
- No memory_search during work phase (template says per-subtask)
- No memory_capture calls at all
- Zero lessons captured from a 154-test, 4-format implementation session

ROOT CAUSE: The template says 'Call memory_search' but doesn't enforce utilization. Claude calls it to satisfy the instruction, then ignores results.

FIX:
1. In lfg.md, add at each phase transition:
   ```
   ## MEMORY CHECK — REQUIRED
   Before starting Phase N, call memory_search with the current task description.
   If results are returned:
   - List them explicitly in your response
   - State which ones are relevant and WHY
   - Incorporate relevant ones into the plan/work/review
   If no results: state 'No relevant lessons found.' and proceed.
   ```
2. In work.md, add per-subtask memory check:
   ```
   Before delegating to any agent, call memory_search with that agent's specific task.
   Include retrieved lessons in the agent's prompt.
   ```
3. In compound.md, add explicit memory_capture enforcement:
   ```
   At minimum, capture:
   - 1 lesson per significant implementation decision
   - 1 pattern for any code approach chosen over alternatives
   - 1 solution for any problem that was debugged/resolved
   ```
4. Make the instruction explicit: 'Display memory results to the user. Do not silently discard them.'

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- All phase templates include explicit memory_search checkpoints
- Memory results must be displayed (not silently consumed)
- work.md requires per-subtask memory_search in agent prompts
- compound.md requires minimum capture counts
close_reason: MEMORY CHECK added to all 5 lfg phases. Display requirement in work.md. Min capture in compound.md.

Add a lightweight pattern-match safety net as fallback.

title: Documentation must be crystal clear and user-friendly
description:
# Documentation Quality for v0.2.4

All documentation must be crystal clear and user-friendly. This is especially important for v0.2.4 since the system is being redesigned.

## Documentation Targets

### 1. README.md
- Quick start in < 30 seconds of reading
- Clear value proposition
- One-shot setup command prominently displayed

### 2. doc/SPEC.md
- Update retrieval strategy section (no more plan-time hook)
- Update architecture section with MCP server
- Update hook configuration section
- Add MCP tool specifications

### 3. Prime Output
- The prime output IS documentation for Claude
- Must use trust language patterns effectively
- Must be scannable and actionable

### 4. MCP Tool Descriptions
- Tool descriptions are documentation Claude sees
- "Mandatory recall" language must be clear
- Trigger conditions must be explicit

### 5. Error Messages
- All CLI errors must be helpful
- Include suggested fixes
- Never just "Error: failed"

## Quality Criteria

### Clarity
- [ ] Can a new user set up in < 2 minutes?
- [ ] Is the hybrid model explained clearly?
- [ ] Are tool trigger conditions obvious?

### Completeness
- [ ] All commands documented
- [ ] All tools documented
- [ ] Migration path for v0.2.3 users clear

### Consistency
- [ ] Terminology consistent (lesson vs memory)
- [ ] Command names consistent (lna everywhere)
- [ ] Trust language patterns consistent

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] README can be followed by a new user without issues

title: Fix SQLite schema: add pattern columns, update FTS5 index, sync, and row conversion
description:
## Problem
The SQLite schema and sync logic completely omit the pattern field. Pattern-based items lose their bad/good code transformation data when cached in SQLite.

## Files to Change
- src/memory/storage/sqlite/schema.ts: Add pattern_bad, pattern_good TEXT columns to lessons table. Add pattern_bad, pattern_good to FTS5 virtual table (lessons_fts). Bump SCHEMA_VERSION to 3.
- src/memory/storage/sqlite/types.ts: Add pattern_bad, pattern_good to LessonRow interface. Rename LessonRow → MemoryItemRow for clarity.
- src/memory/storage/sqlite/sync.ts: Update INSERT_LESSON_SQL to include pattern_bad, pattern_good. Extract pattern fields from MemoryItem during rebuild.
- src/memory/storage/sqlite/search.ts: Update rowToLesson() → rowToMemoryItem() to reconstruct pattern field from pattern_bad/pattern_good columns. Return MemoryItem type instead of Lesson. Fix the `as LessonRow[]` casts at lines 114 and 129.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Pattern items round-trip through SQLite without data loss
- [ ] FTS5 indexes pattern_bad and pattern_good for keyword search
- [ ] Schema auto-rebuilds on version mismatch (v2 → v3)
- [ ] All existing tests pass + new tests for pattern storage/retrieval
- [ ] rowToLesson renamed to rowToMemoryItem

title: P0: Harden plan.md to GUARANTEE review+compound task creation
description:
PROBLEM: plan.md lines 97-104 instruct Claude to create review and compound blocking tasks. In practice, Claude skipped this step entirely.

FIELD EVIDENCE (session 37b79df7):
- Plan phase created 6 work tasks (8yi, e2j, 6fa, gnp, i1d, 89v)
- NO review task was created
- NO compound task was created
- The instruction at line 97-104 was ignored

ROOT CAUSE: The instruction is embedded in a numbered list. Claude executes steps 1-8 (create work tasks) and considers the plan 'done' before reaching step 9.

FIX (in src/setup/templates/commands.ts, plan.md template):
1. Move review+compound task creation to STEP 1 (before work task creation), not step 9
2. Add verification: 'Run bd list | grep Review. If empty, you FAILED to create the review task. Create it NOW.'
3. Add a POST-PLAN VERIFICATION section:
   ```
   ## POST-PLAN VERIFICATION — MANDATORY
   Run these commands and verify output:
   bd list --status=open | grep 'Review:'    # MUST show a result
   bd list --status=open | grep 'Compound:'  # MUST show a result
   If either is missing, CREATE THEM NOW. The plan is NOT complete without these.
   ```
4. Make review task BLOCK epic closure via dependency: bd dep add <epic> <review-id>

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- Review+compound task creation is step 1-2 in plan.md, not buried at step 9
- Template includes post-plan verification that checks tasks actually exist
- Review task blocks epic (dependency set)
- Compound task blocks review task (dependency chain: work → review → compound → epic close)
close_reason: Review+compound creation moved to step 8. POST-PLAN VERIFICATION section added.

title: Bulk-read cached embeddings instead of N individual queries
description: searchVector() in vector.ts calls getCachedEmbedding(repoRoot, item.id, hash) in a loop — one prepared statement execution per lesson. For 200 lessons, that is 200 separate SQLite queries (~15-40ms wasted). The knowledge/search.ts path already solves this correctly with a single bulk SELECT: 'SELECT id, embedding FROM chunks WHERE embedding IS NOT NULL'. Fix: add a bulk getCachedEmbeddings() function to sqlite/cache.ts that returns a Map<id, {vector, hash}>, then use it in searchVector to compute similarities without per-item SQLite round-trips. Key files: src/memory/storage/sqlite/cache.ts, src/memory/search/vector.ts, reference: src/memory/knowledge/search.ts.

title: Add 'uninstall' command to remove all learning-agent artifacts
description:
User asked 'How can I uninstall this whole setup?' - there's no clean uninstall option.

**Components to uninstall:**
1. Claude Code hooks (from settings.json)
2. Pre-commit hook (from .git/hooks/pre-commit)
3. AGENTS.md section (Learning Agent block)
4. Lessons directory (.claude/lessons/)
5. SQLite cache (.claude/.cache/)

**Proposed commands:**
```bash
npx learning-agent uninstall --all        # Everything
npx learning-agent uninstall --hooks      # Just hooks
npx learning-agent uninstall --dry-run    # Preview
npx learning-agent setup claude --uninstall  # Already exists for Claude
```

**Note:** 'setup claude --uninstall' exists but other uninstall options are missing
notes:
## Tests to Implement

### Unit Tests
- [ ] uninstall --all removes Claude hooks
- [ ] uninstall --all removes pre-commit hook block
- [ ] uninstall --all removes AGENTS.md section
- [ ] uninstall --hooks only removes hooks (not lessons)
- [ ] uninstall --dry-run shows what would be removed without removing

### Integration Tests
- [ ] Full cycle: init -> learn -> uninstall --all -> clean state
- [ ] uninstall preserves lessons with --keep-lessons flag
- [ ] After uninstall, Claude session shows no hooks active

### Edge Cases
- [ ] Partial installation (some components missing) -> uninstall what exists
- [ ] Pre-commit has other hooks -> only remove our block
- [ ] AGENTS.md has custom content -> only remove our section
- [ ] Settings.json has other hooks -> only remove ours
- [ ] Nothing installed -> graceful no-op with message
- [ ] Lessons directory has data -> require --force or prompt

title: Fix: PostToolUseFailure counters broken by process isolation
description:
PROBLEM: The failure tracking in hooks.ts uses module-level variables (failureCount, sameTargetCount, lastFailedTarget). Each npx ca hooks run post-tool-failure spawns a NEW Node.js process. The counters always start at 0, increment to 1, check thresholds (>=2 same target OR >=3 total), never reach them, and exit. The FAILURE_TIP is never emitted.

EVIDENCE: src/setup/hooks.ts:149-151 (module-level let variables). processToolFailure() at lines 189-214. processToolSuccess() at lines 219-221 is equally broken — resets counters that are already 0.

IMPACT: The entire PostToolUseFailure/PostToolUse hook pair is dead code in practice. The failure detection feature advertised in the changelog does not work.

FIX OPTIONS:
1. Use a temp file (.claude/.ca-failure-state.json) to persist counters across process invocations
2. Use a lightweight SQLite table (the DB is already used elsewhere)
3. Remove the feature if not worth fixing (simplest)

RECOMMENDATION: Option 1 (temp file) is simplest and lowest risk. Write JSON with {count, lastTarget, sameTargetCount, timestamp}. Clean up stale files (>1h old) on read.
close_reason: PostToolUseFailure state persisted to .ca-failure-state.json

title: Code quality audit: Verify compliance with doc/ standards
description:
## Overview
Audit codebase against quality standards defined in doc/ and .claude/CLAUDE.md.

## Standards to Verify

### From .claude/CLAUDE.md

#### Module Design (Parnas Principles)
- [ ] Public APIs exported via index.ts only
- [ ] Internal files NOT exported through index
- [ ] Clear module boundaries

#### Code Size Limits
- [ ] Functions < 50 lines
- [ ] Files < 300 lines
- [ ] Single responsibility per module

#### Professional Standards
- [ ] Type hints on all public APIs
- [ ] JSDoc on all public functions
- [ ] Clear, descriptive names
- [ ] No magic numbers
- [ ] No commented-out code

### From doc/SPEC.md
- [ ] API matches specification
- [ ] Error handling follows spec
- [ ] CLI commands match spec

### Anti-Patterns to Detect
- [ ] No utils/helpers modules (unclear responsibility)
- [ ] No deep nesting (prefer early returns)
- [ ] No over-engineering
- [ ] No global variables

## Files to Audit
All src/**/*.ts files, with focus on:
- Public API files (index.ts)
- Core modules (storage/, search/, embeddings/)
- CLI commands (commands/)

## Process
1. Generate list of all source files
2. For each file:
   - Check line count
   - Check function lengths
   - Verify JSDoc on exports
   - Check for anti-patterns
3. Report violations
4. Fix critical issues
5. Create follow-up tasks for non-critical improvements

## Output
- Compliance report
- List of violations (if any)
- PRs for critical fixes

title: Claude doesn't trust/follow lessons from hooks
description:
# Problem

Claude doesn't trust or effectively use lessons that are auto-injected via hooks. Field testing showed:
- Claude ignores or forgets injected lessons
- The "push" model doesn't align with how Claude naturally works
- Lessons feel like external noise rather than Claude's own knowledge

# Root Cause Analysis

The current system uses a **push model**: lessons are injected into context at session start and plan time. This creates several issues:

1. **No agency**: Claude is a passive recipient, doesn't "own" the knowledge
2. **Context noise**: Injected lessons may not be relevant to current task
3. **Trust deficit**: Auto-injected content feels less authoritative than tool results
4. **Forgetting**: After compaction or long sessions, injected context fades

# Solution: Hybrid Push-Pull Model

The v0.2.4 redesign addresses this by combining:

## 1. Trusted Injection (Push)
- Keep SessionStart/PreCompact hooks
- But inject **guidelines on HOW to use lessons**, not just raw lessons
- Use Beads-style trust language ("MUST", "CRITICAL")
- Include only top 3-5 high-severity lessons

## 2. Native MCP Tools (Pull)
- `lesson_search` tool Claude can call when needed
- Tool description includes "Mandatory recall" language
- Claude has agency to search, builds ownership
- Results feel more trustworthy (Claude requested them)

## 3. Capture Tool
- `lesson_capture` tool for Claude to take notes
- When Claude captures lessons itself, it owns them
- More likely to remember and trust its own notes

# Why This Should Work

Research from Moltbot shows tool-based memory creates stronger engagement:
- Agent actively decides to search → more trust in results
- "Mandatory recall" language in tool description → consistent behavior
- Two-step retrieval (search then fetch) → keeps context small

Research from Beads shows injection can work when:
- It teaches HOW to use the system, not just dumps data
- Trust language patterns are used consistently
- Clear protocols with explicit consequences

# Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Claude proactively uses `lesson_search` when making complex decisions
- [ ] Claude captures lessons when corrected without prompting
- [ ] Lessons retrieved via tool feel more trusted than auto-injected
- [ ] Field testing shows improved lesson adherence
close_reason: Addressed by v0.2.4 MCP integration and trust language redesign in prime command and AGENTS.md

title: Task 2: Status file module (embed-status.ts)
description:
Create embed status file module for reporting embedding progress/completion to the user.

## Context
When background embedding spawns after ca init/setup, the user needs to know when it completes. A status file at .claude/.cache/embed-status.json tracks state transitions: idle -> running -> completed|failed.

## Files
- NEW: src/memory/knowledge/embed-status.ts
- NEW: src/memory/knowledge/embed-status.test.ts

## Implementation
Status file path: .claude/.cache/embed-status.json

### Types
interface EmbedStatus {
  state: 'idle' | 'running' | 'completed' | 'failed';
  chunksTotal?: number;
  chunksEmbedded?: number;
  startedAt?: string;       // ISO timestamp
  completedAt?: string;     // ISO timestamp
  error?: string;           // Error message on failure
  durationMs?: number;
}

### Functions
- writeEmbedStatus(repoRoot: string, status: EmbedStatus): void
  - writeFileSync with JSON.stringify(status, null, 2)
  - mkdirSync parent dir if needed

- readEmbedStatus(repoRoot: string): EmbedStatus | null
  - readFileSync + JSON.parse
  - Return null on ENOENT or JSON parse error (graceful)

## Tests (TDD - write first)
1. Write then read roundtrips correctly for each state (idle, running, completed, failed)
2. readEmbedStatus returns null when file does not exist
3. readEmbedStatus returns null for malformed JSON content
4. All optional fields preserved through roundtrip
5. writeEmbedStatus creates parent directory if missing

## Dependencies
None (Layer 0). Uses only node:fs, node:path.

## Patterns to follow
- Same as .ca-failure-state.json pattern in hooks-failure-tracker.ts
- JSON file with graceful read (try/catch returning null)
close_reason: Closed

title: FEATURE: Manual invalidation CLI (lna wrong)
description:
## Problem
No way to mark lessons as incorrect after retrieval. Bad lessons continue to be retrieved, poisoning agent context.

## Research Findings
- LANDSCAPE.md: 'No Feedback Loop: Missing this memory was wrong mechanism'
- Research synthesis: Most production systems use implicit signals, but explicit invalidation is the simplest lightweight mechanism
- HaluMem shows systems accumulate hallucinations without correction mechanism

## Proposed Solution
1. Add CLI command: `lna wrong <id> --reason 'explanation'`
2. Add schema fields: `invalidatedAt`, `invalidationReason`
3. Invalidated lessons excluded from retrieval by default
4. Add `lna validate <id>` to undo invalidation
5. Add `lna list --invalidated` to show invalidated lessons

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Schema includes `invalidatedAt` (ISO timestamp) and `invalidationReason` (string)
- [ ] `lna wrong <id>` sets invalidatedAt and excludes from retrieval
- [ ] `lna wrong <id> --reason 'text'` stores explanation
- [ ] `lna validate <id>` clears invalidation (re-enables retrieval)
- [ ] `lna list --invalidated` shows invalidated lessons with reasons
- [ ] Invalidated lessons shown with strikethrough or [INVALID] tag in list
- [ ] Tests cover full invalidation/validation cycle
close_reason: Closed

description:

1. Beads tasks: Does a 'Review: /compound:review' task exist for this epic? Is it closed?
2. Beads tasks: Does a 'Compound: /compound:compound' task exist? Is it closed?
3. Quality: Has 'pnpm test && pnpm lint' passed? (check exit code or recent test output)
4. Reviewer: Has /implementation-reviewer been invoked? (check beads notes or recent git log for reviewer markers)

Output: PASS/FAIL with specific failures listed.
Exit code: 0 = all gates pass, 1 = gates failed (blocks epic closure in template).

IMPLEMENTATION NOTES:
- Add to src/commands/ as verify-gates.ts
- Wire into src/cli.ts via registerVerifyGatesCommands()
- Parse beads issue list (bd show <epic-id>) to find child tasks by title pattern matching
- The lfg.md and plan.md templates will call this command before allowing epic closure

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- 'ca verify-gates <epic-id>' returns PASS only when review+compound tasks exist AND are closed
- Returns structured FAIL output listing which gates are missing
- Exit code 1 on failure (so templates can check)
- Unit tests covering: no review task, review task open, review task closed, no compound task, all gates pass

title: EPIC 3: Setup Command & Claude Code File Generation
description:
# Redesign setup command to generate all Claude Code integration files

## Goal
Single npx compound-agent setup generates all agent definitions, slash commands,
phase skills, hooks config, and MCP config. Claude Code discovers them natively.

## Scope
- Redesign setup command (currently lna setup)
- Generate .claude/agents/compound/*.md (agent definitions)
- Generate .claude/commands/compound/*.md (slash commands)
- Generate .claude/skills/compound/*.md (phase skills)
- Update .claude/settings.json (hooks)
- Update .mcp.json (MCP server)
- --update flag: regenerate files preserving user customizations
- --uninstall flag: remove all generated files
- --status flag: show current installation status

## Exit Criteria
- npx compound-agent setup generates all files
- Claude Code discovers agents, commands, skills
- Hooks fire correctly (SessionStart, PreCompact, UserPromptSubmit)
- MCP server registers with correct tool names
- --update regenerates without losing user changes
- --uninstall cleans up completely
- All tests pass

## Context
- Architecture: doc/ARCHITECTURE-V2.md (Distribution section)
- Current setup: src/cli/setup/
- Generated file templates will be embedded in the package

title: Implement PreToolUse Edit/Write hook for phase guard warnings
description:
NEW HOOK: PreToolUse on Edit|Write — soft warning when Claude edits without reading the current phase's skill.

HOOK CONFIG (templates.ts):
- matcher: 'Edit|Write'
- command: 'npx ca hooks run phase-guard 2>/dev/null || true'

HANDLER (hooks.ts):
1. Read JSON from stdin (contains tool_name, tool_input)
2. Check if .claude/.ca-phase-state.json exists and lfg_active=true
3. If no state file or not active: exit 0 with empty JSON (no interference)
4. Read current_phase and skills_read from state
5. Build expected skill path: .claude/skills/compound/{current_phase}/SKILL.md
6. Check if expected path is in skills_read array
7. If NOT read: exit 0 with JSON containing additionalContext warning:
   'PHASE GUARD WARNING: You are in LFG phase {N}/5 ({phase_name}) but have NOT read the skill file yet. Read .claude/skills/compound/{phase}/SKILL.md before continuing. The skill file contains the mandatory workflow for this phase.'
8. If read: exit 0 with empty JSON (allow silently)

ENFORCEMENT TYPE: Soft — warning injection via additionalContext. Does not block the Edit/Write.

DEPENDS ON: ca phase-check + PostToolUse Read hook (needs state file with skills_read tracking)
close_reason: Fully implemented: PreToolUse Edit/Write phase guard warns on missing skill read

title: CCT subagent: mistake-derived test injection in TDD pipeline
description: New TDD pipeline step. The CCT (Claude Code Tests) subagent plugs into the pipeline BEFORE test-first-enforcer. FLOW: (1) Read the CCT config file (populated by compounding subagent), (2) Analyze the current feature/task context (what files are being changed, what domain), (3) Check if any testable patterns from CCT config apply, (4) If matches found: output test requirements that feed into the test-first-enforcer step, (5) If no matches: pass through silently. PIPELINE POSITION: invariant-designer -> CCT-subagent -> test-first-enforcer -> ... This means the TDD pipeline now has an 'institutional memory' — it remembers past mistakes and ensures tests cover them. The subagent should be a .claude/agents/ prompt file that can be invoked as /cct-check or similar.

title: Update AGENTS.md template with strong 'no manual editing' warning
description:
Add explicit instructions preventing Claude from manually editing JSONL files.

**Current AGENTS.md template issue:**
Claude in testing manually edited the JSONL file instead of using CLI commands, causing:
- Wrong field used (type='high' instead of severity='high')
- SQLite out of sync
- Lesson not loaded at session start

**Required additions to AGENTS_MD_TEMPLATE:**

```markdown
### ⚠️ IMPORTANT: Never Edit JSONL Directly

**DO NOT** manually edit `.claude/lessons/index.jsonl`.

Always use CLI commands:
```bash
# Create lessons
npx learning-agent learn "insight" --severity high --yes

# Update lessons  
npx learning-agent update L001 --insight "new insight"

# Delete lessons
npx learning-agent delete L001

# List lessons
npx learning-agent list --json
```

Manual edits will:
- Break SQLite sync
- Bypass schema validation
- Potentially corrupt data
```

**Also update:**
- Pre-commit hook message to mention CLI commands
- README with CLI-first approach
- Help text for each command

title: Document linting standards for agent-compliant codebases
description:
## Context

Companion to learning_agent-e625 (Implement agent-compliant linter rules). While that issue 
adds mechanical enforcement, this issue creates the documentation standard that explains 
the *philosophy* and *methodology* behind our linting approach.

This is informed by three research papers (docs/research/):
- **Anthropic** (Carlini): Test harness design, context window management
- **OpenAI** (Lopopolo): Custom linters, taste invariants, golden principles, 
  agent-targeted error messages
- **Agentic Codebase Guide**: File/function limits, layered architecture, 
  dependency management

The goal is a reusable standard that goes beyond this project — it should work as 
a reference for any TypeScript codebase built with agentic AI.

## Deliverables

### 1. New standard: `docs/standards/linting-for-agents.md`

A comprehensive document covering:

**A. Philosophy: Why lint differently for agents**
- Agents lack accumulated project knowledge — they depend on mechanical feedback
- Documentation drifts; lint rules don't
- The OpenAI insight: "custom linters with error messages that inject remediation 
  instructions into agent context"
- The Anthropic insight: "context window pollution" — lint output must be concise 
  and actionable, not verbose dumps
- The hierarchy: enforce mechanically > document > hope agents read it

**B. Rule categories and enforcement tiers**

Map our existing tier system (Inviolable / Strong Default / Soft Default / Recommended) 
to enforcement mechanisms:

| Tier | Enforcement | Example |
|------|------------|---------|
| Inviolable | ESLint error (blocks commit) | no-explicit-any, no-sql-interpolation |
| Strong Default | ESLint warn (visible, non-blocking) | max-lines, max-depth |
| Soft Default | Structural test (visible in test run) | no-utils-dirs, barrel exports |
| Recommended | Documentation only | naming conventions, ADR format |

Explain why each tier maps to its enforcement mechanism.

**C. Writing agent-targeted error messages**

A pattern guide for writing lint errors that agents can act on:

Bad: `File too long (312/300)`
Good: `File exceeds 300-line limit (312 lines). Split this module: (1) Extract related 
functions into a new file in same directory, (2) Re-export from index.ts to maintain 
public API, (3) Run pnpm test:fast to verify. See: docs/standards/code-organization.md`

Rules for error messages:
- State the violation (what)
- State the fix (how, step by step)
- Link to documentation (where to learn more)
- Keep under 3 lines (context window budget)

**D. Custom rule development guide**

How to add new custom ESLint rules to tools/eslint-rules/:
- File structure and naming convention
- How to write the AST visitor
- How to write the meta.messages with agent-targeted text
- How to test custom rules
- How to register in eslint.config.js

**E. Structural tests guide**

How to add new structural tests to src/__structural__/:
- What qualifies as a structural test vs a unit test
- Pattern: describe("Project Structure", () => { ... })
- How to verify file existence, import graphs, config correctness
- When to promote a structural test to an ESLint rule

**F. The "golden principles" pattern**

Borrowing from OpenAI: how we encode subjective preferences as mechanical rules:
- Identify a repeated agent mistake
- Define the rule precisely enough to be mechanical
- Write the ESLint rule or structural test
- Write the agent-targeted error message
- Add to eslint.config.js or structural tests
- Document in this standard

This creates a continuous improvement loop: mistake → rule → enforcement → prevention.

### 2. Update existing standards

**docs/standards/README.md**: Add entry for the new document.

**docs/standards/index.md** (if exists): Add to the documentation map.

**docs/standards/code-organization.md**: Add cross-reference to linting-for-agents.md 
for the mechanical enforcement of its rules (file size, function size, module boundaries).

**docs/standards/anti-patterns.md**: Add cross-reference noting which anti-patterns 
now have corresponding lint rules.

### 3. Update docs/research/index.md

Add entries for the three research papers now in docs/research/:
- BuildingACCompilerAnthropic.md
- HarnessEngineeringOpenAi.md  
- AgenticAiCodebaseGuide.md

With a brief synthesis note on the key finding: mechanical enforcement > documentation.

## Design principles

- This document is itself agent-targeted: it should be discoverable, concise, 
  and actionable for Claude Code reading it
- Cross-reference everything (no orphan docs)
- Include concrete examples, not abstract advice
- The standard should be stable enough to reference from CLAUDE.md

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] docs/standards/linting-for-agents.md exists with all sections (A-F)
- [ ] Existing standards updated with cross-references
- [ ] docs/research/index.md updated with all three papers
- [ ] Document is concise (< 400 lines, following our own rules)
- [ ] All cross-references are valid (no broken links)

title: Add explicit AgentTeam mechanics to workflow templates
description:
Templates say 'spawn agents' but never explain HOW to use Claude Code's team tools. Claude defaults to solo sequential execution. Fix ALL phases to use AgentTeam (TeamCreate) for heavy parallel work, subagents (Task tool) only for quick lookups.

Phase-by-phase AgentTeam usage:

**Brainstorm**: AgentTeam for parallel research
- docs-explorer + code-explorer run simultaneously as teammates
- Subagent OK for: single memory_search call

**Plan**: AgentTeam for parallel analysis
- docs-analyst + repo-analyst + memory-analyst run simultaneously as teammates
- Subagent OK for: checking bd list for existing epic

**Work**: AgentTeam for TDD execution
- test-analyst → then test-writer + implementer as teammates (ping-pong or parallel)
- Define all tasks upfront, parallelize where possible
- Subagent OK for: trivial one-line fixes

**Review**: AgentTeam for 11 parallel reviewers (biggest win)
- All 11 reviewer agents spawn as teammates, run simultaneously
- Reviewers communicate findings via SendMessage
- Subagent OK for: running pnpm test/lint quality gates

**Compound**: AgentTeam for 6 parallel analysts
- context-analyzer + lesson-extractor + docs-reviewer + pattern-matcher + solution-writer + compounding as teammates
- Downstream agents receive upstream results via SendMessage pipeline
- Subagent OK for: single memory_search dedup check

**LFG**: Chains the above — each phase creates its own AgentTeam, tears it down before next phase

What each template needs:
1. Explicit TeamCreate instruction with team naming convention
2. TaskCreate for each agent role (define tasks upfront)
3. Spawn teammates via Task tool with team_name + subagent_type
4. SendMessage for inter-agent coordination and result passing
5. TaskUpdate to track completion
6. TeamDelete / shutdown_request when phase completes
7. Clear guidance: 'Use AgentTeam for heavy parallel work, subagent for quick lookups'
close_reason: Closed

title: Integrate CCT patterns into retrieval engine
description: The compounding subsystem generates cct-patterns.jsonl with synthesized 'playbook' patterns derived from clusters of individual lessons. However, the retrieval engine (searchVector, searchKeyword, retrieveForPlan, loadSessionLessons) does not currently read or use this file. When a user searches for lessons, they only get individual items — never the higher-level synthesized patterns that could provide more actionable guidance. Implement: (1) read cct-patterns.jsonl during search if it exists, (2) include matching patterns in search results alongside individual items, (3) rank patterns slightly higher than individual lessons (they represent validated, recurring knowledge). This is what makes the 'compounding' vision real — individual mistakes become reusable playbooks. Related: src/compound/, src/memory/search/vector.ts, src/memory/retrieval/.

title: Implement 'setup claude' command for hooks integration
description:
## Overview

Implement `npx learning-agent setup claude` command that installs SessionStart hooks into Claude Code's settings file, enabling automatic lesson injection at session start.

## Background

Claude Code supports hooks via `~/.claude/settings.json` (global) or `.claude/settings.json` (project). The SessionStart hook fires on: startup, resume, clear, compact. Any stdout from hook commands becomes Claude context automatically.

Reference: https://code.claude.com/docs/en/hooks

Beads uses this exact pattern with `bd setup claude` → `bd prime`.

## Requirements

### Core Functionality

1. **Detect settings location**
   - Global: `~/.claude/settings.json`
   - Project: `.claude/settings.json` (with `--project` flag)

2. **Read existing settings**
   - Parse JSON safely (handle malformed)
   - Preserve all existing configuration
   - Handle missing file (create new)

3. **Merge hooks configuration**
   - Add `hooks.SessionStart` array entry
   - Do NOT overwrite existing SessionStart hooks
   - Use marker in command to identify our hook

4. **Write settings back**
   - Pretty-print JSON (2-space indent)
   - Atomic write (temp file + rename)

### Hook Configuration

```json
{
  "hooks": {
    "SessionStart": [
      {
        "matcher": "startup|resume|compact",
        "hooks": [
          {
            "type": "command",
            "command": "npx learning-agent load-session 2>/dev/null || true"
          }
        ]
      }
    ]
  }
}
```

**Key details**:
- `matcher`: Fire on startup, resume after idle, and after compaction
- `2>/dev/null || true`: Never block Claude startup even if errors
- Output goes to stdout → becomes Claude context

### CLI Interface

```bash
# Install globally (default)
npx learning-agent setup claude

# Install to project only
npx learning-agent setup claude --project

# Remove hooks
npx learning-agent setup claude --uninstall

# Show what would change
npx learning-agent setup claude --dry-run

# Machine-readable output
npx learning-agent setup claude --json
```

### Output Messages

**Success (global)**:
```
[ok] Claude Code hooks installed
  Location: ~/.claude/settings.json
  Hook: SessionStart (startup|resume|compact)
  
Lessons will be loaded automatically at session start.
```

**Success (project)**:
```
[ok] Claude Code hooks installed (project-level)
  Location: .claude/settings.json
  Hook: SessionStart (startup|resume|compact)
  
Note: Project hooks override global hooks.
```

**Already installed**:
```
[info] Learning agent hooks already installed
  Location: ~/.claude/settings.json
```

**Uninstall**:
```
[ok] Learning agent hooks removed
  Location: ~/.claude/settings.json
```

### JSON Output Format

```json
{
  "installed": true,
  "location": "~/.claude/settings.json",
  "hooks": ["SessionStart"],
  "action": "created" | "updated" | "unchanged" | "removed"
}
```

## Implementation Notes

### Settings File Structure

Claude Code settings can have many fields. Only touch `hooks`:

```json
{
  "permissions": { ... },
  "mcpServers": { ... },
  "hooks": {
    "SessionStart": [ ... ],
    "PreToolUse": [ ... ]
  }
}
```

### Idempotency

Check if our hook already exists before adding:
- Look for command containing `learning-agent load-session`
- If found, skip (don't duplicate)

### Error Handling

- Settings file doesn't exist: Create new with just hooks
- Settings file malformed: Error with helpful message, don't corrupt
- Permission denied: Error with suggestion to check permissions
- No home directory: Error (should not happen)

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `setup claude` installs hooks to global settings
- [ ] `setup claude --project` installs to project settings
- [ ] `setup claude --uninstall` removes hooks
- [ ] `setup claude --dry-run` shows changes without writing
- [ ] `setup claude --json` outputs machine-readable result
- [ ] Idempotent (re-running doesn't duplicate)
- [ ] Preserves existing settings and hooks
- [ ] Tests cover all scenarios
- [ ] Works on macOS and Linux

## Testing Strategy

1. Create temp directory with mock settings
2. Test install to empty file
3. Test install with existing hooks (preserve them)
4. Test install with our hook already present (idempotent)
5. Test uninstall
6. Test dry-run shows but doesn't write
7. Test --project flag uses correct path
8. Test JSON output format
close_reason: setup claude command implemented with all options (--project, --uninstall, --dry-run, --json)

title: Create ca clean-lessons CLI command
description:
New CLI command that scans all lessons for similar pairs and outputs a structured diagnostic that tells Claude to spawn a reviewer subagent.

## Depends on
- findSimilarLessons() function (learning_agent-vos)

## New file: src/commands/clean-lessons.ts

Register in `src/commands/index.ts`: add import and call `registerCleanLessonsCommand(program)` inside `registerManagementCommands()`.

## Add to SQLite preflight

In `src/cli-preflight.ts`, add `'clean-lessons'` to the `NEEDS_SQLITE` set (currently at line ~19-31). This ensures native-module failures produce polished error messages instead of raw runtime crashes. Update `src/cli-preflight.test.ts` to include the new command in assertions.

## Model error handling

Don't rely on `isModelAvailable()` alone (it's file-existence only). Instead:
1. Check `isModelAvailable()` first — if false, print clear error and exit
2. Attempt an early `embedText('test')` probe wrapped in try/catch — if it throws, print error about model being unusable and exit. This catches runtime failures (wrong architecture, missing SIMD, etc.)
3. Call `unloadEmbedding()` in the error path too

## Command definition

```
ca clean-lessons
  Description: Analyze lessons for semantic duplicates and contradictions
```

## Logic (cleanLessonsAction)

1. `getRepoRoot()` from `../cli-utils.js`
2. Model availability + usability check (see above)
3. `syncIfNeeded(repoRoot)`
4. `readMemoryItems(repoRoot)` — get all items
5. For each non-invalidated item, call `findSimilarLessons(repoRoot, item.insight, { excludeId: item.id })`
   IMPORTANT: Use `item.insight` only, not trigger + insight (avoid false positives from generic triggers)
6. Collect pairs, dedup symmetric pairs using sorted ID key: `[idA, idB].sort().join(':')` in a Set
7. `unloadEmbedding()` in finally block
8. Output via `console.log()` (stdout-as-prompt pattern)

### If no pairs found:
```
No similar lessons found. Your lesson database is clean.
```

### If pairs found:
```markdown
# Lessons Review Required

Found N similar lesson pair(s) that may need attention.

## Flagged Pairs

### Pair 1: L12345678 <-> La1b2c3d4 (similarity: 92%)
- **L12345678**: <full insight text>
- **La1b2c3d4**: <full insight text>

## Instructions

Spawn the lessons-reviewer subagent to analyze these pairs:

/lessons-reviewer

The reviewer will classify each pair and propose cleanup actions.
```

## Performance

O(n^2) in embedding comparisons. With SQLite cache, each lesson is embedded once. For 100 lessons: ~100 embed calls (most cached) + ~10,000 cosine similarity ops (sub-ms each). Acceptable.

## Tests (TDD)

New file: `src/commands/clean-lessons.test.ts`
- Outputs 'clean' message when no similar pairs
- Outputs flagged pairs when duplicates exist
- Deduplicates symmetric pairs (A-B and B-A counted as one)
- Outputs reviewer instructions in the flagged output
- Fails gracefully with clear error when model unavailable
- Fails gracefully with clear error when model file exists but runtime init fails
close_reason: ca clean-lessons command created with model probe, pairwise scan, dedup, structured diagnostic output. Added to NEEDS_SQLITE preflight. 5 new tests.

Inline markdown, no SKILL.md.

title: Audit subagent + lna audit CLI command
description: Inspired by OpenAI 'garbage collection' concept. A subagent that checks the codebase against stored lessons and rules, flagging violations. TWO INTERFACES: (1) CLI: lna audit — runs mechanical checks (rule engine from Phase 1) + outputs violations in agent-legible format, (2) Subagent: /audit — uses Claude to do deeper analysis, checking if code patterns match known mistake patterns from lessons, even when the match is semantic not syntactic. WHAT IT CHECKS: (a) Rule engine violations (from lna rules), (b) Code matching 'bad' patterns from lessons that have pattern.bad defined, (c) Files that haven't been updated but relate to modified files (drift risk), (d) Lessons with high severity that may apply to current changes. OUTPUT: Structured report with: file, issue, related lesson ID, suggested fix. NEW PIPELINE STEP: This becomes the audit step between module-boundary-reviewer and implementation-reviewer.

title: UserPromptSubmit hook: Gentle lesson tool reminders
description:
## Overview
Implement a UserPromptSubmit Claude Code hook that detects two scenarios in the user's message and injects gentle reminders about lesson tools.

**Goal**: Encourage Claude to naturally use lesson_capture and lesson_search without being aggressive.

## How UserPromptSubmit Works
```
User types message → Hook fires → Analyzes user's words → Injects additionalContext → Claude sees reminder
```
- Hook receives: `{ prompt: "user's message", session_id: "...", cwd: "..." }`
- Hook outputs: `{ hookSpecificOutput: { additionalContext: "reminder text" } }`
- Exit 0 = success, context injected (subtle, non-blocking)

## Scenario 1: Correction Detected

### Trigger Patterns (in user's message)
- 'actually', 'no,', 'wrong', 'that\'s not right', 'that\'s incorrect'
- 'use X instead', 'I told you', 'I already said', 'not like that'
- 'you forgot', 'you missed', 'stop', 'wait'

### Reminder (gentle)
```
Remember: You have lesson tools available - lesson_capture to save insights, lesson_search to find past solutions.
```

## Scenario 2: Planning/Strategy/Implementation

### Trigger Patterns (in user's message)
**Decision language:**
- 'decide', 'choose', 'pick', 'which approach', 'what do you think'
- 'should we', 'would you', 'how should', 'what\'s the best'

**Implementation language:**
- 'implement', 'build', 'create', 'refactor', 'fix'
- 'add feature', 'write', 'develop', 'set up'

### Reminder (encouraging)
```
If you're uncertain or hesitant, remember your lesson tools: lesson_search may have relevant context from past sessions.
```

## Implementation Details

### Hook Registration
```json
// Installed by 'npx lna init' or 'npx lna setup claude'
{
  "hooks": {
    "UserPromptSubmit": [{
      "matcher": "",
      "hooks": [{
        "type": "command",
        "command": "npx lna hooks run user-prompt 2>/dev/null || true"
      }]
    }]
  }
}
```

### Command Handler
```typescript
// npx lna hooks run user-prompt
// 1. Read stdin JSON
// 2. Extract prompt field
// 3. Check correction patterns → inject correction reminder
// 4. Else check planning patterns → inject planning reminder  
// 5. Else exit 0 silently (no injection)
```

### Rate Limiting (prevent fatigue)
- Track last reminder type + timestamp in temp file
- Don't repeat same reminder type within N prompts
- Correction reminders take priority over planning reminders

## Files to Modify
- src/commands/setup/hooks.ts - Add `runUserPromptHook()` handler
- src/commands/setup/templates.ts - Add CLAUDE_USER_PROMPT_HOOK_CONFIG
- src/cli.ts - Register `hooks run user-prompt` subcommand
- src/commands/setup/claude.ts - Install hook in settings.json

## Testing
- Unit: Pattern detection for corrections
- Unit: Pattern detection for planning/implementation
- Unit: JSON output format
- Unit: Rate limiting logic
- Integration: Hook installation in settings.json

## Success Criteria
- Reminders feel helpful, not nagging
- Claude starts using lesson tools more naturally
- No false positives on casual conversation
close_reason: Closed

title: Add 'setup claude --status' diagnostic command
description:
## Overview

Add `--status` flag to `setup claude` command that checks if hooks are properly installed and working.

## Background

Users need a way to verify their Claude Code integration is working correctly. This is similar to `bd doctor` in beads.

## Requirements

### Status Check

```bash
npx learning-agent setup claude --status
```

**Output (all good)**:
```
Claude Code Integration Status

Settings file: ~/.claude/settings.json
  [ok] File exists
  [ok] Valid JSON
  [ok] SessionStart hook installed

Hook test: npx learning-agent load-session
  [ok] Command executes
  [ok] Output format valid

All checks passed.
```

**Output (issues found)**:
```
Claude Code Integration Status

Settings file: ~/.claude/settings.json
  [ok] File exists
  [ok] Valid JSON
  [warn] SessionStart hook NOT installed

Run 'npx learning-agent setup claude' to install hooks.
```

### JSON Output

```json
{
  "settingsFile": "~/.claude/settings.json",
  "exists": true,
  "validJson": true,
  "hookInstalled": false,
  "hookTest": null,
  "status": "incomplete"
}
```

### Checks to Perform

1. **Settings file exists**: Check path exists
2. **Valid JSON**: Can parse without error
3. **Hook installed**: Our command in SessionStart
4. **Hook test** (optional): Actually run load-session, verify output

## Implementation

Add to `setup claude` command handler:
- New flag: `--status`
- When set, run checks instead of install
- Can combine with `--json` for machine output

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `--status` flag runs diagnostic checks
- [ ] Shows settings file location and status
- [ ] Shows hook installation status
- [ ] Provides actionable suggestion if not installed
- [ ] `--json` output works with `--status`
- [ ] Tests cover status scenarios

## Notes

Depends on: learning_agent-p4d (setup claude command must exist first)
close_reason: Added --status flag to 'setup claude' command. Shows settings file location, hook installation status, slash commands presence, and overall connection status.

title: FEATURE: Count warning for context pollution prevention
description:
## Problem
Research shows context pollution begins at 6-10 lessons. Users may not realize they've accumulated many lessons, degrading agent performance.

## Research Findings
- LANDSCAPE.md Reviewer 1: 'Context pollution: 6-10 lessons competing for attention. Research: degradation begins at this threshold.'
- GitHub Copilot: uses strict retrieval limits
- Current defaults: load-session=5, check-plan=5 (already good)

## Proposed Solution
1. In `lna stats`, show warning when >20 active lessons
2. In `load-session`, add note: 'X lessons in index. Consider `lna compact`.' when >20
3. Keep existing limits (5) for retrieval commands

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `stats` shows yellow warning when lesson count >20
- [ ] `load-session` shows informational note when >20 lessons exist
- [ ] Warning thresholds are configurable constants (not magic numbers)
- [ ] Tests verify warning appears at correct thresholds

title: Tighten hook pattern matching to reduce false positives
description: ENHANCEMENT: src/commands/setup/hooks.ts:37-75. Correction patterns include \bstop\b, \bwait\b; planning patterns include \bfix\b, \bwrite\b, \bcreate\b. These are common words. Current patterns generate noise that trains users to ignore reminders.

title: Test cleanup: Remove redundant tests, consolidate assertions
description:
## Overview
Review test suite for redundancy and noise. Apply moderate cleanup:
- Remove obvious duplicates
- Consolidate similar assertions
- Keep comprehensive coverage for edge cases

## Guiding Principles
- **Keep**: Edge case tests, property-based tests, error handling tests
- **Remove**: Trivial tests that add no value, duplicate assertions
- **Consolidate**: Multiple tests that verify the same behavior

## Files to Review

### CLI Tests (highest potential for cleanup)
- src/cli/*.test.ts - Check for duplicate command tests
- Look for tests that just verify 'command exists'

### Unit Tests
- src/storage/*.test.ts
- src/search/*.test.ts
- src/embeddings/*.test.ts
- src/commands/*.test.ts

## Red Flags (candidates for removal)
1. Tests that verify TypeScript types exist (type system already verifies)
2. Tests that just call a function and check it doesn't throw (without meaningful assertions)
3. Multiple tests for the same edge case in different files
4. Tests that duplicate what property tests already cover

## Process
1. Run `pnpm test` to get baseline count
2. Review each test file
3. Mark tests for removal with reasoning
4. Consolidate similar tests
5. Run `pnpm test` to verify same coverage
6. Document final count and what was removed

## Constraints
- Do NOT reduce actual behavioral coverage
- Keep all property-based tests (fast-check)
- Keep all integration tests
- Keep all error/edge case tests

## Expected Outcome
- ~5-15% test count reduction
- Faster test suite
- Same or better coverage of actual behaviors
close_reason: Test cleanup: removed 67 duplicate tests from setup.test.ts (1189→1122 tests). Duplicates from init.test.ts, setup-claude.test.ts, download-model.test.ts. All 1122 remaining tests pass.

title: Consolidate commands into skills: single source of truth
description:
## Problem
Commands (commands.ts) and skills (skills.ts) maintain separate copies of the same phase content. This caused drift (v1.2.5 had to sync them) and doubles maintenance cost. After v1.2.6 simplification, they are ~80% identical.

## Desired State
Skills become the SINGLE source of truth. Commands are either:
- A) Dropped entirely (if Claude Code no longer needs .claude/commands/)
- B) Auto-generated from skills (thin wrapper adding argument-hint frontmatter)

## Current Differences
- Commands have argument-hint in YAML frontmatter — skills don't
- Commands have Beads Integration / Docs Integration as separate sections
- Skills have Common Pitfalls / Quality Criteria sections
- Skills have 4000 char limit — commands don't
- Commands have $ARGUMENTS placeholder — skills don't

## Approach
1. Verify Claude Code still loads .claude/commands/ (or if skills fully replace them)
2. If commands still needed: generate them from skills by adding argument-hint + $ARGUMENTS wrapper
3. If commands deprecated: remove commands.ts, update installWorkflowCommands() to skip or warn
4. Merge the unique content from commands into skills (Beads Integration sections are useful)
5. Either raise or remove the 4000 char skill limit if skills need to carry more content
6. Update all tests accordingly
7. Update primitives.ts install functions

## Files Affected
- src/setup/templates/commands.ts — remove or make it a thin derivation layer
- src/setup/templates/skills.ts — becomes the single source
- src/setup/primitives.ts — update installWorkflowCommands() and installPhaseSkills()
- src/setup/templates/commands.test.ts — update or remove
- src/setup/templates/skills.test.ts — update char limits and assertions
- All phase test files — simplify (no more cross-format consistency tests needed)

## Prerequisite
Check learning_agent-9cd9 (v1.3.0 skills migration investigation) for Claude Code docs on whether commands are still loaded. If commands are fully deprecated, this is simpler — just drop them.
notes:
APPROACH:
- Skills become the SINGLE source of truth for phase content
- Merge user's detailed workflow from commands into skills (12-step work, adaptive review tiers, etc.)
- Commands become THIN wrappers: frontmatter + $ARGUMENTS + brief summary + 'Follow the <phase> skill'
- Raise 4000 char skill limit if needed (self-imposed, no technical constraint)
- Both tasks (bqfj + rjum) implemented together in one AgentTeam
close_reason: Agent roles moved to skills, commands consolidated. 86 files, 1988 tests pass, lint clean.

title: P1: Add phase state tracking to beads epic metadata
description:
PROBLEM: After context compaction or long sessions, Claude loses track of which LFG phase it's in. The lfg.md has a 'Resume' section (line 338) that says to check bd list --status=in_progress, but this only tells you WHAT task is open, not WHICH PHASE you're in.

FIELD EVIDENCE (session 37b79df7):
- Session completed phases 1-3 but never entered phase 4
- No mechanism to detect 'you finished work but haven't started review'
- After compaction, Claude would have no way to know review/compound are pending

FIX:
1. Add phase tracking via bd update --notes on the epic:
   ```bash
   bd update <epic-id> --notes='Phase: brainstorm COMPLETE'
   bd update <epic-id> --notes='Phase: plan COMPLETE'
   bd update <epic-id> --notes='Phase: work COMPLETE'
   bd update <epic-id> --notes='Phase: review IN_PROGRESS'
   ```
2. Update lfg.md template to write phase state at each transition
3. Update prime.md / resume logic to read phase state from epic notes
4. Add to lfg.md Phase Control section:
   ```
   After each phase completes, update epic notes:
   bd update <epic-id> --notes='Phase: <phase-name> COMPLETE | Next: <next-phase>'
   On resume, read epic notes to determine current phase.
   ```

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- lfg.md writes phase state to epic notes at each transition
- Phase state survives context compaction (it's in beads, not in-memory)
- Resume logic in lfg.md reads phase state to determine where to continue
- bd show <epic-id> clearly shows current phase
close_reason: bd update --notes phase tracking at each transition. Resume reads notes field.

title: CRITICAL: No way to create high-severity lessons via CLI
description:
The 'learn' command only creates quick lessons without severity. Users have no way to create high-severity lessons that get loaded at session start.

**Observed behavior:**
- 'npx learning-agent learn "..." --yes' creates lessons with type='quick' and no severity
- loadSessionLessons() requires type='full' AND severity='high' AND confirmed=true
- User manually edited JSONL with type='high' (wrong field!) and it didn't work

**Expected behavior:**
- CLI should allow creating full lessons with severity
- e.g. 'npx learning-agent learn "insight" --severity high'
- Or 'npx learning-agent add-full --insight "..." --severity high'

**Root cause:**
- learn command hardcodes type: 'quick' at line 819 of cli.ts
- No --severity or --type flags exist

**Evidence from user testing:**
User created lesson with CLI, then checked in new session:
```
SessionStart:startup hook success: No high-severity lessons found.
```
Even after manually creating a lesson, it wasn't picked up because:
1. Wrong field: user put type='high' instead of severity='high'
2. No documentation explains required format

**Impact:** P0 - Core feature (session-start lessons) is unusable
notes:
## Tests to Implement

### Unit Tests
- [ ] 'learn --severity high' creates lesson with severity='high'
- [ ] 'learn --severity high' automatically sets type='full'
- [ ] 'learn --type full' creates full lesson (no severity)
- [ ] Severity validation: only 'high', 'medium', 'low' accepted
- [ ] Help text documents --severity and --type flags

### Integration Tests
- [ ] Create high-severity lesson -> new session loads it
- [ ] loadSessionLessons() returns lessons created via CLI
- [ ] Round-trip: create -> list -> load-session shows same lesson

### Edge Cases
- [ ] Invalid severity value -> error with valid options listed
- [ ] --severity without --type -> infer type='full'
- [ ] --severity low -> creates lesson but not loaded at session start
- [ ] Empty insight with --severity -> appropriate error
close_reason: Implemented --severity flag for learn command. 13 tests passing. Data coupling invariant enforced. APPROVED by implementation-reviewer.

**Files:** src/retrieval/session.ts

Also fix lesson-extractor which says 'ensure actionable' at agents.ts:316 - reword to 'prefer actionable when possible'.

title: CRITICAL: Add embedding cache in SQLite
description:
**Problem**: Vector search recomputes embeddings for ALL lessons on EVERY search - O(N) expensive operations.

**Location**: src/search/vector.ts:60-66

**Solution**: Cache embeddings in SQLite using the existing `embedding BLOB` column (already defined in schema but never populated).

**Implementation**:
1. Compute embedding when lesson is appended/indexed
2. Store in SQLite `embedding` column as binary blob
3. At search time, load pre-computed vectors instead of recomputing
4. Add content hash to detect when re-embedding is needed

**Impact**: Search goes from O(N * embedding_time) to O(N * cosine_similarity) - 100x faster.

**Ref**: Code analysis review
close_reason: Implemented with TDD - all tests pass

title: Remove dead exact-match code in quality.ts
description: ENHANCEMENT: src/capture/quality.ts:147-153. Exact match check is unreachable because the Jaccard check above it always catches identical strings (Jaccard similarity of identical strings = 1.0, which is >= 0.8 threshold). Code always returns from Jaccard branch before reaching exact match check. Fix: Remove the dead exact-match block, or reorder it before Jaccard as a fast-path optimization (though performance difference is negligible for ~100 lessons).
close_reason: Wave 1 complete, merged to main

title: Implement ca phase-check CLI command (state machine)
description:
NEW COMMAND: ca phase-check — manages LFG phase state.

SUBCOMMANDS:
- ca phase-check init <epic-id>: Creates .claude/.ca-phase-state.json with lfg_active=true, epic_id, current_phase='brainstorm', phase_index=1, skills_read=[], gates_passed=[], started_at=now
- ca phase-check start <phase>: Updates current_phase and phase_index. Validates phase is one of [brainstorm, plan, work, review, compound]
- ca phase-check gate <name>: Appends gate name to gates_passed array. Valid gates: post-plan, gate-3, gate-4, final
- ca phase-check status: Reads and prints current state in human-readable format
- ca phase-clean: Deletes .ca-phase-state.json (escape hatch for stuck sessions)

STATE FILE: .claude/.ca-phase-state.json
{
  lfg_active: boolean,
  epic_id: string,
  current_phase: string,
  phase_index: number (1-5),
  skills_read: string[] (file paths),
  gates_passed: string[],
  started_at: ISO timestamp
}

STATE FILE LOCATION: Resolve via getRepoRoot(), write to .claude/.ca-phase-state.json. File should be in .gitignore (session-scoped, not committed).

CLEANUP: verify-gates should auto-delete the state file when FINAL GATE passes. ca phase-clean provides manual cleanup.

NEW FILE: src/commands/phase-check.ts
REGISTER: in src/cli.ts alongside other commands
TESTS: src/commands/phase-check.test.ts
close_reason: Fully implemented: ca phase-check CLI with init/get/update/clean/gate subcommands + 70 tests passing

title: Deprecated dependencies from node-llama-cpp → cmake-js
description:
**Problem**: Installing learning-agent shows deprecation warnings for are-we-there-yet, gauge, npmlog, and tar.

**Root Cause**: node-llama-cpp@3.15.1 depends on cmake-js@^7.4.0, which uses:
- tar@^6.2.0 (deprecated)
- npmlog@^6.0.2 (deprecated, pulls in are-we-there-yet and gauge)

**Solution**: cmake-js@8.0.0 fixes this by:
- Upgrading tar to ^7.5.6
- Removing npmlog entirely

**Workaround for consumers**: Add to package.json:
```json
{"pnpm": {"overrides": {"cmake-js": "^8.0.0"}}}
```

**Upstream fix needed**: node-llama-cpp should update cmake-js to ^8.0.0

**Tasks**:
1. Open issue in withcatai/node-llama-cpp requesting cmake-js update
2. Document workaround in README
3. Close this issue when upstream fixes it
close_reason: Superseded by compound-agent mega plan. Fresh start.

title: Fix compaction TOCTOU window
description: CRITICAL BUG: src/storage/compact.ts:206-229. JSONL file read 5 times and written twice during compaction. Concurrent MCP lesson_capture between archiveOldLessons write and rewriteWithoutTombstones read loses data. archiveOldLessons (line 154) calls readLessons, then writes temp + renames (196-198); append between read and rename is silently dropped. Fix (minimal): Document limitation. Fix (proper): Read once, compute in memory, single atomic rewrite. Practical risk moderate since compaction is manual, but MCP server could run simultaneously.

title: Drift detection as pipeline step
description: New step in the TDD pipeline between module-boundary-reviewer and implementation-reviewer. WHAT IT DOES: Before final review, check if the implementation has drifted from: (1) Documented invariants (docs/invariants/), (2) Architecture decisions (docs/adr/), (3) Known patterns from lessons, (4) Rule engine constraints. This is lighter than the full audit — focused on the current PR/change only. It answers: 'does this change violate anything we already know?' IMPLEMENTATION: A .claude/agents/ prompt file that can be invoked as /drift-check. Reads the git diff, cross-references against docs and lessons, reports any concerns. PIPELINE: ... -> module-boundary-reviewer -> drift-check -> implementation-reviewer

title: FEATURE: Optional citation field for lesson provenance
description:
## Problem
Lessons lack context about where they came from. When reviewing stale lessons, there's no way to verify if the source is still valid.

## Research Findings (from GitHub Copilot)
Copilot uses citations with just-in-time verification:
- Stores file/line/commit alongside memories
- Validates at retrieval time
- 28-day expiration + revalidation extends lifespan

## Proposed Solution (Simplified)
1. Add optional `citation` field to schema:
   ```typescript
   citation?: {
     file: string;      // e.g., 'src/api/client.ts'
     line?: number;     // Optional line number
     commit?: string;   // Optional git commit hash
   }
   ```
2. Add CLI flags: `--citation 'file:line'` and `--citation-commit 'hash'`
3. In `list` and `load-session`, show citation if present
4. Lightweight staleness hint: if file modified after lesson created, show note
5. NO automatic verification or blocking

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Schema includes optional `citation` field
- [ ] `learn` command accepts `--citation` and `--citation-commit` flags
- [ ] `list --verbose` shows citations
- [ ] Staleness hint shown when file mtime > lesson created (if file exists)
- [ ] Tests cover citation parsing and display
close_reason: Closed

title: Investigate whether MCP server is still needed vs CLI-only (like beads)
description: The beads project (https://github.com/steveyegge/beads) uses a CLI-only approach — no MCP server. Our compound-agent registers as MCP via .mcp.json to expose memory_search/memory_capture tools. Questions: (1) Is MCP still the recommended integration path for Claude Code tools, or has the ecosystem shifted? (2) Could we achieve the same with CLI + hooks (like beads does)? (3) What do we lose if we drop MCP? (memory_search in-context, auto-invocation by Claude) (4) What do we gain? (simpler install, no .mcp.json, fewer moving parts) (5) Check latest Claude Code docs for MCP vs CLI guidance. (6) Evaluate hybrid approach: keep MCP for search, use CLI for mutations. Output: recommendation with trade-off analysis.
close_reason: Investigation complete: decision is to drop MCP, go CLI-only like beads. Implementation tracked in new tasks under v1.2.9 epic.

title: Test output summarizer for agent consumption
description: Inspired by Carlini paper: context window pollution from test output. Claude wastes context reading thousands of lines of test output when it only needs pass/fail + first failure. WHAT TO BUILD: A wrapper script or lna test-summary command that: (1) Runs pnpm test (or any test command), (2) Captures full output to a log file (e.g., .claude/.cache/last-test-run.log), (3) Prints only: total pass/fail/skip counts, first failing test name + error message (truncated to 10 lines), path to full log file. This keeps agent context clean while preserving full debugging info on disk. BONUS: Add --fast flag that runs pnpm test:fast with summary, --full for pnpm test with summary.

title: Float32Array consistency: stop Array.from() conversions in lesson path
description: The knowledge path (sqlite-knowledge/cache.ts) already returns Float32Array directly to cosineSimilarity(). The lesson path (sqlite/cache.ts, nomic.ts) converts Float32Array -> number[] via Array.from() then back again. This is inconsistent and wastes ~5-15ms on 200 lessons from unnecessary allocations + GC pressure. cosineSimilarity() already accepts ArrayLike<number> which Float32Array satisfies. Fix: change embedText() return type to Float32Array, change getCachedEmbedding() to return Float32Array, update the cctEmbeddingCache Map type, remove Array.from() calls. ~10 type signature changes, no behavioral change. Key files: src/memory/embeddings/nomic.ts (lines 167, 205), src/memory/storage/sqlite/cache.ts (line 53), src/memory/search/vector.ts (cctEmbeddingCache type).

title: Add init command with AGENTS.md injection (like beads)
description:
## Updated Approach: Init Command with Instruction Injection

Based on beads framework analysis, learning-agent should implement a similar pattern:

### Reference: How Beads Does It

Beads uses `bd init` which:
1. Calls `addLandingThePlaneInstructions()` to inject into AGENTS.md
2. Creates `.claude/settings.local.json` with onboarding prompt
3. Is idempotent (checks for existing section before appending)
4. Has `--stealth` flag to skip injection

Key files in beads:
- `/cmd/bd/init.go` - Main orchestration
- `/cmd/bd/init_agent.go` - Contains injection logic and templates

### What Learning-Agent Should Implement

Create `npx learning-agent init` command that:

1. **Creates directory structure**:
   ```
   .claude/
   └── lessons/
       └── index.jsonl (empty or with welcome lesson)
   ```

2. **Injects into AGENTS.md** (create if missing):
   - "Learning Agent Integration" section
   - Session-start retrieval instructions
   - Plan-time retrieval instructions
   - Lesson proposal flow with quality gate
   - Confirmation UX format
   - CLI quick reference

3. **Optionally updates .claude/settings.local.json**:
   - Add learning-agent hooks configuration

4. **Flags**:
   - `--skip-agents` - Skip AGENTS.md modification
   - `--quiet` - Minimal output

### Template Content for AGENTS.md

```markdown
## Learning Agent Integration

This project uses learning-agent for session memory.

### Retrieval Points
- **Session start**: High-severity lessons loaded automatically
- **Plan creation**: Semantic search for relevant lessons

### Proposing Lessons

Propose when: user correction, self-correction, test failure fix, or manual request.

**Quality gate (ALL must pass):**
- Novel (not already stored)
- Specific (clear guidance)
- Actionable (obvious what to do)

**Confirmation format:**
```
Learned: [insight]. Save? [y/n]
```

### CLI Commands
```bash
npx learning-agent load-session --json  # Session start
npx learning-agent check-plan --json    # Plan-time
npx learning-agent capture --trigger "..." --insight "..." --yes
```

See AGENTS.md in learning-agent repo for full documentation.
```

### Idempotency

Like beads, check for existing "Learning Agent Integration" section before appending. This allows safe re-running of init.

### Related Issues
- This replaces the original approach of manually editing CLAUDE.md
- Aligns with beads pattern for better user experience
close_reason: Implemented init command with AGENTS.md injection following beads pattern

title: P1: Harden session completion protocol in all templates
description:
PROBLEM: The session completion protocol (6-step git checklist from CLAUDE.md) was not followed consistently. The lfg.md template doesn't explicitly include it.

FIELD EVIDENCE (session 37b79df7):
- Git commit and push happened (good)
- But quality gates (pnpm test && pnpm lint) not documented as final run
- bd sync timing unclear
- No ca verify-gates before closing epic

FIX:
1. Add to lfg.md at the very end (after Phase 5):
   ```
   ## SESSION CLOSE — INVIOLABLE
   DO NOT say 'done' or 'complete' until ALL of these pass:
   1. ca verify-gates <epic-id>    # All workflow gates satisfied
   2. pnpm test && pnpm lint       # Quality gates green
   3. git status                   # Review changes
   4. git add <specific-files>     # Stage (never git add .)
   5. bd sync                      # Sync beads
   6. git commit -m '...'          # Commit
   7. bd sync                      # Post-commit sync
   8. git push                     # Push to remote
   If ANY step fails, FIX IT before proceeding. Work is NOT done until git push succeeds.
   ```
2. Add same close protocol to work.md and review.md (not just lfg.md)
3. Consider: add a 'ca session-check' command that verifies all of the above

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- lfg.md ends with explicit session close protocol
- Protocol includes ca verify-gates check
- All workflow templates reference the close protocol
- Steps are numbered and marked INVIOLABLE
close_reason: SESSION CLOSE -- INVIOLABLE 8-step checklist added to lfg.md end.

description:
# README Update for v0.2.4

The README needs comprehensive updates to reflect the v0.2.4 hybrid memory system redesign.

## Key Changes to Document

### 1. New Setup Flow
```bash
# Install
pnpm add -D learning-agent

# One-shot setup (MCP + hooks + model)
lna setup

# Done! Claude now has lesson_search and lesson_capture tools
```

### 2. How It Works Section

Explain the hybrid approach:
- **Prime injection** at session start teaches Claude HOW to use lessons
- **MCP tools** let Claude search and capture lessons natively
- **Commit reminder** prompts lesson capture before commits

### 3. Tool Documentation

Document the MCP tools:
- `lesson_search` - When to use, parameters, example output
- `lesson_capture` - When to use, parameters, what gets saved

### 4. CLI Commands

Update command reference:
- `lna setup` - One-shot configuration
- `lna prime` - Manual prime context output
- `lna search` - CLI version of lesson search
- `lna learn` - CLI version of lesson capture
- `lna list` - List recent lessons
- `lna remind-capture` - Manual capture reminder

### 5. Remove Outdated Content

- Remove references to `load-session` (replaced by `prime`)
- Remove references to `check-plan` (removed)
- Update hook documentation
- Remove manual hook configuration (setup does it)

### 6. Architecture Diagram

Update to show:
```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                    Claude Code Session                       │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│                                                              │
│  SessionStart ──► lna prime ──► Guidelines + Top Lessons    │
│                                                              │
│  During Work:                                                │
│    ├── lesson_search tool ◄── Claude calls when needed      │
│    └── lesson_capture tool ◄── Claude saves lessons         │
│                                                              │
│  PreCommit ──► lna remind-capture ──► Capture reminder      │
│                                                              │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Setup section shows one-shot command
- [ ] Tool usage documented with examples
- [ ] CLI commands documented
- [ ] Architecture diagram updated
- [ ] Old hook names removed
- [ ] Clear explanation of hybrid push-pull model
