title: Fix type casting: replace unsafe 'as Lesson' casts with proper MemoryItem types
description:
## Problem
Widespread use of `as Lesson` casts across search, retrieval, and MCP logic. This causes runtime objects to mismatch their declared types and hides type-specific fields (e.g., pattern data on pattern items, or solution-specific fields).

## Files to Change (9 casts across 5 files, minus 4 in search.ts handled by SQLite fix)
- src/memory/search/vector.ts:96 — `item as Lesson` → use MemoryItem type, rename field from `lesson` to `item` in ScoredLesson/ScoredMemoryItem
- src/memory/retrieval/session.ts:48 — `as LessonWithSeverity[]` → use proper type guard narrowing
- src/mcp.ts:161 — `item as Lesson` backward compat return → handle properly
- src/memory/storage/compact.ts:239 — `as unknown as Lesson` double cast → use Zod parse/safeParse

## Also
- Rename ScoredLesson → ScoredMemoryItem in vector.ts (or keep alias)
- Rename RankedLesson → RankedMemoryItem in ranking.ts (or keep alias)
- Ensure downstream consumers handle MemoryItem union correctly

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Zero `as Lesson` casts in non-test code (except explicit backward-compat wrappers)
- [ ] All types are properly narrowed via Zod parsing or type guards
- [ ] No new TypeScript errors
- [ ] All existing tests pass + updated type tests
close_reason: Closed

title: Epic: Fix .ca-* state files in worktree context
description: Phase state files (.ca-phase-state.json, .ca-failure-state.json) are misrouted when worktrees are used as additional directories instead of primary working directories. All hooks and phase-check CLI resolve paths via bare process.cwd(), which points to the main repo when the worktree is an additional dir. This causes: (1) state pollution in the main repo, (2) stale files on interrupted LFG sessions, (3) cross-epic conflicts with multiple worktrees. Root cause: Claude Code hooks always run with cwd=primary dir. Hooks from additional dirs' settings.json are never loaded. Fix approach: (a) Unify path resolution via getRepoRoot() for COMPOUND_AGENT_ROOT support, (b) Add 72h TTL to phase state, (c) Improve ca worktree create UX with clear next-step command, (d) Update documentation. Confirmed by independent Opus and Sonnet architectural reviews. Blocks v1.3.7 release.
close_reason: Closed

title: Remove check-plan hook and update documentation
description:
# Overview

Remove the plan-time hook (`check-plan`) as part of the v0.2.4 redesign. This hook is being replaced by the tool-based `lesson_search` approach.

## Why Remove

The check-plan hook auto-injected lessons at plan creation time. Problems:
1. Claude didn't trust auto-injected content as much as tool results
2. Injected lessons often weren't relevant to the specific plan
3. Added context noise without clear benefit
4. No agency - Claude was passive recipient

## Replacement

Instead of auto-injection at plan time:
- **MCP tool** `lesson_search` with "Mandatory recall" language
- **Prime injection** tells Claude to search BEFORE architectural decisions
- Claude has agency to search when needed, builds ownership

## Changes Required

### 1. Remove check-plan command
- Delete `src/commands/check-plan.ts` (or equivalent)
- Remove from CLI registration
- Remove from exports

### 2. Update setup command
- Don't register any plan-time hooks
- Only register: SessionStart, PreCompact, PreCommit

### 3. Update documentation
- Remove references to check-plan in README
- Remove from SPEC.md retrieval strategy section
- Update CLAUDE.md if it mentions plan-time retrieval

### 4. Migration
- Existing users with check-plan hook: `lna setup` will overwrite
- Add note in changelog about removal

## Files to Modify

- `src/commands/` - Remove check-plan
- `src/cli.ts` - Remove registration
- `README.md` - Update hook documentation
- `doc/SPEC.md` - Update retrieval strategy section
- `CHANGELOG.md` - Document removal

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] check-plan command removed from codebase
- [ ] No plan-time hooks in setup configuration
- [ ] Documentation updated to reflect new approach
- [ ] Migration path clear for existing users
close_reason: Replaced check-plan hook with MCP tool approach, updated AGENTS.md template and slash commands

title: Add findSimilarLessons() function
description:
Create a new exported function in src/memory/search/vector.ts that finds existing lessons semantically similar to a given text using embedding cosine similarity.

## What to build

A function `findSimilarLessons(repoRoot, text, options?)` that:
1. Checks `isModelAvailable()` from `src/memory/embeddings/model.ts` — returns `[]` if model not present (graceful degradation, no error)
2. Reads all items via `readMemoryItems(repoRoot)` from `src/memory/storage/jsonl.ts`
3. Embeds query text via `embedText(text)` from `src/memory/embeddings/nomic.ts`
4. For each non-invalidated item (skip if `item.invalidatedAt` is set), excluding `options.excludeId`:
   - Build text: `item.insight` ONLY (NOT trigger + insight — generic triggers like 'Manual capture' add noise and cause false positives)
   - Compute hash via `contentHash(item.trigger, item.insight)` from cache.ts (keep full hash for cache compat)
   - Try `getCachedEmbedding(repoRoot, item.id, hash)` — if miss, compute via `embedText(item.insight)` and cache
   - Compute `cosineSimilarity(queryVector, itemVector)` (already in same file, line 30)
5. Collect items with score >= threshold (default 0.80), sort descending
6. Does NOT include CCT patterns (unlike `searchVector()`)

## IMPORTANT: insight-only similarity

Use `item.insight` for embedding, NOT `${item.trigger} ${item.insight}`. Most lessons have generic triggers like 'Manual capture' which would inflate similarity scores between unrelated lessons. The insight is the actual knowledge content.

The query text parameter should also be insight-only when called from handleLearn and clean-lessons.

## Interface

```typescript
export interface SimilarLesson {
  item: MemoryItem;
  score: number;
}

export interface FindSimilarOptions {
  threshold?: number;   // Default: 0.80
  excludeId?: string;   // Skip this lesson ID
}

export async function findSimilarLessons(
  repoRoot: string,
  text: string,
  options?: FindSimilarOptions
): Promise<SimilarLesson[]>
```

## Pattern to follow

Reuse the embed-and-cache loop pattern from `searchVector()` in the same file (lines 118-141). Key differences: no CCT patterns, no limit, threshold-based filtering, insight-only text.

## Barrel exports to update

Add `findSimilarLessons`, `SimilarLesson`, `FindSimilarOptions` to `src/memory/search/index.ts` (currently lines 12-13 export vector.ts items).

## Tests (TDD — write first)

File: `src/memory/search/vector.test.ts` (or new `src/memory/search/find-similar.test.ts`)
- Returns `[]` when no items exist
- Returns `[]` when model unavailable (mock `isModelAvailable` to return false)
- Finds items above threshold (create two lessons with near-identical insight text)
- Excludes the specified `excludeId`
- Skips invalidated items (item with `invalidatedAt` set)
- Respects custom threshold
- Results sorted by score descending
- Does NOT match lessons with same trigger but different insights (validates insight-only comparison)

Use the project's established pattern for embedding-dependent tests. NEVER call `isModelUsable()` at module top-level (causes 150MB native memory leak in tests — see lesson L5db4de96).
close_reason: findSimilarLessons() added to vector.ts with 7 unit tests, insight-only embedding, barrel exports updated

title: Research: AgentTeam vs subagents — confirmed gap and workaround
description:
## Research Findings (completed)

Three research agents investigated how Claude Code handles AgentTeam vs subagents.

### Key Finding
.claude/agents/*.md files work for subagents (Task without team_name) but NOT for agent teams (TeamCreate + Task with team_name). This is confirmed by GitHub issue #24316 (13 upvotes, no Anthropic response).

### Workaround Chosen
Move agent role definitions to .claude/skills/compound/agents/<name>/SKILL.md. Skills are automatically available to all Claude Code sessions including teammates. See learning_agent-bqfj for implementation.

### Other Approaches Considered
- A. Lead reads agent files at spawn time — fragile
- B. Embed role prompts in commands — bloats commands, duplicates content
- C. Orchestration skill (compound-engineering pattern) — useful but doesn't solve root cause
- D. Wait for #24316 — no timeline

### Sources
- https://code.claude.com/docs/en/agent-teams
- https://code.claude.com/docs/en/sub-agents
- https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code/issues/24316
- https://github.com/EveryInc/compound-engineering-plugin
- https://gist.github.com/kieranklaassen/4f2aba89594a4aea4ad64d753984b2ea

This task is informational — close immediately.
close_reason: Research completed, findings captured in description

title: E5b: Core Embedding Provider Swap (node-llama-cpp → Transformers.js)
description:
## Scope
Replace the embedding runtime from node-llama-cpp (C++ native addon, 431MB RSS) to @huggingface/transformers + onnxruntime-node (ONNX, 23MB RSS). Public API (embedText/embedTexts) unchanged.

### What's IN scope
- Rewrite nomic.ts: Transformers.js pipeline('feature-extraction', 'nomic-ai/nomic-embed-text-v1.5', {dtype:'q8'}) with singleton pattern
- Rewrite model.ts: ONNX model resolution/download via Transformers.js auto-download
- Update model-info.ts: MODEL_URI, MODEL_FILENAME, DEFAULT_MODEL_DIR, isModelAvailable for HuggingFace cache
- Update index.ts barrel: new EmbeddingContext type replacing LlamaEmbeddingContext
- Cache invalidation: bump SCHEMA_VERSION 5→6 and KNOWLEDGE_SCHEMA_VERSION 2→3
- Defense layer 2: add model ID to contentHash() in cache.ts
- Fix direct imports: route embed-chunks.ts and knowledge/search.ts through barrel
- Update download-model.ts to download ONNX model
- Swap package.json deps: remove node-llama-cpp, add @huggingface/transformers
- Rewrite 5 embedding test files + update check-model-info-rss.mjs

### What's OUT of scope
- Consumer code changes (vector.ts, prewarm.ts — no changes needed)
- Doctor/primitives/native-diagnostic string cleanup (Epic 2)
- Vitest pool config changes (Epic 2)
- Documentation/CHANGELOG (Epic 2)

## EARS Requirements
- E1: System SHALL use @huggingface/transformers with onnxruntime-node + nomic-embed-text-v1.5
- E2: embedText/embedTexts SHALL return 768-dim Float32Array (same public API)
- E3: RSS delta SHALL be < 50MB when model loaded
- S1: When ONNX model not downloaded, SHALL auto-download from HuggingFace Hub
- S2: When cached embeddings from old provider exist, SHALL invalidate and re-embed
- E4: download-model CLI SHALL pre-download ONNX model for offline use
- E5: unloadEmbeddingResources() SHALL dispose pipeline and release memory
- O1: isModelAvailable() SHALL detect ONNX model without native imports (zero-native contract)
- U1: If model download fails, SHALL report clear error (no silent fallback)
- U2: If embedding fails, SHALL propagate error (no empty vectors)

## Interface Contracts
- embedText(text: string): Promise<Float32Array> — UNCHANGED
- embedTexts(texts: string[]): Promise<Float32Array[]> — UNCHANGED
- getEmbedding(): Promise<EmbeddingContext> — return type changes
- unloadEmbedding/unloadEmbeddingResources — simpler dispose (single pipeline.dispose())
- withEmbedding<T> — UNCHANGED
- isModelAvailable() — checks HuggingFace cache path
- Implicit: singleton mutex (pendingInit), lazy init, retry-on-failure — all preserved

## Key Assumptions
- nomic-embed-text-v1.5 produces 768 dims (validated in E4 spike)
- Transformers.js pipeline has dispose() method (validated in E4 spike)
- @huggingface/transformers does NOT require native compilation
- Schema version bump auto-deletes and rebuilds SQLite caches

## Verification Gate
- pnpm test:fast passes
- embedText('hello') returns Float32Array[768]
- RSS < 50MB
- check-model-info-rss.mjs passes

## Scenarios
| ID | Category | Trigger | Expected |
|----|----------|---------|----------|
| S1 | Happy | embedText("hello") | Float32Array[768] |
| S2 | Happy | embedTexts(["a","b"]) | Float32Array[][768] |
| S3 | Happy | embedText, model not downloaded | Auto-downloads, returns vector |
| S4 | Happy | ca download-model | Pre-downloads ONNX model |
| S5 | Boundary | Measure RSS with model loaded | < 50MB delta |
| S6 | Happy | Search with old cached vectors | Invalidates, re-embeds |
| S7 | Happy | unloadEmbeddingResources() | Pipeline disposed |
| S8 | Boundary | isModelAvailable(), model present | true, zero native imports |
| S9 | Boundary | isModelAvailable(), model absent | false, zero native imports |
| S10 | Error | embedText, no network, no model | Clear error |
| S11 | Error | embedText, corrupted model | Error propagated |
| S12 | Boundary | unloadEmbeddingResources(), not loaded | No-op |
| S13 | Happy | Concurrent embedText() calls | Single init (mutex) |
notes: Phase: WORK COMPLETE | Gate 3 passed | T1: core swap committed (4d80882) | T2: CLI+imports committed (c9dacb1) | Next: REVIEW
close_reason: Closed

title: Remove redundant rebuildIndex in isNovel
description:
**Problem**: `isNovel()` and CLI `search` rebuild the entire SQLite index on every call.

**Impact**: Performance bottleneck. O(N) writes for every read.

**Fix**: Implement smart sync/staleness check.
1. Store `last_synced_mtime` in a metadata table or file.
2. Compare with `fs.stat(JSONL_PATH).mtime`.
3. Only `rebuildIndex` if JSONL is newer than the DB sync time.
4. Also allow explicit force rebuild via CLI flag.

**Files**: `src/storage/sqlite.ts`, `src/capture/quality.ts`, `src/cli.ts`
close_reason: Implemented smart sync with mtime tracking - rebuildIndex now only runs when JSONL changes

title: Clean zombie/legacy test code from Epic 2 migration
description:
# Test Suite Cleanup: Zombie and Legacy Code

## Problem
After Epic 2 (unified memory schema with 4 item types), several test files contain zombie references to the old type system. These create false confidence — tests pass due to loose constraints but test semantically invalid data.

## Scope: 3 files, 3 categories

### Category 1: Invalid pattern field types (test-utils.test.ts)
Lines 111, 147: Tests pass `pattern: 'anti-pattern'` and `pattern: 'best-practice'` (strings) when the schema requires `{ bad: string, good: string }` objects. Tests pass only because `Partial<Lesson>` doesn't enforce Zod validation at runtime.

**Fix**: Replace string patterns with valid `{ bad, good }` objects. Update assertions accordingly.

### Category 2: Zombie SQLite INSERTs using deprecated 'quick' type (sqlite.test.ts)
Lines 62, 81, 154, 171, 194, 499: Raw SQL inserts hardcode `type='quick'` which is no longer a valid MemoryItemType. Tests pass because SQLite TEXT column has no CHECK constraint.

**Fix**: Change all `'quick'` → `'lesson'` in raw SQL INSERT statements. These are NOT backward-compat tests — they're test setup code that should use current types.

### Category 3: Outdated test names referencing old quick/full distinction (sqlite.test.ts)
Lines 440, 456, 472: Test descriptions say "quick lesson" and "full lesson" but the quick/full distinction was removed in Epic 2. All items are now type='lesson'.

**Fix**: Rename to describe the actual behavior being tested (e.g., "preserves evidence/severity when optional fields are set").

## Explicitly NOT in scope
- types.test.ts backward-compat tests (LegacyLessonSchema) — these are correct
- jsonl.test.ts legacy migration tests — these are correct
- createQuickLesson/createFullLesson helpers — still valid API, just poor naming (rename is separate task)

## Plan

1. Fix test-utils.test.ts:111 — change `pattern: 'anti-pattern'` to `pattern: { bad: 'old way', good: 'new way' }`, update assertion
2. Fix test-utils.test.ts:147 — change `pattern: 'best-practice'` to `pattern: { bad: 'old way', good: 'new way' }`, update assertion to use toEqual
3. Fix sqlite.test.ts:62,81,154,171,194,499 — change 'quick' → 'lesson' in all 6 raw SQL INSERTs
4. Fix sqlite.test.ts:440 — rename "preserves evidence/severity on quick lesson" → "preserves evidence/severity when optional fields are set"
5. Fix sqlite.test.ts:456 — rename "returns undefined for missing evidence/severity on full lesson" → "returns undefined when evidence/severity not provided"
6. Fix sqlite.test.ts:472 — rename "preserves evidence/severity exactly on full lesson" → "preserves evidence/severity values exactly"
7. Run pnpm test:fast to verify all tests still pass
close_reason: Closed

title: Epic: Two-scope logging for infinity loop (micro observability)
description:
## Problem

The infinity loop currently operates as a black box. Users can only see macro-level events (epic start/complete/fail/skip/retry) in the bash loop output. There is no visibility into what the active Claude session is doing moment-to-moment — which tools it calls, what files it reads/writes, how many tokens each turn costs, or where it is in the workflow.

## Solution

Add a second 'micro' scope of logging alongside the existing 'macro' scope, using Claude Code's stream-json output format (available in headless -p mode). This gives structured, real-time event data without any additional hooks or infrastructure.

### Scope 1: Macro (existing, unchanged)
- Epic-level events: start, complete, fail, skip, retry
- Summary stats (completed/failed/skipped counts)
- Written to agent_logs/loop_<epic>-<ts>.log

### Scope 2: Micro (new)
- Full structured JSON stream: tool calls, prompts, token counts, partial messages
- Written to agent_logs/trace_<epic>-<ts>.jsonl (one JSON event per line)
- Observable in real-time via 'ca watch' command in a second terminal
- Only active during loop execution (not normal interactive sessions)

### Implementation approach
1. Modify loop.ts script generation to use --output-format stream-json --include-partial-messages
2. Add an inline bash splitter that tees the stream: raw JSONL to trace file, text extraction for marker detection (EPIC_COMPLETE etc.) to the macro log
3. Create new watch.ts command that tails the latest trace JSONL and pretty-prints it with colors
4. Update loop.test.ts for the new script shape; add watch.test.ts
5. Update changelog

### What ca watch looks like
```
$ ca watch
[14:30:45] EPIC learning_agent-abc started
[14:30:46] PROMPT  'Load context...'  (142 tokens)
[14:30:48] TOOL    Bash -> npx ca load-session          OK 1.2s
[14:30:50] TOOL    Read -> docs/SPEC.md                 OK 0.3s
[14:31:08] TOOL    Bash -> pnpm test:fast                FAIL 2.1s
[14:31:20] TOKENS  Turn 4: 12,340 in / 3,210 out
[14:45:02] MARKER  EPIC_COMPLETE
```
close_reason: All tasks complete: TDD tests, loop.ts stream-json, watch command, changelog. 75 tests passing.

title: External reviewer: Gemini CLI headless integration
description:
## Summary

Add Gemini CLI as an optional external reviewer in the compound-agent review pipeline. When enabled via config, a dedicated agent template calls gemini in headless mode after /implementation-reviewer approves, providing a cross-model review perspective.

## Motivation

Different LLMs have different blind spots. A Gemini review after Claude's internal pipeline catches issues that Claude might miss -- security patterns, edge cases, alternative approaches. Cross-model review is a genuine quality multiplier.

## Design

### Config flag

Add externalReviewers to plugin.json or a new .compound-agent.json:

  { "externalReviewers": ["gemini"] }

### Agent template

New file: .claude/agents/compound/external-reviewer-gemini.md

The agent should:
1. Check command -v gemini is available (graceful skip if not)
2. Read the current beads issue context (bd show <id>)
3. Build a context-rich prompt: beads issue description + git diff of session changes
4. Call gemini in headless mode:
   git diff HEAD~N | gemini -p "<review prompt with beads context>" --output-format json
5. Parse the JSON response (.response field)
6. Present findings as actionable items to the user

### Integration point

- Runs AFTER /implementation-reviewer returns APPROVED
- Part of the review process, not a gate (advisory, not blocking)
- Triggered by the /work or /review workflow when gemini is in externalReviewers config

### Gemini headless specifics

- Use --output-format json for structured parsing
- Use -p flag for non-interactive mode
- Can use --model flag to select specific Gemini model
- JSON response schema: { "response": "string", "stats": {...}, "error": {...} }

### Review prompt template

Should include:
- Beads issue title + description (the WHY)
- Git diff (the WHAT)
- Instruction to focus on: correctness, security, missed edge cases, code quality
- Instruction to be concise and actionable (no fluff)
- Instruction to output structured findings (numbered list)

### Setup / activation

- npx ca config set externalReviewers gemini (or manual edit)
- npx ca setup --update deploys the new agent template
- Status check: npx ca config get externalReviewers

### Error handling

- Tool not installed: skip gracefully, log warning
- Tool not authenticated: skip gracefully, log warning
- API error / timeout: skip gracefully, log warning
- Never block the pipeline on external reviewer failure

## Files to modify

- src/setup/templates/agents-phase11.ts -- add external-reviewer-gemini.md template
- src/setup/templates/index.ts -- export new template
- Config reading logic (new or existing module)
- Workflow templates that orchestrate the review phase

## Testing

- Unit test: agent template exists and has correct structure
- Unit test: config flag parsing
- Integration test: graceful skip when gemini not available
- Integration test: review prompt includes beads context + diff
close_reason: Closed

title: Reduce lint noise: scope rules to prod files and ratchet toward --max-warnings=0
description:
## Context
ESLint reports 190 warnings, making the signal too noisy to be actionable. Top offenders:
- max-lines-per-function: 104 warnings (almost all in test files)
- enforce-barrel-exports: 41 warnings (many in test files importing internals)
- @typescript-eslint/no-unused-vars: 22
- @typescript-eslint/no-explicit-any: 12
- max-lines: 9

## Fix
1. Scope `max-lines-per-function` and `max-lines` to non-test files only (tests legitimately have long describe blocks)
2. Scope `enforce-barrel-exports` to non-test files (tests need to import internal modules for unit testing)
3. Scope `explicit-module-boundary-types` to non-test files (already done)
4. Add `--max-warnings` target to lint script for ratcheting
5. Set initial target: `--max-warnings=50` (production-only warnings), ratchet down over time

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Test files excluded from max-lines-per-function, max-lines, enforce-barrel-exports
- [ ] Warning count drops below 50
- [ ] `pnpm lint` script includes `--max-warnings=N` enforcement

title: Apply ranking in MCP lesson_search
description: CRITICAL BUG: src/mcp.ts:131-141 and 165-187. MCP lesson_search calls searchVector() directly returning raw cosine similarity. A recent high-severity confirmed lesson can appear below an older unconfirmed one. Fix: Apply rankLessons() to search results before returning. ~3 lines in both registerTool callback and toolHandlers duplicate.

title: Fix CLI instructions across all templates: replace MCP tool names with CLI commands
description:
## Problem
Templates reference memory_capture and memory_search (MCP tool names) 147 times across 13 files. Claude doesn't reliably know how to invoke these — evidence shows Claude trying 'ca learn' (fails, not in PATH) then falling back to 'npx compound-agent learn' (works but not intuitive).

MCP server may be dropped in v1.3.0. All instructions should use CLI commands that work reliably.

## Evidence from user's compound phase
Claude tried: ca learn '...' → Error: command not found
Then tried: npx compound-agent learn '...' → worked
The templates say 'memory_capture' which is an MCP tool name, not a CLI command.

## CLI Command Mapping
MCP tool → CLI equivalent:
- memory_capture → npx ca learn '<insight>' (or: npx compound-agent learn '<insight>')
- memory_search → npx ca search '<query>' (or: npx compound-agent search '<query>')
- memory_search (load session) → npx ca load-session
- memory_capture with type → npx ca learn --type=<lesson|solution|pattern|preference> '<insight>'

Note: 'npx ca' works because package.json has bin.ca = './dist/cli.js'

## Scope: 147 replacements across 13 files

### Template files (source of truth — these generate the installed files):
1. src/setup/templates/commands.ts — 20 occurrences across all 5 phase commands
2. src/setup/templates/skills.ts — 19 occurrences across all 5 phase skills
3. src/setup/templates/agents-workflow.ts — 8 occurrences (test-writer, implementer agents)
4. src/setup/templates/agents-review.ts — 3 occurrences (review agents)
5. src/setup/templates/agents-phase11.ts — 7 occurrences (phase 11 agents)

### Test files (assertions that check for these strings):
6. src/setup/templates/work-phase.test.ts — 22 occurrences
7. src/setup/templates/review-phase.test.ts — 14 occurrences
8. src/setup/templates/compound-phase.test.ts — 22 occurrences
9. src/setup/templates/brainstorm-phase.test.ts — 10 occurrences
10. src/setup/templates/plan-phase.test.ts — 9 occurrences
11. src/setup/templates/lfg-phase.test.ts — 8 occurrences
12. src/setup/templates/commands.test.ts — 3 occurrences
13. src/setup/templates/skills.test.ts — 2 occurrences

## Replacement Strategy
For each template:
- Replace 'memory_search' context instructions with: 'npx ca search <query>' (backtick-wrapped)
- Replace 'memory_capture' instructions with: 'npx ca learn <insight>' (backtick-wrapped)
- Add note where relevant: 'npx ca learn --type=solution' for solutions, '--severity=high' for critical lessons
- Keep the WHEN to use (before implementation, after corrections) — just change the HOW

For each test:
- Update assertions to check for 'npx ca search' or 'npx ca learn' instead of 'memory_search'/'memory_capture'

## TDD Approach
1. Update test files first (change expected strings)
2. Verify tests fail
3. Update template files (bulk replace + review context)
4. Verify tests pass
5. pnpm test && pnpm lint

## Important: Do NOT change
- The actual MCP server code (src/mcp/) — that's a separate concern for v1.3.0
- The CLI implementation (src/cli/) — already works correctly
- Any non-template files outside src/setup/templates/

title: P1: Dual embedding cache — eliminate searchVector/findSimilarLessons cache conflict
description:
## Problem

searchVector() caches embeddings under contentHash(trigger, insight) (vector.ts:125), while findSimilarLessons() caches under contentHash(insight, '') (vector.ts:209). Both write to the SAME single embedding+content_hash columns in the lessons table (schema.ts:36-37, cache.ts:82). This means the two access patterns continuously overwrite each other's cache, causing O(n) re-embedding on every alternating access (search → clean-lessons → search cycle).

## Root Cause

SQLite lessons table has only one cache slot per lesson:
- `embedding BLOB` (line 36)
- `content_hash TEXT` (line 37)

setCachedEmbedding() does: `UPDATE lessons SET embedding = ?, content_hash = ? WHERE id = ?` (cache.ts:82)

## Solution

Add a second pair of columns for insight-only embeddings:
1. Add `embedding_insight BLOB` and `content_hash_insight TEXT` to schema
2. Bump SCHEMA_VERSION from 3 to 4 (triggers auto-rebuild per connection.ts)
3. Add `getCachedInsightEmbedding()` / `setCachedInsightEmbedding()` in cache.ts
4. Update findSimilarLessons() to use the new insight-specific cache functions
5. Update collectCachedEmbeddings() to preserve both cache slots during rebuild
6. Update CachedEmbeddingData type if needed

## Key Files
- src/memory/storage/sqlite/schema.ts (SCHEMA_VERSION, SCHEMA_SQL)
- src/memory/storage/sqlite/cache.ts (get/set functions, collectCachedEmbeddings)
- src/memory/storage/sqlite/types.ts (CachedEmbeddingData)
- src/memory/search/vector.ts (findSimilarLessons cache calls)
- src/memory/storage/index.ts (barrel exports)

## Tests (TDD)
- Verify searchVector cache is not invalidated after findSimilarLessons runs
- Verify findSimilarLessons cache is not invalidated after searchVector runs
- Verify both caches survive collectCachedEmbeddings round-trip
- Verify schema migration (version bump) triggers rebuild

title: P3: Add clearCctEmbeddingCache() to findSimilarLessons test afterEach
description:
## Problem

In vector.test.ts, the searchVector describe block's afterEach calls clearCctEmbeddingCache() to prevent test state leakage. The findSimilarLessons describe block (lines 451-462) does NOT — its afterEach only calls closeDb(), rm(tempDir), and vi.restoreAllMocks(). While findSimilarLessons doesn't use CCT patterns directly, shared module state could leak if test ordering changes.

## Fix

Add clearCctEmbeddingCache() to the afterEach block of the findSimilarLessons describe block in vector.test.ts (around line 459), matching the pattern used by the searchVector block.

## Key Files
- src/memory/search/vector.test.ts:458-462 (afterEach block to update)

title: P3: Replace fragile call-count mock with content-based mock in threshold test
P3: Add boundary test for exactly 0.98 cosine similarity in isNovel
description:
## Problem

The novelty gate uses DUPLICATE_THRESHOLD = 0.98 with >= comparison (quality.ts:17, quality.ts:49). There is no test that verifies the exact boundary behavior: a lesson with exactly 0.98 similarity should be flagged as non-novel, while 0.979 should pass as novel. This is a critical boundary for the capture gate.

## Fix

Add two tests to quality.test.ts:
1. `isNovel returns novel: false when similarity is exactly 0.98` — mock findSimilarLessons to return a result with score 0.98
2. `isNovel returns novel: true when similarity is 0.979` — mock findSimilarLessons to return empty (nothing above threshold)

## Key Files
- src/memory/capture/quality.test.ts (add boundary tests after existing novelty tests)

title: P3: Fix unconditional ellipsis in capture similarity warning
description:
## Problem

capture.ts:222 unconditionally appends '...' to insight text:
```typescript
console.log(`  ${chalk.default.dim(s.item.id)} (${(s.score * 100).toFixed(0)}%) ${s.item.insight.slice(0, 60)}...`);
```

This appends '...' even when the insight is shorter than 60 characters (no truncation occurred), e.g.:
- 'Always use Polars' (17 chars) → 'Always use Polars...' (implies truncation when none happened)

## Fix

Conditionally append '...' only when truncation actually occurs:
```typescript
const truncated = s.item.insight.length > 60;
const display = s.item.insight.slice(0, 60) + (truncated ? '...' : '');
```

## Key Files
- src/commands/capture.ts:222 (truncation line)

## Tests
- Update capture-similarity.test.ts if it asserts on the exact output format

title: P3: Add debug logging to silent catch blocks in isNovel and checkSimilarityPostCapture
description:
## Problem

Two catch blocks silently swallow ALL errors with no observability:
1. quality.ts:59-61 — isNovel() catch returns { novel: true } on any error
2. capture.ts:230 — checkSimilarityPostCapture() outer catch is empty

While fail-open is the correct design (never block capture), there is zero observability into silent failures. If the embedding model is broken, sync fails, or the DB is corrupt, users have no way to know similarity checking is disabled.

## Solution

Add debug-level logging to both catch blocks using the project's existing logging pattern. Check what logging utility the project uses (console.debug, a custom logger, or environment-gated logging). The logs should be:
1. Only visible in verbose/debug mode (not in normal output)
2. Include the error message for diagnosis
3. Clearly state that similarity checking was skipped

Example:
```typescript
catch (error) {
  // Debug-level: visible only in verbose mode
  if (process.env.DEBUG || process.env.VERBOSE) {
    console.error('[debug] Similarity check skipped:', (error as Error).message);
  }
  return { novel: true };
}
```

## Key Files
- src/memory/capture/quality.ts:59-61 (isNovel catch)
- src/commands/capture.ts:230 (checkSimilarityPostCapture catch)

## Acceptance Criteria
- Normal output unchanged (no new messages in default mode)
- Debug/verbose mode shows diagnostic messages
- Error message included in log for troubleshooting

title: P1: Install lessons-reviewer agent template for end users
description:
## Problem

clean-lessons.ts:78-80 tells users to spawn /lessons-reviewer, but the agent only exists in this repo's .claude/agents/lessons-reviewer/AGENT.md. The setup pipeline (ca init / ca setup) installs templates into .claude/agents/compound/ only, via AGENT_TEMPLATES in src/setup/templates/agents.ts. End users who run `ca init` in their project will NOT get the lessons-reviewer agent, making the clean-lessons workflow incomplete.

## Solution

1. Create src/setup/templates/agents-lessons.ts with LESSONS_AGENT_TEMPLATES containing the lessons-reviewer template
2. Import and spread into AGENT_TEMPLATES in agents.ts (following the existing pattern: REVIEW_AGENT_TEMPLATES, PHASE11_AGENT_TEMPLATES, etc.)
3. Create a corresponding role skill template in src/setup/templates/agent-role-skills.ts for the lessons-reviewer
4. The template should be a thin wrapper referencing the role skill (matches the pattern of other agents like repo-analyst.md)

## Template Pattern (from existing agents)
```yaml
---
name: Lessons Reviewer
description: Reviews flagged lesson pairs for duplicates, refinements, and contradictions
model: sonnet
---
```
Body should reference the lessons-reviewer role skill.

## Key Files
- src/setup/templates/agents-lessons.ts (NEW)
- src/setup/templates/agents.ts (add import + spread)
- src/setup/templates/agent-role-skills.ts (add lessons-reviewer skill)
- .claude/agents/lessons-reviewer/AGENT.md (reference for content)
- src/setup/primitives.ts (no changes needed — installAgentTemplates auto-picks up new entries)

## Tests
- Verify AGENT_TEMPLATES includes 'lessons-reviewer.md' key
- Verify installAgentTemplates creates the file in .claude/agents/compound/
- Verify template content matches expected frontmatter (name, model, tools)

title: P1: Suppress noisy native model errors in learn post-capture warning
description:
## Problem

When `ca learn` runs checkSimilarityPostCapture() (capture.ts:206-233) and node-llama-cpp encounters a backend error, native error messages print to stderr BEFORE the outer try/catch can handle them. The command succeeds silently, but users see confusing native error output that obscures whether the similarity check actually worked.

Current code (capture.ts:208-230):
- isModelAvailable() check at line 212 — only checks file existence, not runtime usability
- embedText/findSimilarLessons call at line 218 — can throw with noisy stderr from native layer
- Outer catch at line 230 swallows the error — but stderr already printed

## Solution

Suppress stderr during the similarity check by temporarily redirecting process.stderr writes, OR use a more targeted approach:
1. Add a try/catch specifically around the embedText probe (similar to clean-lessons.ts:97-111 pattern)
2. If the probe fails, log a single clean debug-level message instead of letting native errors through
3. Consider using the `NODE_LLAMA_CPP_LOG_LEVEL=0` env var approach to silence native logging during the probe

Alternative: Redirect stderr to /dev/null for the probe duration using a helper function that captures and discards native output.

## Key Files
- src/commands/capture.ts (checkSimilarityPostCapture function, lines 206-233)
- src/memory/embeddings/nomic.ts (embedText, native model interaction)

## Acceptance Criteria
- `ca learn` with a broken/missing model produces NO stderr noise
- `ca learn` with a working model shows similarity warnings as before
- Best-effort nature preserved (never blocks capture)
- Debug-level diagnostic available for troubleshooting

title: P2: Accept pre-loaded items in findSimilarLessons to eliminate O(n²) JSONL reads
description:
## Problem

findDuplicatePairs() in clean-lessons.ts:28-53 loops through N active items and calls findSimilarLessons() for each. Each call to findSimilarLessons() calls readMemoryItems(repoRoot) (vector.ts:196), which reads and parses the entire JSONL file from disk (jsonl.ts:190). For N lessons, this results in N redundant full file reads + JSON parses.

## Solution

Add an optional `items` parameter to FindSimilarOptions:

```typescript
export interface FindSimilarOptions {
  threshold?: number;
  excludeId?: string;
  items?: MemoryItem[];  // Pre-loaded items to avoid re-reading JSONL
}
```

In findSimilarLessons():
- If options.items is provided, use it directly instead of calling readMemoryItems()
- If not provided, fall back to readMemoryItems() (backward compatible)

In findDuplicatePairs():
- Pass the already-loaded activeItems array via the items option

## Key Files
- src/memory/search/vector.ts (findSimilarLessons, FindSimilarOptions — lines 170-228)
- src/commands/clean-lessons.ts (findDuplicatePairs — lines 28-53)
- src/memory/search/index.ts (barrel re-export of FindSimilarOptions)

## Tests
- Verify findSimilarLessons uses provided items instead of reading from disk
- Verify backward compat: omitting items still reads from disk
- Verify clean-lessons passes items correctly (mock readMemoryItems, assert not called during pairwise scan)

title: P2: Fix stale documentation referencing Jaccard, old thresholds, and archival
description:
## Problem

Four files still reference outdated implementation details from before the semantic similarity rewrite:

### 1. src/memory/capture/README.md:26
**Stale**: `Novelty - Not similar to existing lessons (Jaccard > 0.8)`
**Should be**: `Novelty - Not similar to existing lessons (semantic cosine similarity >= 0.98)`

### 2. docs/invariants/capture.md:7
**Stale**: `D1: SIMILARITY_THRESHOLD = 0.8, MIN_WORD_COUNT = 4`
**Should be**: `D1: DUPLICATE_THRESHOLD = 0.98 (cosine similarity), MIN_WORD_COUNT = 4`

### 3. src/setup/templates/skills.ts:287
**Stale**: `Apply quality filters: novelty check (>0.85 similarity = skip), specificity check`
**Should be**: `Apply quality filters: novelty check (>= 0.98 semantic cosine similarity = skip), specificity check`

### 4. src/setup/templates/docs.ts:298 and :510
**Stale**: `Remove tombstones and archive old items` / `Removes tombstones and archives old items, then rebuilds index`
**Should be**: `Remove tombstones and rebuild index` / `Removes tombstones, then rebuilds index`

## Acceptance Criteria
- All 4 files updated
- No remaining references to Jaccard in non-archive files
- No remaining references to 0.85 threshold
- No remaining references to compaction archiving old items
- grep sweep: `grep -r 'Jaccard\|shouldArchive\|archive old' src/ docs/ --include='*.ts' --include='*.md'` returns zero matches (excluding archive/ and CHANGELOG)

title: P2: Filter clean-lessons to lesson-type items only
description:
## Problem

clean-lessons.ts:115-116 reads all memory items and only filters out invalidated ones. findSimilarLessons() (vector.ts:196-202) also iterates all items. The command is named 'clean-lessons' but actually analyzes all item types (solutions, patterns, preferences, etc.), which can produce misleading output — suggesting two unrelated patterns are 'duplicate lessons' when they serve different purposes.

## Solution

Filter items by type in clean-lessons before passing to findDuplicatePairs:
- Filter to items where `item.type === 'lesson'` (or include a reasonable subset of types that should be deduplicated)
- Alternatively, if all types should be analyzed, rename the command or update its description to clarify scope

## Investigation Needed
Check what item types exist in the codebase (MemoryItem type field). Determine which types should be subject to dedup analysis. The command name suggests only lessons, but the review may have a valid point about other types too.

## Key Files
- src/commands/clean-lessons.ts (lines 115-116, item filtering)
- src/memory/types.ts (MemoryItem type field definition)
- src/memory/search/vector.ts (findSimilarLessons item iteration)

## Tests
- Verify non-lesson items are excluded from analysis (or update command description)
- Update existing tests if filtering changes results

title: P2: Consolidate isModelAvailable import path
description:
## Problem

isModelAvailable is imported from two different module paths:
- quality.ts:12 and vector.ts:10 import from `../embeddings/model.js` (direct)
- clean-lessons.ts:12 imports from `../embeddings/index.js` (barrel, which re-exports from nomic.js)

These are technically two separate function references. In tests, this forces separate mocks (vi.mock for model.js vs nomic.js). The barrel re-export in embeddings/index.ts:9 re-exports isModelAvailable from nomic.js, NOT model.js — so they may even be different implementations.

## Solution

Standardize all imports to use the barrel path (`../embeddings/index.js`) or all to use the direct path (`../embeddings/model.js`). The direct path is preferred since model.ts is the canonical source and avoids re-export indirection.

1. Check embeddings/index.ts — verify which module it re-exports isModelAvailable from
2. Standardize: all consumers import from model.js (canonical source)
3. If embeddings/index.ts re-exports from nomic.js instead of model.js, fix the barrel to re-export from model.js
4. Update all test mocks to use a single vi.mock path

## Key Files
- src/memory/embeddings/model.ts (canonical isModelAvailable)
- src/memory/embeddings/index.ts (barrel re-export)
- src/memory/embeddings/nomic.ts (may have its own isModelAvailable?)
- src/memory/capture/quality.ts:12 (import)
- src/memory/search/vector.ts:10 (import)
- src/commands/clean-lessons.ts:12 (import)

## Tests
- Verify all import paths resolve to the same function
- Verify test mocks only need one vi.mock call for isModelAvailable

title: P3: Add clean-lessons to preflight exhaustive test list
description:

cli-preflight.test.ts:32-41 declares an exhaustive sqliteCommands list that should match NEEDS_SQLITE in cli-preflight.ts:31. The actual NEEDS_SQLITE set correctly contains 'clean-lessons', but the test array does NOT include it. title: P0: Setup command must create .mcp.json for MCP tool registration
description:
REVISED AFTER CODE REVIEW: ca setup ALREADY creates .mcp.json via addMcpServerToMcpJson(). The bug is narrower than initially thought.

ACTUAL BUG: 'ca setup --update' only regenerates template files (agents, commands, skills) but does NOT re-check hooks or MCP config. So if a user:
1. Ran 'ca setup' with v1.1.0 (before MCP feature)
2. Upgraded to v1.2.0
3. Ran 'ca setup --update' to get new templates
...they get new templates but NO .mcp.json because runUpdate() skips config.

Also: 'ca prime' (session start hook) doesn't warn if MCP tools are missing. So the session starts, AGENTS.md says 'use memory_search', but the tool doesn't exist. Silent failure.

FIX:
1. In src/setup/all.ts runUpdate(): After updating templates, also call configureClaudeSettings() to ensure hooks+MCP are current
2. In src/commands/management-prime.ts: Add a check - if MCP is not configured, emit a warning: 'WARNING: MCP server not registered. Run ca setup to enable memory_search/memory_capture.'
3. In src/setup/all.ts runSetup output: Make the 'Already configured' message clearer - show actual .mcp.json path

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- 'ca setup --update' also ensures hooks and MCP are configured (not just templates)
- 'ca prime' warns if MCP tools are missing
- 'ca doctor' existing check is sufficient (no change needed)
- Tests cover: update with missing MCP, prime with missing MCP

title: Make init hook installation non-destructive and honor core.hooksPath
description:
## Problem

`init` writes `.git/hooks/pre-commit` unconditionally, which can overwrite existing hooks. It also ignores `core.hooksPath`, so repos using custom hook directories are missed.

## Goals
- Never clobber existing hooks.
- Respect `core.hooksPath` when present.
- Keep behavior idempotent via the existing marker.

## Proposed Behavior
1. **Resolve hooks directory**:
   - If `core.hooksPath` is configured, use it (relative paths resolved from repo root).
   - Else use `.git/hooks`.
   - If no hooks directory exists, skip with message and exit 0.
2. **Non-destructive install**:
   - If `pre-commit` does not exist: create with template (marker included).
   - If it exists and already contains marker: do nothing.
   - If it exists without marker: append a new block with marker + hook invocation. Preserve existing content.
3. **Permissions**: ensure resulting file is executable.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `init` uses `core.hooksPath` when configured (relative/absolute).
- [ ] Existing `pre-commit` is never overwritten.
- [ ] Marker prevents duplicate installs.
- [ ] Appended block is added with clear marker and newline separation.
- [ ] Resulting hook is executable (0o755).
- [ ] If hooks directory missing, skip gracefully.

## Tests
1. Existing hook without marker → content preserved and block appended.
2. Existing hook with marker → unchanged.
3. Missing hook file → created with template.
4. `core.hooksPath` set (absolute + relative) → installs to that directory.
5. Hooks dir missing → skip with message, exit 0.

## Files
- src/cli.ts (installPreCommitHook, init output)
- src/cli.test.ts (new tests for append + hooksPath)
close_reason: Hook installation is now non-destructive (appends to existing) and honors core.hooksPath

title: Add test tags and test:fast script
description:
## Purpose

Enable selective test execution for faster development feedback.

## Implementation

### 1. Tag slow tests

In `cli.test.ts`:
```typescript
describe('CLI integration', { tags: ['integration'] }, () => {
  // All CLI tests
});
```

### 2. Add package.json scripts

```json
{
  "scripts": {
    "test": "vitest run",
    "test:fast": "vitest run --exclude-tags integration",
    "test:watch": "vitest",
    "test:all": "pnpm download-model && vitest run"
  }
}
```

## What test:fast Skips

- CLI integration tests (cli.test.ts) - process spawning is slow

## What test:fast Keeps

- Property tests (types.test.ts) - catches schema bugs, not that slow
- Unit tests - all other test files
- SQLite tests - still needed for storage logic verification

## Rationale

Property tests catch type contract bugs that unit tests miss. The CLI tests are the real bottleneck (~4000 lines spawning Node processes). Skipping integration only gives good balance of speed vs coverage.

## Verification

1. `pnpm test:fast` should skip cli.test.ts
2. `pnpm test` should run everything
3. CI should use `pnpm test` (full suite)
close_reason: All optimizations implemented and verified

title: E3: Pure Test Infrastructure
description:
## Scope
Split test utilities and vitest workspace to isolate native-module-dependent tests from pure logic tests.

## In Scope
- Create src/test-utils-pure.ts (Section 1 fixture creators + Section 2 skip helpers — zero native imports)
- Update src/test-utils.ts to re-export all symbols from test-utils-pure.ts (backward compat)
- Update vitest.workspace.ts: add 'pure' pool (threads, maxThreads 4), rename existing 'unit' → 'native' pool (threads, maxThreads 2)
- Classify unit tests: identify which ~28 test files only use fixture creators/skip helpers
- Update classified pure test files to import from test-utils-pure.ts
- Verify no test time regression > 10% from baseline

## Out of Scope
Changing test logic/assertions, changing production code, integration/embedding workspace changes

## EARS Requirements

R1 (Ubiquitous): src/test-utils-pure.ts shall contain zero imports of native module packages (better-sqlite3, onnxruntime-node, @huggingface/transformers) or modules that transitively import them.

R2 (Ubiquitous): src/test-utils.ts shall re-export all public symbols from src/test-utils-pure.ts, maintaining backward compatibility for all existing importers.

R3 (State-driven): When test-utils-pure.ts is imported at module scope, the process shall not load any native .node addons.

R4 (Ubiquitous): vitest.workspace.ts shall define a 'pure' project using threads pool with maxThreads >= 4.

R5 (Ubiquitous): vitest.workspace.ts shall define a 'native' project using threads pool with maxThreads <= 2, for unit tests that require better-sqlite3.

R6 (Ubiquitous): The integration and embedding workspace projects shall remain unchanged.

R7 (Ubiquitous): All unit test files that only use fixture creators and skip helpers shall import from test-utils-pure.ts and be included in the pure workspace.

R8 (Event-driven): When pnpm test:unit completes, total elapsed time shall not exceed 110% of the pre-migration baseline.

## Scenario Table

| ID | Source | Category | Precondition | Trigger | Expected Outcome |
|----|--------|----------|--------------|---------|------------------|
| S1 | R1,R3 | happy | test-utils-pure.ts created | Static import scan | Zero native imports found |
| S2 | R2 | happy | test-utils.ts updated | Import check | All pure symbols available via test-utils.ts |
| S3 | R4 | happy | workspace updated | pnpm test --project=pure | Runs with 4+ threads |
| S4 | R5 | happy | workspace updated | pnpm test --project=native | Runs with 1-2 threads |
| S5 | R7 | happy | ~28 files updated | pnpm test --project=pure | Only pure-classified tests execute |
| S6 | R6 | boundary | integration/embedding workspaces | pnpm test | No behavior change |
| S7 | R8 | boundary | migration complete | pnpm test:unit | Time <= baseline * 1.1 |

## Key Estimates
- ~28 of 52 test-utils consumers only use fixture creators → migrate to pure variant
- test-utils.ts coupling: closeDb import on line 17 from memory/storage/sqlite/connection.ts
- shouldSkipEmbeddingTests is pure (only reads process.env)

## Assumptions
Depends on Epic 2 (model-info.ts makes pure classification possible). Both prereqs completed (learning_agent-f4oj, learning_agent-863u).

title: Plan-time lessons workflow not optimal
description:
User Report: Current plan-time lessons implementation not efficient. Claude received 5 lessons at startup but missed applying one during planning.

User Suggestion: Hook when entering plan mode that instructs Claude to use lna command to query for relevant lessons.

Alternative approaches to explore:
1. Plan-mode hook that triggers lna check-plan automatically
2. Explicit instruction in AGENTS.md to run check-plan before finalizing plans
3. Different lesson injection timing
4. More prominent lesson formatting

⚠️ REQUIRES USER CLARIFICATION BEFORE STARTING:
Discuss with the user to clarify:
- What does 'entering plan mode' mean technically? Is there a hook point?
- Should lessons be re-queried with plan context, or just reminded?
- Trade-off: automatic injection vs explicit Claude action?
close_reason: Superseded by v0.2.4 redesign: Plan-time hook is being removed entirely, replaced by tool-based lesson_search that Claude uses when needed. The 'not optimal' workflow is solved by giving Claude agency to search rather than auto-injecting.

title: Write tests for loop.ts stream-json changes and watch.ts command
description:
## What
TDD tests for both the modified loop script generation and the new watch command.

## Why
Project mandates TDD — tests before implementation. Both the loop changes and the watch command need comprehensive test coverage.

## Implementation details

### loop.test.ts additions
- Test that generated script includes --output-format stream-json --include-partial-messages
- Test that generated script creates trace JSONL file alongside macro log
- Test that stream splitter correctly extracts text content from JSON events
- Test that marker detection still works via the macro log (not the JSONL)
- Test DRY_RUN mode with the new script shape
- Test the .latest symlink/pointer is created

### watch.test.ts (new file)
- Test event parsing: tool_use, text_delta, API request events
- Test formatted output matches expected patterns (tool name, duration, status)
- Test --epic flag selects the correct trace file
- Test graceful behavior when no trace file exists
- Test that the command is registered and has correct help text

## Acceptance criteria
- All tests written BEFORE implementation (TDD)
- Tests verify real behavior, not mocked internals
- loop.test.ts covers the stream-json script generation changes
- watch.test.ts covers the new command end-to-end
- pnpm test passes with the new tests (they should initially fail — red phase)
close_reason: Tests written and verified failing (red phase). 7 new stream-json tests in loop.test.ts, new watch.test.ts with 10 tests.
